research.summary: fiskaly SIGN IT (API version 2026-02-03) is a country-specialized rendering of fiskaly's "Unified API" — one templated OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec (hosted at https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml) shared by SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE BE and E-INVOICE DE, with per-country text injected client-side from /static/unified/2026-02-03/IT_en.yaml. The API exposes 27 operations over 8 resources (tokens, subjects, organizations, taxpayers, locations, systems, records, files) on hosts live.api.fiskaly.com / test.api.fiskaly.com, versioned by a mandatory date-based X-Api-Version header. Auth is fiskaly's usual API key+secret exchanged at POST /tokens for a JWT bearer; POST/PATCH require an X-Idempotency-Key (UUIDv3/v4, 24h replay cache, X-Idempotency-Replayed response header). The Italian flow ("SIGN IT lite") relays each B2C receipt in real time to Agenzia delle Entrate's "Online Commercial Document" web portal using the merchant's Fisconline credentials (which expire every 90 days); records follow a two-call pattern (INTENTION then TRANSACTION of type RECEIPT/CORRECTION/CANCELLATION) and a state machine ACCEPTED→COMPLETED/FAILED (or REJECTED) with mode PROCESSING→FINISHED, the AdE progressive number returned in compliance.data and raw AdE payloads in transmission.request/response. Docs quality is mixed: genuinely good CalVer/idempotency/state-machine/error-handling-during-AdE-outage documentation, weekly changelog, Postman collection and a clear step-by-step guide; but the spec ships 168 unresolved description placeholders (rendered as blank descriptions), has zero request/response examples and zero code samples, documents no rate-limit numbers or Retry-After, mentions webhooks only in a changelog line with no webhook docs anywhere, and contains copy-paste/typo defects (E-Invoice links in the SIGN IT intro, "401.xf", "stabability", a leftover /api/sign-it/local dev page).

key_facts.fact: The SIGN IT 2026-02-03 API reference is rendered client-side by Docusaurus + redocusaurus-dynamic from a templated OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec at https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml, with country-specific text values substituted from https://developer.fiskaly.com/static/unified/2026-02-03/IT_en.yaml (mapping found in the JS bundle main.2c5ea9ba.js). The same OAS file backs sign-fr, e-invoice-be and e-invoice-de.

The API has exactly 27 operations across 15 path items: POST /tokens; GET+POST /subjects, PATCH+GET /subjects/{subject_id}; same CRUD-without-DELETE pattern for /organizations, /taxpayers, /locations, /systems, /records; GET /files and GET /files/{path}. There are no DELETE operations anywhere (decommissioning is done via PATCH state changes).

Auth: POST /tokens (security: []) takes {type: API_KEY, key, secret} (credentials created in fiskaly HUB/Dashboard) and returns a JWT with bearer, issued_at, expires_at; every other endpoint requires 'JWT' http-bearer security. Securityscheme: type http, scheme bearer.

Versioning is CalVer via a required X-Api-Version header (pattern YYYY-MM-DD); supported versions: 2026-02-03 (SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE BE), 2025-08-12 (SIGN IT, SIGN FR), 2024-10-31 (SIGN IT only). Servers: live.api.fiskaly.com (Live) and test.api.fiskaly.com (Test), no base path.

Idempotency: X-Idempotency-Key (UUIDv4 or v3) is required on all POST and PATCH (explicitly NOT honored by POST /tokens — documented as a known exception); cached key/payload/response pairs expire after 24h; 422 means header/payload mismatch on retry, 409 means concurrent in-flight retry; X-Idempotency-Replayed response header (true/false) was added 2026-03-18.

Record lifecycle: state enum ACCEPTED/REJECTED/COMPLETED/FAILED and mode enum PROCESSING/FINISHED; REJECTED, COMPLETED and FAILED are final; all record state/mode transitions are performed internally by the fiskaly service, not by API calls.

Taxpayer, Location and System share lifecycle state ACQUIRED→COMMISSIONED→DECOMMISSIONED (decommission irreversible, transitions via PATCH) and mode INACTIVE/OPERATIVE/SUSPENDED/DEGRADED — DEGRADED is documented as 'not yet implemented'.

Italian fiscalization (SIGN IT lite) transmits each transaction in real time to the AdE 'Online Commercial Document' web procedure using taxpayer Fisconline credentials (pin, password, personal tax_id_number); credentials expire every 90 days (renewal recommended every 60), SPID is not supported, and the taxpayer must do a manual first login to the AdE portal. AdE's progressive number is returned in compliance.data; raw transmitted/received AdE payloads are exposed base64-encoded in transmission.request/transmission.response.

Creating a fiscal record always takes two POST /records calls: first a Record type INTENTION (operation TRANSACTION), then a Record type TRANSACTION referencing it; Italy supports transaction operations RECEIPT, CORRECTION, CANCELLATION (the unified spec also defines ABORT, DRAFT_RECEIPT, ENRICHMENT, INVOICE, PRO_FORMA_INVOICE, PROOF_OF_PAYMENT for other countries).

Error model: every error (400/401/404/405/409/422/429/500/501/503) wraps {content: {status:int, code:string e.g. E_UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS, error:string, message:string}}; responses carry X-Api-Version and X-Trace-Identifier headers. No Retry-After header and no numeric rate limits are documented anywhere (429 schema only; changelog mentions 'improved createToken endpoint rate limit behavior').

AdE outage handling is documented on a dedicated 'Connection Loss' page: if retry fails, issue a paper document, then ensure compliance by issuing an electronic invoice within 12 days with prescribed data ('Portal unavailable' note, consumer fiscal code, amounts, etc.).

Version changes: 2024-10-31→2025-08-12 introduced the Unified API hosts (test/live.api.fiskaly.com), mandatory X-Api-Version, moved content.details→content.operation.details, added compliance.artifact PDF retrieval and credentials.tax_id_number; 2025-08-12→2026-02-03 renamed /assets→/organizations and split /entities→/taxpayers + /locations, made credentials.tax_id_number required, added E_INVOICE_SERVICE and PAYMENT_DEVICE system types and BE fiscalization; a Location::HEAD_OFFICE is auto-created with the Taxpayer's ID.

Docs defect: the rendered 2026-02-03 IT spec contains 168 unresolved template placeholders (e.g. oas_components_schemas_italian_fiscalization_description, oas_components_responses_locations_response_description) because IT_en.yaml does not define them; the live Redoc page silently renders these as empty descriptions (0 literal placeholders visible in the rendered DOM).

The spec contains no operation-level request/response examples (0 'examples:' blocks; only 67 field-level 'example:' values), no x-codeSamples (so no curl/Python/etc. samples beyond Redoc's auto-generated payloads), no webhooks/callbacks definitions, and fiskaly's public GitHub has no SIGN IT spec or SDK repos (only legacy German Cloud-TSE/KassenSichV SDKs).

Test guidance: all fiskaly accounts start in TEST (full API functionality, sandbox data); TEST and LIVE are fully isolated (separate resources and API keys); LIVE requires a 'Go LIVE' sales-approval flow in HUB; testing in LIVE is forbidden since every LIVE record is transmitted to the tax authority.

research.details:
# fiskaly SIGN IT API — version 2026-02-03 — technical map & docs assessment

## 0. How the spec was obtained (reproducible)

- `https://developer.fiskaly.com/api/sign-it/2026-02-03` is client-side rendered (raw HTML = "Loading ..."), via a custom Docusaurus plugin (`plugins/openapi.js`, plugin id `redocusaurus-dynamic`). The standard `/redocusaurus/plugin-redoc-N.yaml` paths all 404.
- The JS bundle `https://developer.fiskaly.com/assets/js/main.2c5ea9ba.js` contains the product→spec mapping:

```js
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2026-02-03/BE_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.e-invoice-be.2026-02-03.yaml"},
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2026-02-03/DE_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.e-invoice-de.2026-02-03.yaml"},
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2026-02-03/FR_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.sign-fr.2026-02-03.yaml"},
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2026-02-03/IT_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml"},
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/c18c9c21/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2025-08-12/FR_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.sign-fr.2025-08-12.yaml"},
{oas:"https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/c18c9c21/en/oas.yaml",doc:"/static/unified/2025-08-12/IT_en.yaml",api:"fiskaly.sign-it.2025-08-12.yaml"}
```

- **Architecture insight**: SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE BE and E-INVOICE DE are ONE "Unified API" (one OAS per version). The OAS (7,036 lines, `openapi: 3.0.3`, `info.version: '2026-02-03'`) is a template containing `((<key))` placeholders; the per-country `*_en.yaml` file (IT: 1,961 lines) provides the values, substituted client-side. Country-specific behavior appears as inline flags like "Not supported in 🇮🇹 Italy" / "🇮🇹 In Italy, the supported transaction operations are…".
- 2024-10-31 has a separate page (`@site/src/pages/api/sign-it/2024-10-31.jsx`) backed by a non-unified spec (different chunk).
- Local artifacts produced: `/tmp/signit-2026-02-03-oas.yaml` (raw template), `/tmp/signit-doc.yaml` (IT values), `/tmp/signit-rendered.yaml` (merged, 7,353 lines), `/tmp/signit-rendered-2025.yaml` (2025-08-12 merged).

Spec header verbatim:
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
servers:
  - url: 'https://live.api.fiskaly.com'    # "Live Environment"
  - url: 'https://test.api.fiskaly.com'    # "Test Environment"
info:
  version: '2026-02-03'
  title: 'fiskaly SIGN IT'
  contact: {email: info@fiskaly.com, url: 'https://fiskaly.com'}
x-tagGroups:
  - name: Management  # tags: Tokens, Subjects, Organizations
  - name: Operation   # tags: Taxpayers, Locations, Systems, Records, Files
```

## 1. Full endpoint inventory (27 operations; from the OpenAPI spec)

| Method | Path | operationId | Purpose (spec summary/description) |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /tokens | createToken | "Create a token" — Subject::API_KEY based token creation, returns JWT bearer valid until expires_at; recreate only on expiry. `security: []` |
| GET | /subjects | listSubjects | "List subjects" — API_KEY identities; Subject::USER "available soon" |
| POST | /subjects | createSubject | "Create a subject" — create API key; secret returned only in creation response; scoped to an Organization via X-Scope-Identifier |
| PATCH | /subjects/{subject_id} | updateSubject | "Update a subject" |
| GET | /subjects/{subject_id} | retrieveSubject | "Retrieve a subject" |
| GET | /organizations | listOrganizations | "List organizations" |
| POST | /organizations | createOrganization | "Create an organization" (ACCOUNT/GROUP/UNIT hierarchy) |
| PATCH | /organizations/{organization_id} | updateOrganization | "Update an organization" |
| GET | /organizations/{organization_id} | retrieveOrganization | "Retrieve an organization" |
| GET | /taxpayers | listTaxpayers | "List taxpayers" |
| POST | /taxpayers | createTaxpayer | "Create a taxpayer" — COMPANY or INDIVIDUAL + country fiscalization (IT: tax_id_number, vat_id_number, FISCONLINE credentials); initial state ACQUIRED |
| PATCH | /taxpayers/{taxpayer_id} | updateTaxpayer | "Update a taxpayer" — state/mode transitions (commission, suspend, credentials update) |
| GET | /taxpayers/{taxpayer_id} | retrieveTaxpayer | "Retrieve a taxpayer" |
| GET | /locations | listLocations | "List locations" |
| POST | /locations | createLocation | "Create a location" — BRANCH = physical business premises; initial state ACQUIRED |
| PATCH | /locations/{location_id} | updateLocation | "Update a location" |
| GET | /locations/{location_id} | retrieveLocation | "Retrieve a location" |
| GET | /systems | listSystems | "List systems" |
| POST | /systems | createSystem | "Create a system" — ERS/PoS abstraction; IT supports FISCAL_DEVICE only (PAYMENT_DEVICE, E_INVOICE_SERVICE "not yet supported" for IT) |
| PATCH | /systems/{system_id} | updateSystem | "Update a system" — state/mode, location reconnection, software/producer |
| GET | /systems/{system_id} | retrieveSystem | "Retrieve a system" |
| GET | /records | listRecords | "List records" — query filters: taxpayer_id, location_id, system_id, record type, pagination |
| POST | /records | createRecord | "Create a record" — INTENTION first, then TRANSACTION; also accepts X-Replay-Remaining / X-Replay-Hash headers (offline replay, "Not supported in 🇮🇹 Italy") |
| PATCH | /records/{record_id} | updateRecord | "Update a record" — body schema is an empty object; "currently all state and mode transitions are carried out by the fiskaly API service itself internally" |
| GET | /records/{record_id} | retrieveRecord | "Retrieve a record" — on-demand query expansions: operation, file-artifact, file-integrity, compliance-artifact, data-artifact, data-integrity |
| GET | /files | listFiles | "List files" — filters: system_id, created start/end, pagination |
| GET | /files/{path} | streamFile | "Stream a file" — download archived export ZIPs (FileType enum: application/zip) |

No DELETE or PUT operations exist. Response codes per op are an explicit subset of: 200, 400, 401, 404, 405, 409, 422, 429, 500, 501, 503.

Common request headers: `X-Api-Version` (required, all ops), `X-Idempotency-Key` (required, POST/PATCH), `X-Scope-Identifier` (optional; "specifies the organization scope in which the request is executed... linked to the correct organization within the organization hierarchy"). Common response headers: `X-Api-Version`, `X-Trace-Identifier` (added 2025-12-23/30 "to support customer success communication"), `X-Idempotency-Replayed` (POST/PATCH; added 2026-03-18).

## 2. Auth model

- Classic fiskaly pattern: API key + secret → JWT.
- `POST /tokens` body: `TokenCreateContent` → discriminated `oneOf` with single mapping `API_KEY: ApiKeyToken` (`{type: 'API_KEY', key: ApiKeyString, secret: ApiSecretString}`). API keys are created in fiskaly HUB/Dashboard (Management) or via `createSubject` (Subject::API_KEY) for Organization::UNIT scopes.
- Response `Token`: `{id (UUIDv4), authentication: {type: 'JWT', bearer, expires_at, issued_at}, organization: {id}, subject: {id}}`.
- securitySchemes verbatim:
```yaml
securitySchemes:
  JWT:
    $ref: '#/components/securitySchemes/JsonWebToken'
  JsonWebToken:
    description: |
      A JSON Web Token (JWT) used for access control and authorization. Provided as a Bearer token in the HTTP `Authorization` header.
    type: http
    scheme: bearer
```
- Tokens tag text: "API requests without a JWT present will fail with an unauthorized access HTTP error `401`.xf" (note the literal stray `xf` typo) … "The fiskaly API processes only `https` based requests. Plain `http` based requests are redirected to `https`."
- Multi-tenancy: bootstrap flow (integration guide) = Dashboard registration (ACCOUNT) → GROUP org + API key in Dashboard → `createToken` → `createOrganization` (UNIT per merchant) → `createSubject` (API_KEY scoped via X-Scope-Identifier = UNIT org id) → new `createToken` for that UNIT → operational resources.

## 3. Core resources & lifecycles

**Organization** (ACCOUNT | GROUP | UNIT): management hierarchy; state ENABLED/DISABLED. "ACCOUNT… created when you register on the HUB. GROUP… e.g. one Group per country. UNIT represents an individual legal entity… each unit corresponds to a specific merchant."

**Subject** (API_KEY; USER "available soon"): state ENABLED/DISABLED; content includes name, key, secret (secret only returned at creation).

**Taxpayer** (COMPANY | INDIVIDUAL): legal entity + country fiscalization. State machine (shared with Location and System):
- state: `ACQUIRED → COMMISSIONED → DECOMMISSIONED` (PATCH-driven; DECOMMISSIONED irreversible; "Operations cannot be performed" in ACQUIRED)
- mode: `INACTIVE / OPERATIVE / SUSPENDED / DEGRADED` — INACTIVE→OPERATIVE automatic on commissioning; OPERATIVE↔SUSPENDED via API (e.g. credentials update); DEGRADED "(not yet implemented)" for invalid credentials.
- Italian fiscalization verbatim (required for IT taxpayers):
```yaml
ItalianFiscalization:
  required: [type, tax_id_number, vat_id_number, credentials]
  properties:
    type: {enum: [IT]}
    tax_id_number:   # Codice fiscale of the business: 11 digits (COMPANY) or 16 alnum (INDIVIDUAL)
    vat_id_number:   # Partita IVA, 11 digits
    credentials:     # oneOf: ItalianFisconlineCredentials
ItalianFisconlineCredentials:
  required: [type, pin, password, tax_id_number]   # tax_id_number = codice fiscale of the natural person holding Fisconline access
```
- Upon Taxpayer creation a `Location::HEAD_OFFICE` is auto-created sharing the Taxpayer's ID (support article). Unified spec also defines GermanFiscalization (ELSTER), FrenchFiscalization (LNE/Infocert), BelgianFiscalization (MyMinFin) — visible but not applicable to IT.

**Location** (HEAD_OFFICE auto / BRANCH via API): taxpayer's physical premises; same state/mode machine; address, coordinates.

**System** (FISCAL_DEVICE for IT; PAYMENT_DEVICE, E_INVOICE_SERVICE defined but "not yet supported" in IT): an Electronic Recording System (PoS/cash register). Connected to a Location; requires software + producer info. Response carries: journal.cryptography (PEM certificate w/ serial + expiry), compliance (`{software: {state: TRANSMISSION_ONLY | TRANSMISSION_RECEPTION}}`), records (allowed SystemRecordType list), vat_rates, vat_exemptions, logs (severity enum: ERROR only), annotations (e.g. PeppolIdentifierAnnotation), created_at/updated_at.

**Record** — the fiscal artifact. Full type enum verbatim:
```yaml
SystemRecordType:
  enum: ['INTENTION::TRANSACTION','INTENTION::EVENT','INTENTION::EXPORT','INTENTION::DUPLICATE','INTENTION::CLOSING','INTENTION::UPLOAD',
         'TRANSACTION::RECEIPT','TRANSACTION::ABORT','TRANSACTION::CANCELLATION','TRANSACTION::CORRECTION','TRANSACTION::DRAFT_RECEIPT',
         'TRANSACTION::ENRICHMENT','TRANSACTION::INVOICE','TRANSACTION::PROOF_OF_PAYMENT','TRANSACTION::PRO_FORMA_INVOICE',
         'CLOSING::DAILY','CLOSING::MONTHLY','CLOSING::YEARLY','EXPORT::RECORDS','E_INVOICE::TRANSMISSION','E_INVOICE::RECEPTION',
         'UPLOAD::PEPPOL_PROOF_OF_OWNERSHIP']
```
- IT-supported subset (per inline country notes + guide): `INTENTION::TRANSACTION` then `TRANSACTION::RECEIPT` (sale), `TRANSACTION::CORRECTION` (return; references original receipt id; may belong to a different system since 2026-01-26), `TRANSACTION::CANCELLATION` (void; references record id).
- Record response object: `{id, type, state, mode, system{id}, record{id}, used_in{id}, replayed_by{id}, journal{signature, signed_at, record(previous)}, file{location: SERVICE|ARCHIVE, artifact, integrity(JWS, application/jose)}, operation(JsonData), compliance{data, url, artifact}, transmission{request, response}, data, logs[{severity: ERROR, message}]}` — i.e., signed + hash-chained journal per record.
- **AdE transmission**: `RecordTransmissionRequest` = "Base64-encoded raw payload that was submitted to the tax authorities." `RecordTransmissionResponse` = "Base64-encoded raw response from the tax authorities… Note: If this field is not populated, an error occurred during the transmission process. The error can be retrieved inside `content.logs`." `compliance.data` = AdE Progressive Number (per receipt_compliance guide, on state COMPLETED + mode FINISHED); `compliance.artifact` = PDF generated in the "Fatture e Corrispettivi" portal.
- Record state machine verbatim (spec):
```yaml
RecordState:
  description: |
    ACCEPTED: The Record request has been received, the Record resource has been created, and processing has started.
    REJECTED: ...validation errors during creation caused the request to be rejected. Details are available in the logs...
    COMPLETED: The Record request has been successfully processed.
    FAILED: The Record request failed and one or more errors occurred during processing. Details are available in the logs...
  enum: [ACCEPTED, REJECTED, COMPLETED, FAILED]
RecordMode:
  enum: [PROCESSING, FINISHED]
```
Guide adds: REJECTED/COMPLETED/FAILED are final; FAILED specifically = "external transmission failure"; transitions POST→Accepted, POST→Rejected, Accepted→Completed (automatic), Accepted→Failed (automatic).
- Receipt payload: `ReceiptTransaction {type, document{number (taxpayer-generated, unique/correlative), series, text, activity_code, issued_at, operation_date, total_vat}, entries[SALE|RETURN|VOUCHER; item/group; value{base, discount, surcharge} in EUR (CurrencyFormatISO4217 enum: EUR only), vat{rate category/exemption, amounts}, unit{quantity, price}, products], customer{type EXTERNAL|INTERNAL, code}, payments[CASH|CARD|ONLINE|OTHER|OUTSTANDING|VOUCHER], details}`. `CustomerCode` = "Identifier provided by the consumer (e.g. lottery code in Italy 🇮🇹). Currently enabled for EXTERNAL customers only." (deferred receipt-lottery support; instant lottery only in future "full" version). `RecordTrainingOption` boolean for training receipts. `Metadata` on all main resources: ≤20 keys, key ≤40 chars, value ≤500 chars; empty-string value deletes a key.

**File**: ZIP export archives (from INTENTION::EXPORT / automatic archiving). `FileLocation` enum SERVICE ("retrieved from UAPI storage") / ARCHIVE.

## 4. Error model, versioning, idempotency, pagination, environments, rate limits, webhooks

**Error model** (verbatim):
```yaml
ErrorResponse:
  required: [code, message, status, error]
  properties:
    status: {type: integer, example: 401}           # HTTP status
    code:   {type: string, example: E_UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS}  # "unique error name"
    error:  {type: string, example: Unauthorized Access}    # short description
    message:{type: string, example: Not authorized to perform this operation.}
```
Wrapped as `{ "content": ErrorResponse }` for every 4xx/5xx. There is **no catalogue of error codes** anywhere in the docs — only the single E_UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS example. Operational/transmission errors surface separately in `Record.logs` (severity enum has only ERROR).

**Versioning**: CalVer (`https://calver.org/`), `X-Api-Version: YYYY-MM-DD` required on every call. Version table: 2026-02-03 (IT/FR/BE), 2025-08-12 (IT/FR), 2024-10-31 (IT). No documented deprecation/sunset policy for old versions (2024-10-31 page carries no deprecation notice).

**Idempotency** (intro doc, verbatim highlights): follows IETF draft-ietf-httpapi-idempotency-key-header-05; UUIDv4 (or v3) per logically distinct request; required for POST/PATCH, ignored for GET/PUT/DELETE; cache TTL 24h; "Exception: The POST /tokens endpoint currently does not react on the provided X-Idempotency-Key HTTP header. This exception will be resolved by upcoming improvements"; 422 = retry payload mismatch, 409 = concurrent processing; `X-Idempotency-Replayed: "true"/"false"` since 2026-03-18.

**Pagination**: token-based. Query `limit` (1–100, default 10) + `token` (Base64 index pointer); response `pagination {next (URI), token, limit}` — "If not provided, the current elements in the results set are the last elements." List filters (since 2026-03-18) on listLocations/listSystems/listRecords: taxpayer_id, location_id, system_id, type.

**Environments / testing**: test.api.fiskaly.com is a full sandbox (all accounts start in TEST); LIVE requires "Go LIVE" sales approval in HUB; TEST and LIVE are fully isolated (separate orgs, separate API keys); "Testing is not supported in the LIVE environment, as all data is transmitted directly to the authorities." Postman environment + collection downloadable (`/static/postman/fiskaly_sign_it_2026-02-03_postman_{environment,collection}.json`, both HTTP 200). Status page: status.fiskaly.com.

**Rate limits**: 429 + TooManyRequestsResponse defined on every operation, but **no numeric limits, no rate-limit headers, no Retry-After** documented in spec, guides, or FAQ. Changelogs admit limits exist ("improved createToken endpoint rate limit behavior", 2025-12-08/12).

**Webhooks/events**: none in the API. No OpenAPI `callbacks`/`webhooks`. The only mention is changelog 2026-04-20 "improved webhook request handling". No webhook documentation exists on the developer portal; SIGN IT also has no dashboard section yet ("fiskaly is working on a new Dashboard that will include SIGN IT data" — support FAQ). Polling GET /records/{id} is the only state-observation mechanism.

**AdE outage handling** (connection_loss guide): paper document at sale time; e-invoice within 12 days with receipt note "Portal unavailable", date/time, consecutive invoice number, consumer name+fiscal code, VAT number+legal name, totals before/after VAT. Retry semantics for the API itself: only the idempotency section (no backoff guidance, no guidance on what to do when a record lands in FAILED — e.g. whether to re-submit as a new record).

## 5. Version history (2024-10-31 → 2025-08-12 → 2026-02-03)

- **2024-10-31** (verified live, rendered via browser): paths /tokens, /subjects(+id), /assets(+id), /entities(+id), /systems(+id), /records(+id). No /files. Title "fiskaly SIGN IT API (2024-10-31)". Pre-unified host. No deprecation banner.
- **2025-08-12** (first Unified API): hosts test/live.api.fiskaly.com; mandatory X-Api-Version; `content.details` → `content.operation.details`; compliance.artifact (record PDF from Fatture e Corrispettivi); new `fiscalization.credentials.tax_id_number` (fixes "Transmission error: unsupported Agenzia delle Entrate (AdE) flow"). Paths: /tokens, /subjects, /assets, /entities, /systems, /records, /files.
- **2026-02-03**: `/assets` → `/organizations`; `/entities` split into `/taxpayers` + `/locations`; Asset::UNIT→Organization::UNIT, Entity::COMPANY/INDIVIDUAL→Taxpayer::*, Entity::LOCATION→Location::BRANCH; credentials.tax_id_number now required; new system types E_INVOICE_SERVICE + PAYMENT_DEVICE; BE fiscalization support; auto-created Location::HEAD_OFFICE shares Taxpayer ID.
- Post-release rolling changes within version 2026-02-03 (changelog inside the doc): 2026-02-17 (BE e-invoice receiving), 2026-02-23 (updated_at on Taxpayer/Location/System; IT error-handling consistency), 2026-03-18 (list query filters; X-Idempotency-Replayed), 2026-04-03/04-08/04-20 (stability, error mapping, denominazione validation, webhook handling). Note: the API version string does NOT change for these behavior changes — "2026-02-03" is a moving target.

## 6. Docs quality assessment

### Genuinely good
- **Service-level semantics are unusually well documented for a fiscalization API**: CalVer versioning policy, a real IETF-aligned idempotency contract (TTL, 409/422 retry semantics, replay header), token-based pagination with bounds, environments policy.
- **State machines documented twice**: enum-level descriptions inside the spec AND dedicated guide pages with diagrams (`/static/docs/entity_system_state_mode_en_it.png`, `record_state_mode_en.png`) labeling PATCH-driven vs INTERNAL transitions — including honesty about unimplemented DEGRADED mode.
- **Compliance/edge-case guides**: AdE outage procedure (12-day e-invoice fallback), Fisconline credential expiry (90 days, renew every 60, SPID unsupported, mandatory first manual portal login), receipt lottery (deferred supported, instant not), audit handling, glossary, regulatory background (D.lgs 127/2015, Art. 24 D.lgs 1/2024).
- **Transparency features**: raw AdE request/response exposed base64 in `transmission`; per-record signed journal + JWS file integrity; X-Trace-Identifier on every response for support escalation.
- **Detailed, dated changelog** (weekly cadence, embedded in the API reference; RSS/Atom/JSON feeds on the 2024 page) and clear cross-version migration articles on support.fiskaly.com.
- **Quick start**: downloadable Postman collection + environment; step-by-step integration guide with the full org bootstrap chain and a YouTube video; localized guides (de/es/fr/it).
- **Strict schemas**: `additionalProperties: false` nearly everywhere, regex patterns, length bounds, discriminated unions — Redoc renders usable auto-generated payload samples (`onlyRequiredInSamples: true`); spec download button enabled.

### Missing or weak
- **168 unresolved description placeholders** in the IT rendering (e.g. `((<oas_components_schemas_italian_fiscalization_description))`, `oas_components_responses_locations_response_description`, `oas_components_parameters_trace_identifier_description`, `abort_transaction`, `intention_record`, `journal_signature`, `compliance_*`…). The live page silently hides them (verified: 0 literal placeholders in rendered DOM) — so key schemas like ItalianFiscalization, IntentionRecord, Journal.signature, Compliance render with **blank descriptions**. The 2025-08-12 rendering has 129 missing keys. This is the single biggest defect.
- **Zero request/response examples**: no `examples:` blocks, no operation-level `example`, only 67 scattered field-level examples. No real Italian receipt example anywhere in the reference (the Postman collection is the only executable example).
- **Zero code samples**: no `x-codeSamples`; no SDKs for SIGN IT (fiskaly's GitHub only has legacy DE Cloud-TSE/KassenSichV SDKs, last meaningfully updated 2022–2025; no public spec repo).
- **Error documentation is a stub**: one error shape, one example code (E_UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS). No enumeration of error codes, no per-endpoint error causes, no documentation of `Record.logs` message vocabulary (the changelog repeatedly says "improved error mapping", implying a rich undocumented error space). RecordLogSeverity/SystemLogSeverity enums contain only ERROR — no WARN/INFO.
- **No rate-limit documentation**: numbers, scope (per key? per org?), and headers all undocumented despite 429 being on every endpoint and changelog references to createToken throttling. No Retry-After.
- **No webhooks/eventing** and no documented async-completion guidance: records are processed asynchronously (PROCESSING→FINISHED) but the docs never say whether POST /records responds synchronously with the final state or whether/how to poll; no recommended polling interval or backoff.
- **Retry/failure semantics gaps**: what to do after state=FAILED (re-create? same idempotency key? new document number?) is not addressed; AdE-side duplicate handling unexplained (an `AbortProperties` example mysteriously shows `FAC-DUP-RES: 1A2B3C` with no explanation).
- **PATCH /records is a documented no-op**: requires a body whose schema is an empty `additionalProperties: false` object — endpoint exists purely for future use, which is confusing.
- **Unified-spec bleed-through**: IT readers see DE/FR/BE schemas (ELSTER, MyMinFin, replay/offline headers) flagged only by emoji notes; some notes are contradictory in tone (e.g. `ReceiptDetails` says "Not applicable in 🇮🇹 Italy" while the integration guide centers on RECEIPT — the note refers only to the details sub-object, an easy misreading).
- **Copy/paste & QA defects**: SIGN IT intro links to "Service Details: fiskaly.com/electronic-invoicing-b2b" and "Developer Guide: developer.fiskaly.com/e-invoice" (E-INVOICE product links pasted into the SIGN IT doc layer); "`401`.xf" typo in the Tokens tag; "improved system stabability" in changelog; a leftover dev page `https://developer.fiskaly.com/api/sign-it/local` returns 200 and is in the sitemap; changelog inside version 2026-02-03 documents changes through 2026-04-20 without bumping the version (mutable versioned API).
- **`security` granularity**: everything is one JWT scheme; scopes/permissions of Subjects ("defined access rights") are asserted but never specified.
- 2026-02-03 sitemap lacks a `glossary`-equivalent for taxpayer state images alt-text etc.; `TaxpayerCountry` is just `PlainString128` (no ISO country enum) despite a CountryCode2 schema existing.

### Net judgment
Above-average conceptual documentation (lifecycles, compliance context, changelog discipline) sitting on top of a below-average reference (blank descriptions from a leaky i18n templating pipeline, no examples, no error catalogue, no SDKs, undocumented limits/webhooks). An integrator can succeed using guide + Postman + Redoc-generated samples, but will hit undocumented territory exactly where production risk is highest: error codes, FAILED-record recovery, rate limits, and async timing.

checked.claim: Claim 2: The API has exactly 27 operations across 15 path items (POST /tokens; GET+POST collection / PATCH+GET item for subjects, organizations, taxpayers, locations, systems, records; GET /files and GET /files/{path}); no DELETE operations anywhere.
checked.note: Independently re-derived by downloading https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml (216,705 bytes, info.version 2026-02-03) and machine-counting with awk/grep rather than trusting the researcher: exactly 15 path keys (/tokens, /subjects, /subjects/{subject_id}, /organizations, /organizations/{organization_id}, /taxpayers, /taxpayers/{taxpayer_id}, /locations, /locations/{location_id}, /systems, /systems/{system_id}, /records, /records/{record_id}, /files, /files/{path}) and exactly 27 HTTP operations: POST on /tokens; GET+POST on each collection and PATCH+GET on each {id} item for the six resources (6x4=24); GET-only on /files and /files/{path}. Zero 'delete:' method keys exist in the file. The PATCH-based decommissioning is corroborated by the rendered integration guide (https://developer.fiskaly.com/sign-it/2026-02-03/integration_guide: state changed to COMMISSIONED/DECOMMISSIONED via updateTaxpayer/updateLocation/updateSystem endpoints). Could not refute any part.

checked.claim: Claim 4: Versioning is CalVer via a required X-Api-Version header (pattern YYYY-MM-DD); supported versions 2026-02-03 (SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE BE), 2025-08-12 (SIGN IT, SIGN FR), 2024-10-31 (SIGN IT only); servers live.api.fiskaly.com and test.api.fiskaly.com with no base path.
checked.note: Verified beyond the claimed IT_en.yaml source. (1) The OAS itself (https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml) defines parameter ApiVersion: in header, name X-Api-Version, required: true, schema -> DateFormatISO8601 with pattern ^[0-9]{4}-(0[1-9]|1[012])-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])$ (i.e. strict YYYY-MM-DD CalVer). (2) The identical support table appears in three independently fetched country files: https://developer.fiskaly.com/static/unified/2026-02-03/IT_en.yaml (lines 135-137), .../FR_en.yaml (lines 97-99) and .../BE_en.yaml (lines 106-108): '2026-02-03 | SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE BE', '2025-08-12 | SIGN IT, SIGN FR', '2024-10-31 | SIGN IT', with 'The latest version is: X-Api-Version: 2026-02-03'. (3) Servers: IT/FR/BE files all set oas_servers_live_url: 'live.api.fiskaly.com' and oas_servers_test_url: 'test.api.fiskaly.com'; the OAS servers block templates these as https://<host> with no path segment. As of fetch date 2026-06-12 no newer version is advertised. Could not refute.

checked.claim: Claim 8: SIGN IT lite transmits each transaction in real time to the AdE 'Online Commercial Document' web procedure using taxpayer Fisconline credentials (pin, password, tax_id_number); credentials expire every 90 days (renew every 60 recommended), SPID not supported, manual first AdE login required; AdE progressive number returned in compliance.data; raw AdE payloads base64-encoded in transmission.request/response.
checked.note: Each sub-claim corroborated by sources other than the claimed credentials page. (1) 90-day expiry / 60-day renewal: fiskaly support center article 'SIGN IT - How to update the FISCONLINE credentials' (https://support.fiskaly.com/hc/en-001/articles/17856721861020) states credentials 'expire every 90 days... we recommend doing this every 60 days'; independently, Agenzia delle Entrate's own FAQ confirms the Area Riservata password expires after 90 days (https://infoprecompilata.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/-/faq-la-password-di-accesso-al-sito-dei-servizi-telematici-risulta-scaduta-cosa-devo-fare-). (2) The 'documento commerciale online' web procedure exists in AdE's Fatture e Corrispettivi portal and 'requires an always-active network connection' i.e. real-time (https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/se-si-utilizza-la-procedura-documento-commerciale-online and https://ivaservizi.agenziaentrate.gov.it/ser/documenticommercialionline/nonauth.html). (3) pin/password/tax_id_number fields and Fisconline-only auth confirmed in IT_en.yaml spec text (lines 833-844) and the integration guide Step 8. (4) SPID not supported ('would require renewal every 24 hours') and mandatory manual first login confirmed verbatim on the rendered credentials page. (5) compliance.data = AdE progressive number confirmed in the integration guide ('the progressive number assigned by AdE to the commercial document (compliance.data)'). (6) IT_en.yaml lines 1049-1052: 'Base64-encoded raw payload that was submitted to the tax authorities' / 'Base64-encoded raw response from the tax authorities'. Minor contextual nuance, not a refutation: since 1 March 2021 AdE issues Fisconline credentials only to VAT-number holders/professionals, not ordinary citizens, which is consistent with fiskaly's wording ('taxpayers... who possess an Italian VAT number').

checked.claim: Claim 9: Creating a fiscal record always takes two POST /records calls — first Record type INTENTION (operation TRANSACTION), then Record type TRANSACTION referencing it; Italy supports RECEIPT, CORRECTION, CANCELLATION (unified spec also defines ABORT, DRAFT_RECEIPT, ENRICHMENT, INVOICE, PRO_FORMA_INVOICE, PROOF_OF_PAYMENT for other countries).
checked.note: Confirmed from two sources beyond the integration-guide page cited: (1) IT_en.yaml spec text (lines 434-438, 1100-1119): 'Every interaction with a System resource requires an initial Record creation of type INTENTION... Currently the fiskaly API supports only an INTENTION of a TRANSACTION... the transaction can be concluded with a subsequent Record creation call... which links to the previous Record::INTENTION::TRANSACTION'; Italy's supported transaction operations enumerated as RECEIPT (certify sale), CORRECTION (return process), CANCELLATION (void receipt). (2) The unified OAS enum (lines 3375-3389 of oas.yaml) lists TRANSACTION::RECEIPT, ABORT, CANCELLATION, CORRECTION, DRAFT_RECEIPT, ENRICHMENT, INVOICE, PROOF_OF_PAYMENT, PRO_FORMA_INVOICE — exactly the extra operations the claim attributes to the cross-country spec. The rendered integration guide Step 11 also states 'Creating a Record in the SIGN IT API will always require two subsequent calls: Part A) INTENTION... Part B) TRANSACTION'. Note the unified spec additionally defines other INTENTION operations (EVENT, EXPORT, DUPLICATE, CLOSING, UPLOAD) not used by Italy, which does not contradict the claim.

checked.claim: Claim 11: AdE outage handling is documented on a dedicated 'Connection Loss' page: if retry fails, issue a paper document, then ensure compliance by issuing an electronic invoice within 12 days with prescribed data ('Portal unavailable' note, consumer fiscal code, amounts, etc.).
checked.note: The page https://developer.fiskaly.com/sign-it/2026-02-03/connection_loss exists and states verbatim: 'If the retry fails... Provide the customer with a simple paper document... Ensure compliance by issuing an electronic invoice within 12 days of the transaction', listing 'Portal unavailable' note, date/time, consecutive invoice number, consumer name/surname/fiscal code, merchant VAT number and legal name, totals before/after VAT. Independently, the 12-day window is real Italian law, not a fiskaly invention: art. 21(4) DPR 633/72 as amended by the Decreto Crescita (DL 34/2019) allows invoice issuance within 12 days of the operation, effective 1 July 2019 (https://www.brocardi.it/testo-unico-iva/titolo-ii/art21.html; https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/it/web/guest/schede/comunicazioni/fatture-e-corrispettivi/faq-fe/risposte-alle-domande-piu-frequenti-categoria/emissione-delle-fatture-elettroniche), and AdE/industry guidance confirms a 12-day transmission allowance for corrispettivi when the device cannot transmit (https://www.artser.it/approfondimenti/registratore-telematico-come-comportarsi-in-caso-di-assenza-di-rete-o-malfunzionamento.html). AdE confirms the web procedure needs an always-on connection, making the outage fallback necessary (https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/se-si-utilizza-la-procedura-documento-commerciale-online). Could not refute.

research.summary: fiskaly runs two parallel doc platforms. The current site (developer.fiskaly.com) is a Docusaurus 3.9.2 site with Algolia DocSearch, 5 locales (en/es/de/it/fr), a products-by-country matrix (DE/AT/ES/IT/FR/BE/PT + Global HUB/API/E-INVOICE/SAFE/RECEIPT), Redocusaurus read-only API references with mixed versioning (v0/v1/v2 for older products, date-based for Unified API products), Postman collections as the hands-on path, almost no inline code samples, no try-it console, and no AI-readiness files (llms.txt is 404). The new preview platform (workspace.fiskaly.com) is built on Astro 5.18.1 + Starlight 0.37.7 with Pagefind search, and is dramatically more ambitious: a working RAG "Ask AI" assistant (Hono.js backend, SSE streaming, Gemini 2.5 Pro/2.0 Flash via Vertex AI, persona system, live /api/session + /api/chat verified working), a full AI/GEO discovery stack (llms.txt, 8.5MB llms-full.txt, CLAUDE.md, products.json, regulatory.json, human-interventions.json, ai-plugin.json, downloadable OpenAPI specs), a documented 9-tool MCP server, customized SSR Redoc API references with per-operation deep-link pages, date version selectors and spec download buttons, persona-segmented hubs (Developer quickstarts with 5-language code tabs, PM Hub with market matrix/effort estimator/regulatory pulse, Operator Center), page feedback widgets, and a design-system component showcase. Critically for not proposing redundant ideas: a React "Try it out" request-runner component (ApiTryIt: server picker, bearer token, params, body, live fetch, response viewer) exists in the shipped JS bundle but does NOT currently render on the live Redoc-based operation pages (browser-verified absent), the MCP package @fiskaly/docs-mcp is documented but not published on npm, the linked /api/console page is a 404, and try-it has no auth-flow helper or generated code snippets per endpoint. Gaps that remain open on the new platform: no published SDKs (docs claim Node/Python/Java SDKs), no auth-aware console, no per-operation multi-language code generation, naming/status inconsistencies between pages (e.g., Belgium "Live E-Invoice" vs "SIGN BE Beta"), and very heavy API pages (~1.2MB HTML each embedding the full spec).

Docs describe it as a Hono.js service with Vertex AI RAG over 5 sources, dual-model routing (Gemini 2.5 Pro / Gemini 2.0 Flash), persona system (developer/PM/retail operator), rate limits (5 msg/min, 30/hr), jailbreak/PII filtering, daily budget guard, nightly k8s CronJob re-indexing, and a Google-OAuth-protected /admin dashboard

Workspace API references are heavily customized SSR Redoc (vendor/redoc.standalone.js + inline __redoc_state, hash guards, scroll-spy): each product version gets a single-page reference PLUS per-operation deep-link pages (/api/sign-pt/2026-05-04/operations/createrecord/), a date-version dropdown selector (e.g. 2026-06-01 latest / 2026-05-04), and a 'Download OpenAPI specification' button; each page embeds the full spec (~1.2 MB HTML per page)

A React 'Try it out' request runner (ApiTryIt component: Base URL selector Sandbox/Production, Bearer-token password field, path/query param inputs, JSON body textarea, live fetch with response status/duration/headers/body, GA dataLayer 'api_try_it' event) exists in the shipped JS bundle (/_astro/ApiTryIt.hhDLenM6.js), but it does NOT currently render on live Redoc-based operation pages — the mount script exits when #fsk-redoc exists, and browser verification confirmed no try-it panel / 'Send Request' on the createrecord page. It also has no auth-flow helper, no token persistence, and no code-snippet generation

Workspace API versioning per sitemap: sign-it has 5 date versions (2024-10-31 → 2026-06-01), sign-fr/e-invoice-be/e-invoice-de have 3 (2026-02-03/2026-05-04/2026-06-01), sign-pt has 2 (2026-05-04/2026-06-01), sign-at and sign-se have 2026-06-01; sign-de/dsfinvk/management/ereceipt/safe are un-dated (v0/v1/v2 semver)

Workspace segments docs by persona: Getting Started (concepts/planning/quickstart/resource-mapping), countries/* (introduction, quickstart, integration-guide, glossary, FAQ, certification per country), PM Hub (Market Matrix, Effort Estimator with week-by-week benchmarks, Regulatory Pulse, Case Studies), Operator Center (compliance guide, FAQ, troubleshooting playbooks), and AI & Agents — plus a homepage with 'What are you building?' path picker and chat prompt chips

Workspace guides use hand-authored multi-language code tabs — Germany Quickstart has cURL/JavaScript/Python/Java/C# tabs on 4 steps — but API operation pages have only Redoc JSON payload samples (no x-codeSamples in specs, no generated per-language snippets)

Visible gaps/inconsistencies on workspace: /api/overview links to an 'API Console — Test API calls in your browser' page at /api/console/ which is a 404; /api/overview also claims official Node.js/Python/Java SDKs and a generic base URL https://api.fiskaly.com/api/v3 + '100 req/min' rate limits that conflict with product pages; countries index lists Belgium as 'Live (E-Invoice)' and Sweden 'Live' while the homepage labels SIGN BE and SIGN SE 'Beta'; the chat-api page's 'Backend Source Code' links to a private/nonexistent repo github.com/AnomalyCo/workspace

Old site structure: homepage product matrix of country × {API, Quick Start, Guide} links for DE (SIGN DE, SUBMIT DE, DSFINVK DE, E-INVOICE DE, DF Portal), AT, ES, IT, FR, BE (E-INVOICE), PT (SIGN PT) plus Global HUB/Management API/E-INVOICE/SAFE/RECEIPT; ~255 EN URLs; quick starts are Postman-collection driven; guide pages have few/no syntax-highlighted code samples and no try-it; API refs are read-only Redocusaurus; 'coming soon' for E-INVOICE FR/IT/PL and SIGN SE

Both platforms have blogs: old site (44 posts, latest 'Unified API Update: Enhanced Idempotency Handling' Mar 16 2026, Dev News 1-28 series); workspace blog is migrated and extended with newer posts (HUB billing upgrade Jun 3 2026, SIGN PT launch May 21 2026, 2026 strategy update Feb 15 2026) with author pages and tags

## 1. CURRENT SITE — developer.fiskaly.com

### Tech & infrastructure
- **Generator**: Docusaurus v3.9.2 (`<meta name=generator content="Docusaurus v3.9.2">`).
- **Search**: Algolia DocSearch (docsearch:language / docsearch:version meta tags, preconnect to algolia.net, dedicated /search page).

### Structure (sitemap: 255 EN URLs)
Top-level sections: blog (44), sign-es (32), api (26), sign-de (23), sign-it (22), sign-fr (19), dsfinvk (9), safe (7), hub (7), submission (5), e-invoice (4), sign-at (3), fiskalyreceipt (3), downloads, v2-roadmap, search, quick-start.

**Homepage product matrix** — country tabs (🇩🇪🇦🇹🇪🇸🇮🇹🇫🇷🇧🇪🇵🇹 + Global), each product card has {API | Quick Start | Guide} links:
- Germany: SIGN DE, SUBMIT DE, DSFINVK DE, E-INVOICE DE, DF DEUTSCHE FISKAL PORTAL (external login)
- Austria: SIGN AT; Spain: SIGN ES (Veri*factu/TicketBAI); Italy: SIGN IT; France: SIGN FR; Belgium: E-INVOICE BE; Portugal: SIGN PT (API + Guide only, no Quick Start)
- Global: HUB (Guide only), HUB Management API (API only), E-INVOICE, SAFE, RECEIPT (eReceipt)

**Nav**: "Services" mega-dropdown, "Guides" mega-dropdown with per-country hover menus, Blog, external links (Support/Status/HUB/Dashboard/GitHub), language dropdown, Algolia search.

### API references (old)
- Rendered with **Redocusaurus** (Redoc inside Docusaurus) — **read-only, no try-it console** (verified; only false-positive 'try=it' string).
- **Versioning is inconsistent/mixed**: path-based semver for older products (kassensichv v0/v1/v2, dsfinvk v0/v1, ereceipt v0/v1, management v0, rksv v1, safe v0, sign-es v1 + a leaked `sign-es/v0.localhost` URL, sign-de/submission/v1) and **date-based** for Unified API products (sign-fr 2025-08-12 & 2026-02-03; sign-it 2024-10-31, 2025-08-12, 2026-02-03 + a leaked `sign-it/local`; e-invoice-be & e-invoice-de 2026-02-03). research.summary: fiskaly GmbH (Vienna, founded 2019 by Johannes Ferner/CEO, Simon Tragatschnig/COO, Patrick Gaubatz/CTO) positions itself as "Receipts made simple" / "make receipts easy for everyone" — an API-first, 100% cloud compliance layer (fiscalization, e-invoicing, digital receipts, archiving) for POS systems and omni-channel operators. As of mid-2026 it claims 1,600+ B2B customers, 1+ million active POS, 10+ billion digital signatures processed, ~110-120+ employees, and market leadership in German cloud TSE (SIGN DE recertified by BSI until March 30, 2033). Backed by Verdane (minority stake, June 2024, Freya XI fund) with Christian Nentwich (Duco founder) as Chairman since Feb 2025, it made two acquisitions in 2025: DF Deutsche Fiskal (from GK Software, March 2025) and InfraSec Sweden AB (from Yabie, December 2025), adding Sweden as a sixth active market. The portfolio now spans SIGN DE/AT/ES/IT/FR/PT, SUBMIT DE (ELSTER) and SUBMIT IT (POS-RT), DSFINVK DE, Fiskalcheck, E-INVOICE (global Peppol, plus E-INVOICE BE for Belgium's Jan 1, 2026 B2B mandate and E-INVOICE DE), SAFE archiving, RECEIPT digital receipts, and the HUB management UI. 2025-26 momentum rides regulatory waves: Spain VeriFactu (software-vendor compliance since July 2025; taxpayers Jan/Jul 2027), Italy's software-based corrispettivi certification and Jan 2026 POS-RT linkage, France's mandatory third-party POS certification (March 2026), Belgium Peppol mandate, and Poland KSeF (Feb/Apr 2026) flagged on the blog. Internally fiskaly launched an "AI-First initiative" in 2025 and is hiring an "Agentic Backend Engineer" (March 2026) to build multi-agent systems that automate its software development lifecycle (a "90/10" design-vs-code rule, Judge agents auditing Coder agents). Competitors include efsta, fiskaltrust, and RetailForce middleware (notably efsta/fiskaltrust also resell fiskaly's cloud TSE), hardware TSE vendors (Swissbit, cryptovision, D-Trust) in Germany, and incumbent RT/fiscal-printer vendors in Italy; fiskaly differentiates on pure-cloud, no-hardware, single-API, certified-until-2033 longevity.

key_facts.fact: Mission/tagline: "Receipts made simple"; internal phrasing "our mission is simple: make receipts easy for everyone"; positioning: APIs for fiscalization, e-invoicing and digital receipts built for POS systems and omni-channel operators ("Go VAT-Compliant across Europe")
Scale (mid-2026 homepage): 1 million+ active POS, more than 10 billion digital signatures processed, 1,600+ B2B customers; ISO 27001 and ISO 9001 certified

Founded 2019 in Vienna by Johannes Ferner (CEO), Simon Tragatschnig (COO), Patrick Gaubatz (CTO); 110-120+ employees from 40+ nationalities; offices in Vienna, Berlin/Frankfurt, Madrid, Milan (jobs also list Paris and Stockholm)

Ownership: self-sustaining until Verdane (European growth PE, Freya XI fund, €1.1bn) took a minority stake in June 2024; at that time 800+ customers, 500k+ POS, 850 tx/sec peak; Christian Nentwich (founder/ex-CEO of Duco) became Chairman of the Board and investor on Feb 14, 2025

Acquisition 1 (2025): DF Deutsche Fiskal GmbH bought from GK Software SE, announced March 27, 2025, closed March 31, 2025 (price undisclosed) — strengthens German store-based retail position; at the time fiskaly had 600k+ POS and 1,000+ B2B customers

Acquisition 2 (2025): InfraSec Sweden AB acquired from POS vendor Yabie AB, announced Dec 10, 2025 (closing subject to FDI approval) — Sweden's largest cloud VAT-compliance/control-unit provider, first vendor certified for SKVFS 2020:9; adds Sweden as fiskaly's sixth active market

SIGN DE Cloud TSS recertified by Germany's BSI (TR-03153) until March 30, 2033 — announced July 18, 2025; fiskaly claims #1 provider in Germany, >1 million cash registers, marketed against hardware TSEs whose certificates expired in/around 2025

Product portfolio (mid-2026): SIGN DE, SUBMIT DE (ELSTER), DSFINVK DE, Fiskalcheck (DE); SIGN AT (RKSV); SIGN ES (TicketBAI+VeriFactu+SII+NaTicket in one API); SIGN IT (+SIGN IT Lite) and SUBMIT IT (POS-RT); SIGN FR (NF525); SIGN PT (ATCUD/SAF-T/QR); E-INVOICE BE (new, Peppol), E-INVOICE global + E-INVOICE DE; SAFE/SAFE flex (10-yr archiving); RECEIPT (digital receipts); HUB (hub.fiskaly.com management UI, formerly dashboard)

Spain: fiskaly signed AEAT "Social Collaborator" agreement for VeriFactu submission (Feb 25, 2025); VeriFactu timeline — software vendors compliant since July 2025 (July 29 deadline), taxpayers Jan 1, 2027 (corporate income tax) and July 1, 2027 (all others); SIGN ES also covers Navarra's new NaTicket

Italy: fiskaly started AdE certification for SIGN IT (software-based corrispettivi, per AdE technical specs of March 7, 2025) positioning as alternative to Registratori Telematici; from Jan 1, 2026 payment terminals must link to fiscal devices (POS-RT) — SUBMIT IT handles the filing; AdE portal launch expected March 2026

France: SIGN FR launched July 31, 2025 (fifth fiscalization market) — Feb 2025 Finance Act ended self-attestation; POS vendors needed third-party certification (LNE/Infocert) initiated by Aug 31, 2025, mandatory March 1, 2026

Belgium: mandatory structured B2B e-invoicing (EN 16931 / Peppol BIS) from Jan 1, 2026 with 3-month tolerance; fiskaly launched E-INVOICE BE (send/receive via Peppol, API for POS/ERP vendors); near-real-time e-reporting planned 2028

AI strategy: "AI-First initiative" launched 2025 (per 2025 recap); March 2026 job posting for "Agentic Backend Engineer (Golang)" — design multi-agent workflows automating the full SDLC, 90% agent/system design vs 10% hands-on coding, "Judge" agents auditing "Coder" agents to keep fiscal signatures tamper-proof

Notable partners/customers: orderbird, Lightspeed, SumUp, ready2order, Tillhub, Uber, Oracle (Gold Partner; TicketBAI in Oracle OPERA/Simphony), MARKANT, CCV, Star Micronics, sevDesk, RoomRaccoon, eviivo, 3POS, speedy, Nissen & Velten, hokona (SAP Customer Checkout), Mastercard (digital receipt integration, June 2024)

Competitors: middleware efsta (17 countries), fiskaltrust (AT/DE/FR/IT/ES/PT/GR), RetailForce; German hardware TSEs Swissbit (TSE 2 BSI-certified April 2026), cryptovision, D-Trust, Epson; notably efsta and fiskaltrust use/resell fiskaly's cloud TSE — competitors double as channel partners

research.details:
## fiskaly GmbH — research notes (as of June 2026)

### 1. Identity, mission, positioning
- Legal entity: fiskaly GmbH, Vienna, Austria; subsidiary fiskaly Germany GmbH; DF Deutsche Fiskal GmbH (acquired); InfraSec Sweden AB (acquired). Source: https://www.northdata.com/fiskaly%20GmbH,%20Wien/504484p, fiskaly blog.
- Founded 2019 in Vienna by Johannes Ferner (CEO), Simon Tragatschnig (COO), Patrick Gaubatz (CTO). Source: https://www.fiskaly.com/about
- Taglines: footer "fiskaly. Receipts made simple"; homepage hero "Go VAT-Compliant across Europe — APIs for fiscalization, e-invoicing and digital receipts, built for POS systems and omni-channel operators"; job ad: "our mission is simple: make receipts easy for everyone"; press kit mission: "revolutionize fiscalization by making compliance seamless and effortless"; EuroShop quote: "We make compliance easy so retailers and POS providers can focus on their core business." InfraSec PR: mission "to be Europe's leading digital VAT compliance solution provider."
- Model: B2B2C — sells APIs to POS/ERP/invoicing software vendors and large omni-channel retailers; 100% cloud, no hardware; RESTful APIs, sandbox, dev support. "Millions of people interact with fiskaly every day, even if they don't realize it" (job ad).

### 2. Scale (mid-2026)
- 1,600+ B2B customers (homepage, EuroShop post, InfraSec PR).
- 1 million+ active POS / cash registers ("1 Million POS" homepage stat; "more than 1 million points of sale" InfraSec PR Dec 2025; 500k at Verdane investment Jun 2024; 600k at Deutsche Fiskal deal Mar 2025 — rapid growth).
- "More than 10 billion" digital signatures processed (homepage counter, mid-2026).
- Up to 850 transactions/second at peak (June 2024, Verdane post) — likely higher now.
- Employees: "more than 120" (InfraSec PR, Dec 2025); "over 110 team members, 40+ nationalities" (about page); Tracxn: 107 as of Jan 5, 2026.
- Offices: Vienna (HQ), Germany (Berlin; previously Frankfurt mentioned), Madrid, Milan; Greenhouse job locations also include Paris and Stockholm (post-InfraSec).
- Germany: claims "#1 provider" / "market-leading, operating in more than 1 million cash registers" (EuroShop 2026 post; InfraSec PR).
- Certifications: ISO 27001 (revalidated 2025), ISO 9001 (achieved April 7, 2025), BSI TR-03153 (SIGN DE).

### 3. Ownership & governance
- Bootstrapped/self-sustaining until June 2024.
- Verdane (Nordic/European growth PE) minority investment June 11, 2024 from Freya XI fund (€1.1bn hard cap, tickets €20-250m; exact amount/stake undisclosed). Source: https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/verdane-invests-in-fiskaly-for-international-growth, https://verdane.com/fiskaly-secures-investment-from-growth-investor-verdane/
- Christian Nentwich appointed Chairman of the Board Feb 14, 2025; also personally investing. Background: founder/CEO of Duco (data automation, exited to Nordic Capital), PhD CS UCL; expertise in scaling B2B software, compliance, AI, M&A. Source: https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/christian-nentwich-new-chairman-fiskaly
- Earlier angel: weXelerate (Crunchbase).
- Leadership additions (blog): Oliver Abl (Country Manager Germany), Silvio Agresti (CM Italy), Isabel Nogales (CM Spain), Madhumitha Murali (VP Finance), Michael Peter (VP People), Albert Kroisleitner (VP Product), Elias Priesching (VP Sales), Martin Breidenbach (MD Deutsche Fiskal post-acquisition).

### 4. M&A (2025)
**DF Deutsche Fiskal GmbH** (announced Mar 27, 2025; closed Mar 31, 2025)
- Seller: GK Software SE (wholly owned subsidiary); price undisclosed.
- Deutsche Fiskal = cloud fiscalization partner for large store-based retail in Germany (GK Software's retail customer base). Previous MDs Robeck & Scheibner exit to advisors; Martin Breidenbach becomes MD.
- fiskaly maintains separate certificate/terms pages for DF customers (fiskaly.com/certificates-df-deutsche-fiskal).
- Source: https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/fiskaly-acquires-deutsche-fiskal

**InfraSec Sweden AB** (announced Dec 10, 2025; closing subject to FDI approval; completed per Jan 2026 update)
- Seller: Yabie AB (Swedish POS vendor; remains an InfraSec customer). Yabie Group CEO: Siri Hyltén-Cavallius.
- InfraSec: "Sweden's largest provider of cloud-based VAT compliance solutions for POS"; delivering certified services since 2010; first vendor certified for Swedish SKVFS 2020:9 (cloud control unit) standard. CEO Sven Hammar.
- Rationale: Nordic expansion; "drive a new generation of fiscal services for the Nordic Area."
- Source: https://infrasec.se/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/fiskaly-InfraSec-News-English-Press-Release.pdf and https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/fiskaly-acquires-infrasec-sweden
- Mid-2026 homepage now says fiskaly is "currently active in Germany, Austria, Spain, Italy, France, and Sweden" and lists 🇸🇪 SKVFS 2020:9 among covered regulations (no separate "SIGN SE" product page yet; not on /solutions grid as of June 2026).

### 5. Product portfolio (mid-2026)
Per https://www.fiskaly.com/solutions and https://developer.fiskaly.com/ (API versions in parentheses):
- 🇩🇪 SIGN DE — cloud TSS for KassenSichV (api/kassensichv/v2), BSI-certified until March 30, 2033.
- 🇩🇪 SUBMIT DE — electronic cash register reporting to ELSTER (Mitteilungspflicht §146a AO; api/sign-de/submission/v1). Marketing: "Automate POS reporting in Germany 2025" webinar.
- 🇩🇪 DSFINVK DE — fiscal data export for tax audits (api/dsfinvk/v1).
- 🇩🇪 Fiskalcheck / FiskalcheckPOS — POS audit-readiness check app (fiskaly.com/fiskalcheck-app).
- 🇦🇹 SIGN AT — RKSV signature creation (api/rksv/v1); ex-"Obono" brand used by some PMS integrators. 2026 note: new 4.9% tax rate support blog.
- 🇪🇸 SIGN ES — single API for TicketBAI (Bizkaia/Gipuzkoa/Álava), VeriFactu, SII, NaTicket (Navarra) and upcoming B2B e-invoicing (api/sign-es/v1). AEAT Social Collaborator since Feb 2025.
- 🇮🇹 SIGN IT (+ SIGN IT Lite) — software-based corrispettivi transmission to Agenzia delle Entrate, alternative to fiscal printers/RT (api/sign-it/2026-02-03); SUBMIT IT — POS-RT connection compliance filing (Jan 1, 2026 obligation).
- 🇫🇷 SIGN FR — NF525-aligned cloud fiscalization: crypto signature chains, real-time fiscal journal, 7-year archive (api/sign-fr/2026-02-03). Launched July 31, 2025.
- 🇵🇹 SIGN PT — ATCUD, SAF-T, QR code, qualified electronic signature (api/sign-pt/2026-05-04 — newest API, May 2026).
- 🇧🇪 E-INVOICE BE — "New" — B2B e-invoicing send/receive via Peppol for Belgium's Jan 2026 mandate (api/e-invoice-be/2026-02-03).
- 🇩🇪 E-INVOICE DE — German B2B e-invoicing (api/e-invoice-de/2026-02-03); Germany mandate: receive from Jan 2025, phased issuance to 2028 (blog: e-invoicing-requirement-b2b-2026-germany).
- Global E-INVOICE — B2B/B2G via Peppol (fiskaly.com/electronic-invoicing-b2b), incl. validation of incoming invoices (blog: check-and-validate-incoming-e-invoices).
- SAFE & SAFE flex — 10-year compliant fiscal archiving, GoBD (api/safe/v0; fiskaly.com/safe-archive).
- RECEIPT — digital receipts (api/ereceipt/v1); integrations: Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, Mastercard auto-receipt (register card in fiskaly app → receipts delivered automatically; announced June 25, 2024). France anti-waste law (no default paper receipt) used as a driver.
- HUB — hub.fiskaly.com, "fiskaly's web interface (formerly the fiskaly dashboard) for managing your [services]" + Management API (api/management/v0) + DF Portal for Deutsche Fiskal customers.

### 6. Regulatory timelines fiskaly is riding (from its blog)
- **Germany**: hardware TSE certificate expirations through 2025 (D-Trust cloud TSE cert expired Sep 29, 2025) → switch-to-cloud campaign; e-invoicing B2B phase-in 2025-2028; coalition agreement for mandatory cash registers ("Kassenpflicht") from 2027 for businesses >€100k turnover (blogs: cash-register-mandate-2027-germany, coalition-agreement-mandatory-cash-registers); Appinio survey commissioned by fiskaly (Nov 28, 2025) on digital payment/receipt obligation and tax fraud.
- **Spain**: VeriFactu in force for software vendors since July 2025 (July 29 deadline; blogs: software-developers-verifactu-compliance-before-july-29, verifactu-now-mandatory-software-compliance-spain); taxpayers: Jan 1, 2027 (CIT payers), Jul 1, 2027 (others); TicketBAI Bizkaia extension into 2026; NaTicket (Navarra) emerging; B2B e-invoicing (Crea y Crece) royal decree still pending final timeline.
- **Italy**: AdE technical specs for software corrispettivi published Mar 7, 2025; fiskaly began certification May 14, 2025 ("pioneer" claim) — positions SIGN IT vs Registratori Telematici; Jan 1, 2026 POS-RT linkage obligation (penalties €100/transmission capped €1,000/quarter; €1,000-4,000 fines; 15-day to 6-month closure for repeat); AdE portal expected March 2026; RT device expirations in 2026 push cloud switch (blog: italy-2026-fiscal-device-expiration).
- **France**: Feb 2025 Finance Act (Art. 286 CGI) ends publisher self-attestation; third-party certification (LNE/Infocert) mandatory; deadlines Aug 31, 2025 (initiate) / Mar 1, 2026 (hold cert), with extension topic blog "pos-software-certification-france-deadline-extension-august-2026".
- **Belgium**: B2B structured e-invoicing mandatory Jan 1, 2026 (EN 16931/Peppol BIS; 3-month tolerance Jan-Mar 2026; near-real-time e-reporting planned 2028). fiskaly also published GKS 2.0/FDM (hospitality cash register) requirements blog — possible future fiscalization product.
- **Poland**: KSeF blog (Feb 18, 2026): large taxpayers (> PLN 200m) Feb 1, 2026; all VAT businesses Apr 1, 2026; FA(3) XML; no fiskaly PL product announced yet — monitoring/marketing stage.
- **Sweden**: blog (Sep 23, 2025) on SKVFS 2014:9/2014:10 and SKVFS 2021:17 (all registers must comply by Jan 1, 2027, standardized XML journal export); webinar "Unlocking fiscalization in Sweden"; InfraSec acquisition supplies the certified cloud control unit (SKVFS 2020:9).
- **Europe-wide**: pillar content "Fiscalisation in Europe: country-by-country 2026", "E-invoicing mandates in Europe 2026", "Electronic receipts Europe regulations", "third-party vs self-certification in European fiscalization" — SEO/thought-leadership for the compliance-as-API category. EV-charging fiscalization niche (Italy 2025, Austria RKSV) also covered.

### 7. Customers & partners (named)
- POS/hospitality software: orderbird, Lightspeed, SumUp, ready2order (named in both acquisition PRs); Tillhub, speedy, Kassemo, 3POS GmbH, Gastmanager GmbH, studiolution, Flour, bluebeetech (HORECA), Piantala (SIGN IT), Head On Solutions, Nissen & Velten (ERP), hokona (SAP Customer Checkout cloud TSE), Lina (cloud TSS story).
- Hotel/PMS: RoomRaccoon, littlehotelier, eviivo, Snap Consulting; Clock Software (uses fiskaly Obono for Austria).
- Enterprise/retail: Uber, MARKANT (retail cooperation), Oracle Gold Partner — TicketBAI compliance in Oracle OPERA & Simphony; Deutsche Fiskal brings GK Software's large store-based retail chains.
- Payments/hardware: Mastercard (digital receipts), CCV, Star Micronics, BlueStar ISV programme, Grabby Innovations, CCV SHOP.
- Channel/middleware: efsta partnership (fiskaly cloud TSE via efsta EFR), fiskaltrust (cloud TSE option) — i.e., nominal competitors distribute fiskaly's TSE. Sources: https://www.fiskaly.com/our-partners-and-customers, https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/news-fiskaly-tss-middleware
- Sweden: Yabie remains InfraSec customer.

### 8. Competitors & differentiation
- **efsta** (Austria): Electronic Fiscal Register middleware, claims 17 countries; partner+competitor. https://www.efsta.eu/en
- **fiskaltrust** (Austria/Germany): middleware + CloudCashBox SaaS, in AT/DE/FR/IT/ES/PT/GR; open APIs. https://fiskaltrust.eu/en-eu/
- **RetailForce**: single-interface fiscal client for many countries. https://www.retailforce.cloud/fiscal-middleware/?lang=en
- **German hardware TSE vendors**: Swissbit (Hardware TSE 2 BSI-certified April 2026 — hardware is NOT going away), cryptovision (SMAERS cert to Apr 2028), D-Trust (cloud TSE cert ended Sep 2025), Epson. fiskaly attacks on: certificate expiry risk, swap cost, no remote updates; SIGN DE's 2033 horizon is the differentiator. Sources: swissbit.com press, fiskaly hardware-TSS blogs.
- **Italy local RT/fiscal printer vendors** (Epson, RCH, Custom et al. — implied): fiskaly positions certified software corrispettivi as "true alternative to traditional fiscal devices," no hardware, central updates.
- Differentiation summary: pure cloud / API-first (vs middleware-on-premise or hardware), one vendor across 6+ countries + e-invoicing + receipts + archiving on one platform, formal certifications (BSI 2033, AdE process, AEAT collaborator, SKVFS via InfraSec), developer experience (developer.fiskaly.com, dev newsletter, sandboxes, GitHub SDKs at github.com/fiskaly).

### 9. AI/agents strategy signals
- "AI-First initiative" launched 2025 "to increase productivity and strengthen our competitive position" (2025 recap, Dec 29, 2025).
- Job: **Agentic Backend Engineer (Golang)**, first published Mar 5, 2026 (Greenhouse id 4797666101; locations Berlin/Madrid/Milan/Paris/Stockholm/Vienna). Quotes: "You are an Agentic Architect... design the frameworks that allow AI agents to write, test, and deploy our microservices. The 90/10 Rule: 90% designing agents, prompts, feedback loops and system architecture... only hands-on-keyboard for the final 10%." "Build the 'Machine': autonomous systems that take high-level requirements and output production-ready applications." "You will build the 'Judge' agents that audit the 'Coder' agents to ensure our fiscal signatures remain tamper-proof and legally compliant." "Proven track record of using AI to do the work of five engineers." Perks: "If an AI tool helps you code 20% faster, we want you to use it."
- Other open roles (June 2026): CS E-Invoicing Expert (pub. Jun 11, 2026 — e-invoicing scaling), Senior Backend Engineer Golang (Dec 2025), Staff Backend Engineer Golang (Apr 2026), Technical CSM & Pre-Sales (Mar 2026). All Golang backend — Go microservices stack.
- Chairman Nentwich's stated expertise includes AI; appointment framed around scaling + AI.

### 10. Awards & events 2024-2026
- EY Scale-up of the Year, Software & Digital Transformation B2B (Austria) — blog dated Jun 21, 2024; re-cited in 2025 recap and about page ("EY Scale-Up of the Year 2025"). 
- ISO 9001 achieved Apr 2025; ISO 27001 revalidated 2025; Great Place to Work 2024/25; Open IT Employer award; trend Top-100 Austrian startups (#19); LinkedIn Top 10 Startups Austria.
- Events: EuroCIS 2025 (Feb), EHI Tech Days Nov 2025, EuroShop 2026 Düsseldorf Feb 22-26 (Hall 6/F34; talk "Beyond Borders: Cloud Fiscalization & E-Invoicing 2026 in Europe", Feb 25), E-Rechnungs-Gipfel 2026 Berlin, FITUR 2024 Madrid, Salone dei Pagamenti (Italy), webinars (VeriFactu series, Italy specs, Sweden, France certification, Germany e-invoice).
- History: Finance Minister Brunner visit (AT), TeleTrust Innovation Award nomination 2023, P19 Payment Award 2022 (sustainability), first cloud TSS fully BSI-certified (to 2029, then 2033).

### 11. Useful URLs

- Dev portal: https://developer.fiskaly.com/ | HUB: https://hub.fiskaly.com/ | GitHub: https://github.com/fiskaly

checked.claim: Claim 11 (France): SIGN FR launched July 31, 2025; Feb 2025 Finance Act ended self-attestation; POS vendors needed third-party certification (LNE/Infocert) initiated by Aug 31, 2025, mandatory March 1, 2026
checked.note: The historical parts are accurate but the 'mandatory March 1, 2026' endpoint is wrong as of June 2026 — the mandate was first postponed, then repealed. CONFIRMED: Art. 43 of the Loi de finances 2025 removed the editor self-attestation option effective Feb 16, 2025, and publishers had to sign a certification engagement with an accredited body before Aug 31, 2025 (LNE: https://www.lne.fr/fr/actualites/2025/logiciels-caisses-suppression-auto-attestation-conformite-fiscale; BOFiP). REFUTED: certification never became mandatory on March 1, 2026. (1) DGFiP postponed the certificate-obtention deadline from March 1, 2026 to September 1, 2026 via BOFiP update ACTU-2025-00160 published Oct 1, 2025 (https://bofip.impots.gouv.fr/bofip/14826-PGP.html/ACTU-2025-00160), citing certification-body backlog. (2) Art. 125 of the Loi de finances pour 2026 then REVERSED the whole reform, re-establishing editor self-attestation as valid proof of compliance, applicable from Feb 21, 2026 (https://www.legifiscal.fr/actualites-fiscales/4460-loi-finances-2026-retablissement-auto-certification-logiciels-caisse.html; https://entreprendre.service-public.gouv.fr/actualites/A18087?lang=fr). Correct fact: as of June 2026, third-party NF525/LNE certification is no longer mandatory in France — self-attestation is again permitted — which undermines the stated regulatory driver for SIGN FR. The July 31, 2025 launch date itself rests only on fiskaly's own blog (not independently contradicted).

checked.claim: Claim 7 (Germany): SIGN DE Cloud TSS recertified by BSI (TR-03153) until March 30, 2033
checked.note: Verified directly against the BSI's own certificate registry, independent of fiskaly: certificate BSI-K-TR-0717-2025 for 'fiskaly sign Cloud-TSE' Version 1.0.15-1.5.0, holder fiskaly GmbH, certified 31.03.2025, valid through 30.03.2033 (https://www.bsi.bund.de/SharedDocs/Zertifikate_TR/Technische_Sicherheitseinrichtungen/BSI-K-TR-0717-2025.html). Earlier fiskaly certificates (BSI-K-TR-0403-2021, BSI-K-TR-0490-2021, BSI-K-TR-0522-2025) also appear in the BSI TSE list (https://www.bsi.bund.de/DE/Themen/Unternehmen-und-Organisationen/Standards-und-Zertifizierung/Zertifizierung-und-Anerkennung/Listen/Zertifizierte-Produkte-nach-TR/Technische_Sicherheitseinrichtungen/TSE_node.html). Caveats: the marketing claims '#1 provider in Germany' and '>1 million cash registers' come only from fiskaly's own material and could not be independently verified (the company's homepage figure of 1M+ active POS is self-reported); the 'announced July 18, 2025' date is fiskaly's blog-post date, while the certificate itself was issued March 31, 2025.

checked.claim: Claim 9 (Spain): VeriFactu timeline — software vendors compliant since July 2025 (July 29 deadline); taxpayers Jan 1, 2027 (corporate income tax) and July 1, 2027 (all others)
checked.note: Confirmed by multiple independent tax-compliance sources. Vendor deadline: software producers/marketers had to offer VeriFactu-compliant invoicing software from July 29, 2025 per RD 254/2025 (Marosa: https://marosavat.com/vat-news/verifactu-spain-2026-guide). Taxpayer deadlines: the 2027 dates are correct because Spain postponed VeriFactu a second time via Real Decreto-ley 15/2025 (approved Dec 2, 2025, ratified in Congress Dec 12, 2025), moving the original RD 254/2025 dates of Jan 1/July 1, 2026 to Jan 1, 2027 (corporate income tax payers) and July 1, 2027 (all other taxpayers) — KPMG (https://kpmg.com/us/en/taxnewsflash/news/2025/12/tnf-spain-verifactu-invoicing-system-delayed-to-2027.html), Sovos (https://sovos.com/regulatory-updates/vat/spain-further-postponement-of-verifactu-deadlines-officially-approved/), Fonoa (https://www.fonoa.com/resources/blog/spain-delays-verifactu-compliance-deadlines-to-2027), and the AEAT's own information note (https://sede.agenciatributaria.gob.es/Sede/en_gb/iva/sistemas-informaticos-facturacion-verifactu/nota-informativa-ampliacion-plazo-adaptacion-facturacion.html). Note the researcher's framing silently incorporates this December 2025 delay — an orchestrator should know these dates were 2026 until RD-ley 15/2025 moved them.

checked.claim: Claim 10 (Italy): AdE technical specs for software-based corrispettivi dated March 7, 2025; from Jan 1, 2026 payment terminals must be linked to fiscal devices (POS-RT); AdE portal/web service launch expected March 2026; fiskaly started AdE certification for SIGN IT
checked.note: Regulatory core independently confirmed. (1) AdE Provvedimento n. 111204 of March 7, 2025 defines the information to transmit and technical specs for realization, approval and release of software solutions (per art. 24 of D.Lgs. 1/2024) for electronic storage/telematic transmission of daily corrispettivi — AdE official page (https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/-/provvedimento-del-7-marzo-2025) and commentary (https://www.studiocerbone.com/definizione-delle-informazioni-da-trasmettere-e-delle-specifiche-tecniche-per-la-realizzazione-approvazione-e-rilascio-delle-soluzioni-software-di-cui-allarticolo-24-del-decreto-legislativo/). (2) POS-RT linkage obligation effective Jan 1, 2026 per 2025 Budget Law art. 1 paras 74-80 amending D.Lgs. 127/2015; the 'connection' is via an online web service in the AdE 'Fatture e Corrispettivi' portal, expected in the first days of March 2026, with existing POS to be linked by April 20, 2026; fines EUR 1,000-4,000 per unconnected device — PwC TLS (https://blog.pwc-tls.it/en/2025/11/18/from-2026-mandatory-integration-between-cash-registers-and-pos-procedures-deadlines-and-penalties/) and Fiscal Solutions (https://www.fiscal-requirements.com/news/5145, https://www.fiscal-requirements.com/news/5154). Caveat: the sub-claim that fiskaly itself 'started AdE certification for SIGN IT' rests solely on fiskaly's own blog; no independent confirmation (e.g., an AdE-published list of approved software solutions naming fiskaly) was found, though nothing contradicts it.
