checked.note: Core facts confirmed by sources independent of the claimed press-release PDF. FT Partners (Yabie's financial advisor) confirms announcement date Dec 10, 2025, seller Yabie AB, and describes InfraSec as among 'the largest providers of cloud-based VAT solutions for POS Systems' in Sweden (https://www.ftpartners.com/transactions/yabie). TrendingTopics (independent tech outlet) confirms the deal, that Yabie remains a customer, that closing is subject to FDI approval, and that fiskaly's existing markets are Germany, Austria, Spain, Italy and France — making Sweden the sixth, consistent with the claim though not labeled 'sixth' verbatim (https://www.trendingtopics.eu/fiskaly-uebernimmt-schwedischen-mitbewerber-infrasec/). PitchBook also records the Dec 10, 2025 acquisition (https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/485059-69). 'First vendor certified for SKVFS 2020:9' (Nov 2024) comes from InfraSec's own site (https://infrasec.se/ and https://www.infrasec.se/ccu — CCU listed among Skatteverket-approved control units); not contradicted anywhere, but the 'first' superlative is self-reported rather than verified against Skatteverket's certification list.

research.summary: Italy's fiscalization regime is at an inflection point in mid-2026. Since 2019-2020, retail receipts (corrispettivi) have required either a hardware Registratore Telematico (RT) or the AdE's web procedure ("Documento Commerciale on line"); art. 24 of D.Lgs. 1/2024 created a legal fourth channel — a certified pure-software solution — operationalized by Agenzia delle Entrate Provvedimento n. 111204 of 7 March 2025, which defines a PEM/PEL architecture (fiscal modules MF1/MF2), a producer/erogatore accreditation model, third-party certification, and approval by the Commission for fiscal measuring devices. Technical specs have iterated rapidly (v1.0 Feb 2025 → v1.2 on 2 Dec 2025 adding ISO 9001/27001 requirements → v1.3 current, with annexes updated 17 April 2026); a 27 May 2026 working group (AdE, Sogei, AssoSoftware, certifiers) confirmed full market operation of certified software solutions from 2027, with no approved solutions yet publicly listed and fiskaly publicly in the certification pipeline for SIGN IT. Separately, the 2025 Budget Law (L. 207/2024, art. 1 commi 74-77) made POS-RT linkage mandatory from 1 January 2026: implemented by Provvedimento n. 424470 of 31 October 2025 as a purely logical (web-based) association of POS Terminal IDs to RT serial numbers in the Fatture e Corrispettivi portal, live since 5 March 2026 with a 45-day window (deadline 20 April 2026) for pre-existing devices and rolling monthly windows thereafter; sanctions are €1,000-4,000 plus possible license suspension. The receipt lottery continues weekly/monthly/annual draws for cashless purchases with an 8-char lottery code on the documento commerciale, while the instant lottery (DL 36/2022) remains dormant despite ~1.6M RTs being adapted by the 2 Oct 2023 deadline, because the ADM-AdE implementing decree/prize funding never materialized. Core penalties for memorization/transmission failures are 70% of VAT (min €300) plus business-suspension accessories, and €100 per late/omitted transmission (capped €1,000/quarter) for formal violations. Market context: ~1.7M RTs serving 1.5M+ merchants, ~80% reaching end-of-life by 2027 (fiskaly/Format Research), which is the commercial window fiskaly's SIGN IT (cloud fiscalization) and SUBMIT IT (POS-RT linkage automation) target. For integrators, the pain points are the multi-actor certification chain, evolving specs, daily-closing/12-day transmission discipline, offline/malfunction emergency procedures, returns/voids document types, lottery code handling, and now POS Terminal-ID lifecycle management — exactly the complexity that documentation quality must absorb.

key_facts.fact: Provvedimento Agenzia delle Entrate n. 111204 of 7 March 2025 defines the rules and technical specifications for building, certifying, approving and releasing 'soluzioni software' for electronic memorization and telematic transmission of daily corrispettivi, implementing art. 24 of D.Lgs. 8 January 2024, n. 1

The software solution architecture is built on two interdependent fiscal modules: MF1 installed on the PEM (punto di emissione — the device issuing receipts: PC, tablet, smartphone, SmartPOS) and MF2 on the PEL (punto di elaborazione — provider backend that fiscally stores transaction detail and transmits the daily corrispettivi XML to AdE)

Approval path for a software solution: producer pre-accreditation with AdE → development per technical specs → conformity certification by an authorized/accredited certification body → evaluation by the Commissione per l'approvazione degli apparecchi misuratori fiscali (D.M. 23 March 1983) → approval decree by the AdE Director; AdE maintains a public register of accredited erogatori and approved solutions

Technical specifications have iterated: v1.0 (February 2025, attached to provv. 111204), v1.1 (mid-2025), v1.2 published 2 December 2025 (adding ISO certification requirements for erogatori), v1.3 current with annexes (Code List, AttivazionePEM-Esito, Corrispettivi-Esito) updated 17 April 2026

A 27 May 2026 working group between Agenzia delle Entrate, Sogei, AssoSoftware and accredited certification bodies confirmed the roadmap: certified software solutions enter full operation from 2027 as the fourth transmission channel (alongside hardware RT, the web procedure, and RT with BLOB technology); pilot results positive, but producer certification timelines, liability allocation, and e-payment integration specs remain open

Italian Budget Law 2025 (Legge 30 dicembre 2024, n. 207, art. 1 commi 74-77) makes it mandatory from 1 January 2026 to link electronic payment instruments (POS) with the corrispettivi certification instrument (RT or 'Documento Commerciale on line' procedure), with payment data memorized at transaction time and transmitted daily in aggregate to AdE

Provvedimento AdE n. 424470 of 31 October 2025 defines the POS-RT linkage as exclusively digital/logical (no physical connection): merchants associate RT serial numbers (matricola) with POS identifying data (Terminal ID + financial operator) via a web procedure in the Fatture e Corrispettivi reserved area

Per AdE official FAQ (updated 25 March 2026): the POS-RT web procedure went live 5 March 2026; POS owned at 1 Jan 2026 or activated in January must be linked within 45 days of 5 March (i.e. by 20 April 2026); later activations/variations must be registered between the 6th day of the second month after activation and the last working day of that month; SoftPOS and virtual instruments are in scope, bank transfers are not
Sanction for failing to link POS with RT: €1,000-€4,000 administrative fine plus possible accessory suspension of the license/authorization to operate

Core corrispettivi penalties (post-reform D.Lgs. 87/2024, in force 1 Sept 2024): 70% of the VAT (minimum €300) for omitted/unfaithful memorization or transmission (was 90%/€500); €100 fixed per transmission for late/omitted transmission with correct memorization, capped at €1,000 per quarter; accessory suspension of activity 3 days-1 month (1-6 months if contested corrispettivi exceed €50,000); RT tampering €3,000-€12,000

Receipt lottery (lotteria degli scontrini) in 2026: weekly (every Thursday), monthly and annual drawings continue; participation requires electronic/cashless payment and showing the customer's lottery code (from lotteriadegliscontrini.gov.it) at purchase — 1 virtual ticket per euro spent (min €1)

The instant lottery (lotteria istantanea, DL 36/2022 art. 18 c. 4-bis, PNRR measure) is still not live as of late 2025/2026: ~1.6 million RTs were adapted (provv. AdE 18 January 2023 required 2D/QR code emission on receipts, deadline 2 October 2023, RT specs v11; €50 adaptation bonus per provv. 23 June 2023, ~€80M allocated) but the joint ADM-AdE implementing decree and prize funding were never finalized

Adoption stats: ~1.7 million RTs in use by 1.5+ million merchants (mostly installed 2019-2020), vs 3.3+ million active POS terminals (Banca d'Italia); fiskaly/Format Research estimate ~80% of RTs reach end-of-life by 2027 (designed for ~2,500 daily closings ≈ 8 years), 95% of merchants still use fiscal printers, ~70% investing or planning to invest in cloud/software fiscalization in 2026-27

fiskaly has officially started certification of SIGN IT (API-first cloud fiscalization for Italy), offers SIGN IT lite (compliant receipt issuance/transmission via the AdE web-procedure channel) today, and launched SUBMIT IT, an API product that automates the POS-RT linkage filing for retailers, ISVs and PSPs, integrating with AdE web services available since March 2026

Daily corrispettivi must be transmitted within 12 days of the transaction; emergency procedures exist for network absence ('Assenza di rete' — manual upload of the RT-sealed XML via Fatture e Corrispettivi), device malfunction ('Dispositivo fuori servizio' — manual data entry plus paper/electronic emergency register and mandatory technician intervention)

research.details:
## 1. The corrispettivi telematici regime — channels as of mid-2026

Legal foundation: art. 2, comma 1, D.Lgs. 5 agosto 2015, n. 127 — obligation of electronic memorization and telematic transmission of daily corrispettivi (in force for all merchants since 1 Jan 2020; larger merchants 1 July 2019). Certification instrument output is the **documento commerciale** (D.M. 7 dicembre 2016), not the old paper scontrino fiscale.

Four channels (confirmed by the 27 May 2026 AdE/Sogei/AssoSoftware working group — [Studio Pizzano](https://www.studiopizzano.it/corrispettivi-telematici-via-software-la-road-map-verso-il-2027-e-confermata/), [Studio PAA, 29 May 2026](https://www.studiopaa.com/notizie/post/2026-05-29-4317-corrispettivi-trasmissione-tramite-software-dal-2027/)):
1. **Hardware Registratore Telematico (RT)** / Server RT — certified fiscal device with fiscal memory, sealed by authorized technicians (laboratori abilitati), periodic biennial verification.
2. **Web procedure "Documento Commerciale on line"** in the AdE Fatture e Corrispettivi portal (per-transaction manual entry; what fiskaly SIGN IT lite automates).
3. **RT with BLOB technology** (variant referenced in the 2026 roadmap discussions).
4. **NEW: certified Soluzione Software (SSW)** — pure software, no fiscal hardware. Legal basis art. 24, D.Lgs. 8 gennaio 2024, n. 1 ("decreto Adempimenti", in force January 2024). **Full market operation from 2027**.

## 2. The software solution (SSW) — provvedimenti, architecture, certification

### Regulatory timeline (precise)
- **8 Jan 2024** — D.Lgs. 1/2024, art. 24: creates the legal option of software-only memorization/transmission ([fiskaly blog](https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/corrispettivi-telematici-software-as-an-alternative-to-telematic-registers)).
- **July 2024** — AdE notified the draft to the EU Commission (TRIS technical-regulation procedure) ([Agenda Digitale](https://www.agendadigitale.eu/documenti/corrispettivi-addio-ai-registratori-telematici-arriva-il-nuovo-software-ecco-come-usarlo/)).
- **7 Mar 2025** — **Provvedimento n. 111204/2025** (Prot. AGEDC001.111204): defines information to transmit + technical specs for realization, approval and release of SSW ([AdE provvedimento page](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/-/provvedimento-del-7-marzo-2025); [PDF](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/20143/8792480/ProvvSSW-111204-2025.pdf/98b21093-4ea5-9ac8-5147-7339a02c632a)). Same day, **provvedimento n. 33796/2025** updated the general corrispettivi technical specs ([Indicom](https://indicom.eu/conservazione-digitale-a-norma-it/nuovo-provvedimento-dellade-specifiche-tecniche-per-la-gestione-dei-corrispettivi-giornalieri/)).
- Spec versions: **v1.0 Feb 2025** (allegato to 111204); **v1.1** mid-2025 ([AdE](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/d/guest/specifichetecniche_v-1-1)); **v1.2 published 2 Dec 2025** — added ISO certification requirements for erogatori ([AdE PDF](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/20143/9495794/SpecificheTecniche_v.1.2.pdf), timestamp Dec 2025); **v1.3 current**, annexes updated **17 April 2026** ([AdE spec page](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/specifiche-tecniche-allegati-invio-corrispettivi-soluzione-software-imprese)).
- **25 Feb 2026** — AdE published an updated list of certification bodies (enti certificatori) for software solutions ([fiskaly certification-requirements blog](https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/cloud-fiscalization-in-italy-2026-certification-requirements)).
- **27 May 2026** — working group AdE + Sogei + AssoSoftware + certifiers: roadmap to 2027 confirmed; pilot positive, no feasibility objections; **no count of approved solutions disclosed** — no approved solution publicly listed yet as of June 2026.

### Architecture (PEM/PEL, MF1/MF2)
- **PEM (Punto di Emissione)**: merchant-side device (PC/tablet/smartphone/SmartPOS) running **MF1 (Modulo Fiscale 1)**: secure transaction recording, documento commerciale emission, payment-data capture, lottery management. Census + activation flow ("Censimento ed attivazione PEM" YAML/XSD).
- **PEL (Punto di Elaborazione)**: erogatore-managed backend running **MF2 (Modulo Fiscale 2)**: validation, fiscal storage of transaction-level detail, long-term digital preservation, transmission of the **daily corrispettivi XML** to AdE, audit-support REST APIs exposed to AdE.
- Integrity: **hash-chained daily journal** (tamper evidence), minimum **48-hour local retention** at the PEM, bidirectional PEM-PEL web services, **real-time audit** capability for inspectors ([Agenda Digitale](https://www.agendadigitale.eu/documenti/corrispettivi-addio-ai-registratori-telematici-arriva-il-nuovo-software-ecco-come-usarlo/)).
- Published artifacts on the AdE spec page: XSDs for Corrispettivi, Corrispettivi-EM, DocumentoCommerciale, Journal, Metadati, Catalogo, CensimentoPEM, AttivazionePEM-Esito, RichiestaAudit, Segnalazioni(+Esito); REST API specs (Audit APIs exposed by erogatore; APIs exposed by AdE); YAML OpenAPI files (trasmissione corrispettivi giornalieri, comunica stato PEM, censimento/attivazione PEM, audit journal/documento commerciale); **SBOM guidelines** (Allegato-SSW-LineeGuidaSBOM); archive and audit-services guidelines.

### Actors & certification chain
- **Produttore** (software producer): pre-accredits with AdE; builds per specs; must hold **ISO 9001 + ISO/IEC 27001** (per v1.2 requirements); submits solution to an authorized **ente certificatore** (independent certification body; early commentary mentioned universities/CNR-type labs); then AdE approval upon favorable opinion of the **Commissione per l'approvazione degli apparecchi misuratori fiscali** (art. 5, D.M. 23 marzo 1983).
- **Erogatore** (service provider): accredits with AdE; operates the PEL; legally responsible for fiscal storage, correct operation, and audit availability; activates approved solutions for merchants.
- **Esercente** (merchant): uses the PEM; obligations shift largely to provider side ("software responsibility over hardware compliance" — [fiskaly](https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/cloud-fiscalization-in-italy-2026-certification-requirements)).
- AdE keeps a **public register** of accredited erogatori and approved solutions ([Studio Cerbone full provvedimento text](https://www.studiocerbone.com/definizione-delle-informazioni-da-trasmettere-e-delle-specifiche-tecniche-per-la-realizzazione-approvazione-e-rilascio-delle-soluzioni-software-di-cui-allarticolo-24-del-decreto-legislativo/)).

### Open issues flagged (May 2026 working group)
1. Producer certification timelines → market availability risk for the 2027 start.
2. Liability allocation among producer / erogatore / merchant on malfunction.
3. **Technical specs for integrating electronic payments (POS-RT equivalent) into SSW still pending** — GTN (Dec 2025) circulated industry notes on "Collegamento RT_Pos_SSW" ([GTN PDF](https://www.gtngroup.it/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/GTN-Aggiornamenti-e-integrazioni-della-Soluzione-Software-05122025_Collegamento_RT_Pos_SSW.pdf)).

## 3. Budget Law 2025 — POS-RT linkage (what SUBMIT IT addresses)

- **Norm**: L. 30 dicembre 2024, n. 207 (Legge di Bilancio 2025), **art. 1, commi 74-77**, effective **1 January 2026**: (a) obligation to **link** payment instruments to the corrispettivi certification instrument; (b) obligation to **memorize** payment method/amount at transaction time and **transmit daily, in aggregate**, electronic-payment data together with corrispettivi ([Baker Tilly](https://www.bakertilly.it/en/insights/registratori-telematici-strumenti-di-pagamento-elettronico-obbligo-di-collegamento-dal-2026-modalit%C3%A0); [Studio Stefani](https://www.studiopaolostefani.it/2026/03/03/dal-05-03-2026-scatta-lobbligo-di-collegare-i-registratori-di-cassa-agli-strumenti-di-pagamento-elettronici-utilizzati-art-1-commi-da-74-a-77-legge-207-2024-e-provvedimento-del-direttore-d/)).
- **Provvedimento attuativo: n. 424470 del 31 ottobre 2025** ([FIPE](https://www.fipe.it/2025/11/03/provv-ade-n-424470-2025-modalita-operative-per-il-collegamento-tra-registratori-di-cassa-e-strumenti-di-pagamento-elettronico/); [AdE press release 31/10/2025](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/d/guest/059_com-st-provv-rt-pos-31-10-2025); [Altalex](https://www.altalex.com/documents/news/2025/11/11/pagamenti-elettronici-dal-1-gennaio-2026-nuove-regole-collegare-registratori-cassa-pos)). Linkage is **logical, not physical**: a web association in Fatture e Corrispettivi between RT matricola and POS identifiers. No RT replacement/adaptation needed for the association itself.
- **Operational guide** published by AdE ~20 Feb 2026 ([Fisco Oggi](https://www.fiscooggi.it/portale/-/collegamento-tra-registratori-e-pos-istruzioni-in-una-guida-operativa); [fiskaly](https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/operational-guide-for-pos-rt-connection)).
- **Official FAQ, 30 items, updated 25 March 2026** ([AdE PDF](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/d/guest/faq_collegamento_pos_rt_2503)) — full contents verified:
  - Web procedure available from **5 March 2026** (FAQ 3).
  - POS already held at 1 Jan 2026 or activated in January: link **within 45 days from 5 March 2026** → deadline **20 April 2026** ([Confesercenti](https://www.confesercenti.it/blog/registratori-di-cassa-e-pos-20-aprile-e-il-termine-per-per-fare-labbinamento-online-gli-strumenti-di-pagamento-attivi-a-gennaio-2026/)).
  - Variations/new POS after 31 Jan 2026: register between the **6th day of the second month** after activation and the **last working day of that same month** (e.g. POS activated April 2026 → 6-30 June 2026) (FAQ 12).
  - Who: anyone certifying corrispettivi via RT **or** the "Documento Commerciale on line" web procedure who collects electronically (FAQ 5).
  - In scope: physical POS (Terminal ID + acquirer fiscal code/denomination), **SoftPOS apps**, and **virtual instruments** (online payment platforms — identified by acquirer data only) (FAQ 14). Acquirer-reported instruments are pre-loaded into the procedure (FAQ 15); manual entry with convention contract number for missing ones (FAQ 16); franchisor-owned POS must be manually added and linked (FAQ 26).
  - Out of scope: **bank transfers** (FAQ 11); POS used exclusively for exempt sales (tobacco, fuel, distance sales etc.) (FAQ 9), but mixed-use POS must be linked (FAQ 7-8).
  - Many-to-many allowed: one physical POS ↔ multiple RTs at the same address; virtual POS across venues; one RT ↔ multiple POS (FAQ 18, 20). "Dispositivo ambulante" flag for itinerant traders (FAQ 21, 30). "POS non collegati" function to disclaim non-owned/returned/exempt-only POS (FAQ 22-24).
  - Payment-type mapping rules on the documento commerciale: cheques = "contante"; consumer-credit disbursements = "pagamento elettronico"; goods delivered before payment → "NonPagato" mode must be programmed in the RT (FAQ 27, 29). Delegation to intermediaries possible for the RT linkage service, not for the DC-online linkage (FAQ 4).
- **Sanctions**: failure to link → **€1,000-€4,000** + accessory suspension of license/activity. Incorrect daily payment-data transmission → **€100 per transmission, max €1,000/quarter** ([Confindustria Toscana](https://confindustriatoscanacentroecosta.it/collegamento-tra-pos-e-registratori/), [Altalex](https://www.altalex.com/documents/news/2025/11/11/pagamenti-elettronici-dal-1-gennaio-2026-nuove-regole-collegare-registratori-cassa-pos)).
- Interpello **Risposta n. 44/2026** AdE addresses edge cases ([AdE PDF](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/20143/9680917/Risposta+n.+44_2026.pdf)).
- **fiskaly SUBMIT IT** ([product page](https://www.fiskaly.com/submit-it)): single API automating the POS-RT association filing ("zero manual intervention") for software vendors/POS providers, acquirers/PSPs, enterprise retailers; complements SIGN IT (SIGN IT = receipts, SUBMIT IT = terminal-device linkage); integration finalized against AdE web services live since March 2026; 1,600+ existing fiskaly customers cited.

## 4. Lotteria degli scontrini

- **Deferred/weekly regime (active in 2026)**: weekly draws every Thursday (15 prizes €25k), monthly and annual (€5M) draws; cashless payment only + customer **codice lotteria** (8-char alphanumeric obtained at lotteriadegliscontrini.gov.it) shown at checkout; 1 virtual ticket per euro (purchases ≥ €1); RT transmits lottery data with the documento commerciale ([Informazione Fiscale](https://www.informazionefiscale.it/lotteria-degli-scontrini-2026-estrazioni-premi); [official portal](https://www.lotteriadegliscontrini.gov.it/portale/)). Merchant refusal to accept the lottery code can be reported by consumers.
- **Instant lottery (lotteria istantanea)**: legal basis **art. 18, comma 4-bis, DL 36/2022** (PNRR-2 decree). **Provvedimento AdE 18 January 2023**: RTs to be adapted to print a **2D/QR code** on the documento commerciale for instant-win verification via the "Gioco Legale" app; adaptation deadline **2 October 2023**; RT technical specs **version 11** (mandatory for new models approved after 30 June 2023); **€50 tax-credit bonus** per adaptation, operational via provvedimento **23 June 2023** (~€80M allocated) ([Studio Locatelli](https://www.studiolocatelli.com/lotteria-degli-scontrini-istantanea-adeguamento-registratori-telematici-entro-il-2-ottobre-2023-provvedimento-18-01-2023/); [Informazione Fiscale](https://www.informazionefiscale.it/lotteria-scontrini-istantanea-2023-scadenza-2-ottobre-adeguamento-registratori-cassa)).
- **Status**: still dormant as of Dec 2025-mid-2026 — ~**1.6 million RTs adapted** but the joint Agenzia Dogane e Monopoli + AdE decree and the prize fund were never finalized ([Il Fatto Quotidiano, 1 Dec 2025](https://www.ilfattoquotidiano.it/2025/12/01/lotteria-scontrini-istantanea-ferma-notizie/8212776/); [Eutekne](https://www.eutekne.info/Sezioni/Art_894365_la_lotteria_degli_scontrini_diventa_istantanea.aspx)). Integrators must nevertheless support lottery-code capture and the QR/2D-code capability in RT firmware and in SSW MF1 ("lottery management" is an MF1 function per the SSW specs).

## 5. Penalties (corrispettivi, post D.Lgs. 87/2024 reform, in force 1 Sept 2024)

Source: [AdE — Se non si rispetta l'obbligo](https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/se-non-si-rispetta-l-obbligo); [Studio Pizzano](https://www.studiopizzano.it/corrispettivi-telematici-inviati-in-ritardo-guida-alle-sanzioni/).

| Violation | Sanction | Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Omitted/unfaithful memorization or transmission (substantive) | **70% of VAT, min €300** (was 90%/€500 pre-Sept 2024); applied once per operation even if both fail | art. 6 c.2-bis, D.Lgs. 471/1997 |
| RT malfunction / irregular functioning | 70% of VAT | art. 6 D.Lgs. 471/1997 |
| Late/omitted transmission, correctly memorized (formal) | **€100 per transmission, max €1,000/quarter**, no cumulo giuridico | art. 11 c.2-quinquies D.Lgs. 471/1997 |
| Failure to request technician maintenance / biennial verification | €250-€2,000 | D.Lgs. 471/1997 |
| 4 distinct violations on different days within 5 years | **Suspension of license/activity 3 days-1 month**; 1-6 months if contested corrispettivi > €50,000 | art. 12 c.2 D.Lgs. 471/1997 |
| RT tampering | €3,000-€12,000 + suspension 15 days-2 months (2-6 months repeat) + possible criminal liability | — |
| POS-RT non-linkage (from 2026) | €1,000-€4,000 + accessory suspension | L. 207/2024 |

Transmission window: **12 days** from the operation (daily memorization mandatory at transaction time). Ravvedimento operoso available for late transmission.

## 6. Adoption stats / market context

([Giornale delle PMI / fiskaly-Format Research Osservatorio Fiscalità Digitale](https://www.giornaledellepmi.it/rivoluzione-fiscale-per-oltre-15-milioni-di-esercenti-l80-dei-registratori-di-cassa-telematici-sara-obsoleto-entro-il-2027/); [TechFromTheNet, 13 May 2026](https://techfromthenet.it/2026/05/13/la-digitalizzazione-fiscale-manda-in-pensione-i-registratori-di-cassa-telematici/); [Pagamenti Digitali](https://www.pagamentidigitali.it/payment-regulation/pos-e-registratori-di-cassa-telematici-collegati-tutto-quello-che-ce-da-sapere/)):
- ~**1.7M RTs** in use by **1.5M+ merchants**, mostly installed 2019-2020; vs **3.3M+ active POS terminals** (Banca d'Italia 2025).
- **~80% of RTs end-of-life by 2027** (designed for ~2,500 daily closings ≈ 8 years).
- 95% of merchants still on fiscal printers; ~70% investing/planning cloud-software fiscalization 2026-27; 77% aware of the software option, 30% well-informed, ~50% intend to adopt soon; cloud claimed savings up to 50% vs hardware RT lifecycle. Sector adoption intent: clothing 90.9%, body shops 89%, tourism 84.7%. Regional: NW 74.1%, Centre 73.8%, NE 71.1%.
- **Software RT (SSW) adoption = 0 in production** as of June 2026 (no approved solutions listed; first approvals expected to enable the 2027 start). fiskaly publicly in certification ([fiskaly](https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/fiskaly-a-pioneer-in-the-certification-of-cloud-based-fiscalization-in-italy)); fiskaly Italy country manager: Silvio Agresti.

## 7. Integrator (POS software vendor) end-to-end obligations & pain points in 2026

**Today (hardware-RT world):** integrate with a certified RT/fiscal printer (proprietary protocols per manufacturer: Epson, Custom, RCH...); ensure documento commerciale layout compliance (D.M. 7/12/2016), correct VAT ventilation, payment-type fields (Contante/Elettronico/Ticket/NonPagato); daily closing (chiusura giornaliera) generating the sealed XML; transmission within 12 days; **resi e annulli** (return and void documents referencing the original DC); lottery code capture + (adapted) QR emission; emergency flows (assenza di rete: manual XML upload; dispositivo fuori servizio: manual entry on portal + emergency register + technician call-out); biennial verification logistics; from 2026, **POS Terminal-ID/RT association lifecycle** (initial filing by 20 Apr 2026, rolling windows on every POS swap/return/new venue, "POS non collegati" disclaimers, franchising/ambulante edge cases) — a recurring administrative process SUBMIT IT automates.

**Tomorrow (SSW world, from 2027):** become/partner with an accredited **erogatore**; integrate MF1 into the POS app (PEM census/activation, hash-chained journal, 48h local retention, offline behavior, DC emission incl. lottery); rely on PEL for storage/transmission/audit APIs; survive certification (ISO 9001/27001 + ente certificatore conformity + Commissione + AdE approval) and keep pace with spec churn (v1.0→v1.3 in 14 months; annexes changed again 17 Apr 2026).

**Documented pain points:**
- **Certification bottleneck**: multi-stage (producer accreditation → ISO certs → third-party conformity certification → Commission → AdE decree); timelines explicitly flagged as a market-availability risk at the 27 May 2026 meeting; certifier list only published 25 Feb 2026.
- **Spec instability**: 4 spec versions in ~14 months; payment-integration specs for SSW still undefined; liability allocation unresolved.
- **AdE/portal availability**: dedicated emergency procedures exist precisely because AdE-side rejections (trasmissione scartata), network absence and device failure are routine operational events; web-procedure POS-RT service launched 2 months late (March vs January) compressing the compliance window ([FiscoeTasse emergency guide](https://www.fiscoetasse.com/approfondimenti/13501-trasmissione-telematica-corrispettivi-come-gestire-i-casi-di-emergenza.html); [Circolare 16/2023 anomalie](https://www.studiocom.it/2023/03/31/circolare-n-16-2023-corrispettivi-telematici-anomalie-in-sede-di-invio-e-chiarimenti-agenzia-entrate/)).
- **Daily closings**: missed chiusura giornaliera triggers the 70%/€300 regime; RT wear is literally measured in closings (2,500-closing design life driving the 2027 obsolescence wave).
- **Returns/voids**: dedicated DC types (documento commerciale per reso / per annullo) with RT-specific functions; error correction after transmission is procedural and error-prone ([Pierluca & Associati](https://www.pierlucaeassociati.it/sp/scontrino-elettronico-errato.3sp); [SumUp guide](https://www.sumup.com/it-it/gestire-attivita/fiscalita-normative/annullamento-scontrino-fiscale/)).
- **Lottery**: code capture mandatory on request; instant-lottery QR capability built and paid for (~€80M) but feature dormant — integrators must keep dead capability compliant.
- **POS-RT filing**: per-merchant, per-device, deadline-windowed administrative filings with acquirer data dependencies (Terminal IDs often unknown to merchants — AdE FAQ 17 tells them to ask their acquirer) — the concrete gap SUBMIT IT monetizes.

**Why documentation quality matters for fiskaly's entry**: the regime spans 3 laws, 4+ provvedimenti, 4 spec versions, 30 FAQs and several emergency procedures updated as recently as 17 April/25 March 2026; merchants and ISVs face sanction exposure on daily-cadence operations; competitors' guidance is fragmented across Italian-only commercialista circulars. Clear, current, English+Italian developer documentation is itself a compliance feature.
open_questions: Exact publication date of technical specifications v1.1 and v1.3 for the soluzione software (v1.0 Feb 2025 and v1.2 = 2 Dec 2025 are confirmed; v1.3 annexes updated 17 April 2026 but the v1.3 release date itself was not pinned down)

Whether any soluzione software has actually received formal AdE approval (decreto di approvazione) as of June 2026 — the May 27, 2026 working group did not disclose counts; the AdE register of approved solutions/erogatori should be checked directly
Names on the AdE list of accredited certification bodies (enti certificatori) published 25 Feb 2026, and typical certification cost/duration for a producer
Precise status of fiskaly SIGN IT certification (which stage: producer accreditation, conformity certification, or Commission review) as of June 2026

Pending technical specifications for integrating electronic payment data into the soluzione software channel (SSW equivalent of the POS-RT linkage) — announced as 'in regulatory definition' with no provvedimento yet
Whether the instant lottery will receive the joint ADM-AdE decree and prize funding in the 2026 fiscal cycle (Budget Law 2026 was not checked for lottery allocations)
Actual enforcement statistics for the POS-RT obligation after the 20 April 2026 deadline (number of merchants compliant, any sanction moratorium)

checked.claim: Claim 1: Provvedimento AdE n. 111204 of 7 March 2025 defines rules and technical specifications for building, certifying, approving and releasing software solutions for electronic memorization and telematic transmission of daily corrispettivi, implementing art. 24 of D.Lgs. 8 January 2024, n. 1
checked.note: Independently corroborated by multiple non-AdE sources: AteneoWeb lists 'Provvedimento Agenzia Entrate del 7.03.2025 (111204/2025)' (https://www.ateneoweb.com/rassegna-normativa/provvedimento-agenzia-entrate-del-7-03-2025-111204-2025/); Studio Cerbone reproduces the full title 'Definizione delle informazioni da trasmettere e delle specifiche tecniche per la realizzazione, approvazione e rilascio delle soluzioni software di cui all'articolo 24 del decreto legislativo 8 gennaio 2024, n. 1... corrispettivi giornalieri anonimi... art. 2 c.1 D.Lgs. 127/2015 - Provvedimento n. 111204 del 7 marzo 2025'; also confirmed by edotto.com, consulentidellavoro.it, quotidianopiu.it and the AdE portal page itself (https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/-/provvedimento-del-7-marzo-2025). Minor precision: it implements art. 24, comma 3, of D.Lgs. 1/2024, and the corrispettivi concerned are the 'anonymous daily corrispettivi' — consistent with the claim.

checked.claim: Claim 5: A 27 May 2026 working group between AdE, Sogei, AssoSoftware and accredited certification bodies confirmed the roadmap: certified software solutions enter full operation from 2027 as the fourth transmission channel (alongside hardware RT, web procedure, RT with BLOB); pilot results positive, but producer certification timelines, liability allocation and e-payment integration specs remain open
checked.note: Core confirmed by independent sources. ItaliaOggi article of 27 May 2026 (reproduced at https://www.assosoftware.it/dicono-di-noi/corrispettivi-software-italiaoggi-270526/, fetched directly): meeting organized by AssoSoftware with Agenzia delle Entrate and Sogei, attended by pilot-phase companies, prospective producers and 'soggetti certificatori, tra cui Consiglio Nazionale delle Ricerche e Politecnico di Milano'; 'la roadmap resta invariata e il 2027 segnerà l'avvio del progressivo superamento del registratore telematico fisico'; 'la sperimentazione... sta producendo risultati incoraggianti'; 'restano alcuni elementi da affinare sul piano operativo e normativo'. Studio PAA (29 May 2026, https://www.studiopaa.com/notizie/post/2026-05-29-4317-corrispettivi-trasmissione-tramite-software-dal-2027/) independently confirms the 27 May 2026 date, the participants, the fourth-channel framing (alongside physical devices, AdE web procedure, BLOB-technology RTs), and that technical specs for integration between certified software and electronic payment systems are 'ancora in fase di definizione regolamentare'. Caveat: the specific enumeration 'producer certification timelines' and 'liability allocation' as open items appears only in the claimed source (studiopizzano) and could not be independently confirmed; independent sources speak generically of operational/regulatory elements still to be refined plus the e-payment integration specs. Not enough to refute, and the load-bearing facts (date, participants, 2027 start, fourth channel, positive pilot) all check out.

checked.claim: Claim 6: Budget Law 2025 (L. 207/2024, art. 1 commi 74-77) makes it mandatory from 1 January 2026 to link POS with the corrispettivi certification instrument (RT or 'Documento Commerciale on line'), with payment data memorized at transaction time and transmitted daily in aggregate to AdE
checked.note: Multiple independent sources confirm every element. Adacta (https://adacta.it/collegamento-registratori-telematici-pos-obbligo-2026/): obligation effective 1 January 2026; RTs must 'memorizzare le informazioni sui pagamenti elettronici al momento dell'operazione' and 'trasmettere quotidianamente i dati in forma aggregata'. Studio Ripa (https://www.studioripa.it/in-evidenza/obbligo-di-collegamento-pos-registratore-telematico-rt-dal-1-gennaio-2026/) and Confindustria Toscana (https://confindustriatoscanacentroecosta.it/collegamento-tra-pos-e-registratori/) confirm L. 207/2024 art. 1 commi 74-77 and the 1 Jan 2026 start, implemented by Provv. AdE n. 424470 of 31 Oct 2025. The official AdE FAQ (faq_collegamento_pos_rt_2503, full PDF text extracted) confirms scope covers both RT users and the web procedure 'Documento Commerciale on line' (FAQ 5). Note: the legal obligation runs from 1 Jan 2026 but the operational registration window only opened 5 March 2026 — consistent with the claim.

checked.claim: Claim 8: Per AdE FAQ (updated 25 March 2026): POS-RT web procedure live 5 March 2026; POS owned at 1 Jan 2026 or activated in January must be linked within 45 days (by 20 April 2026); later activations/variations registered between the 6th day of the second month after activation and the last working day of that month; SoftPOS and virtual instruments in scope, bank transfers not
checked.note: Verified against the actual FAQ PDF text (downloaded and extracted from https://www.agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/documents/d/guest/faq_collegamento_pos_rt_2503, header 'FAQ - Collegamento POS-RT – aggiornate al 25 marzo 2026'). FAQ 3: 'La procedura web è disponibile a partire dal 5 marzo 2026'. FAQ 12: instruments owned at 1 Jan 2026 or acquired in January 2026 must be linked 'entro 45 giorni dalla data di messa a disposizione della procedura web'; variations/new POS after 31 Jan 2026 'tra il sesto giorno del secondo mese successivo... e l'ultimo giorno lavorativo dello stesso mese' (example: POS activated April 2026 → link 6-30 June 2026). FAQ 14: SoftPOS expressly counted as physical-type instruments; virtual instruments (online platforms) in scope, identified by financial-operator data. FAQ 11: 'il bonifico bancario non rientra tra gli strumenti di pagamento elettronico da collegare'. One precision: the FAQ itself states only '45 giorni' without naming 20 April; 45 days from 5 March is Sunday 19 April 2026, and the 20 April 2026 deadline is the press/practice reading (next working day), reported uniformly by independent secondary sources (fiscal-focus.it, mysolution.it, commercialistatelematico.com). The 20 April date is therefore an interpretation, not literal FAQ text, but is the consensus operative deadline.

checked.claim: Claim 14: fiskaly has officially started certification of SIGN IT (API-first cloud fiscalization for Italy), offers SIGN IT lite (compliant receipt issuance/transmission via the AdE web-procedure channel) today, and launched SUBMIT IT, an API product automating the POS-RT linkage filing for retailers, ISVs and PSPs, integrating with AdE web services available since March 2026
checked.note: Confirmed as an accurate description of fiskaly's announced products, with the caveat that evidence is necessarily fiskaly's own publications (these are first-party product claims; no independent registry confirmation exists). (a) Certification started: fiskaly blog of 14 May 2025 'fiskaly starts the certification process for its cloud-based fiscalization in Italy' states 'fiskaly has officially started the certification process for fiskaly SIGN IT' (https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/fiskaly-a-pioneer-in-the-certification-of-cloud-based-fiscalization-in-italy). Note: started, not completed — SIGN IT full is not yet an approved 'soluzione software' (consistent with the 2027 go-live per claim 5). (b) SIGN IT lite: https://www.fiskaly.com/signit describes it as 'already available', an API that 'connects your POS with the AdE's web portal and issues legally compliant commercial documents without the need of a telematic register', i.e. the AdE web-procedure channel. (c) SUBMIT IT: live product page https://www.fiskaly.com/submit-it markets it to POS/ERP software vendors (ISVs), acquirers/PSPs, and enterprise retailers, automating the POS-RT connection obligation of the 2025 Budget Law; fiskaly's blog (https://www.fiskaly.com/blog/the-2026-pos-rt-obligation-in-italy) states integration would be finalized 'as soon as the Italian Revenue Agency's web services become operational in March [2026]' — the AdE service did go live 5 March 2026 (see claim 8). Unverified residual: whether SUBMIT IT's AdE integration is fully operational today cannot be confirmed from any independent source; independent Italian press (aziendabanca.it, 1 July 2025) confirms fiskaly's market entry and cloud POS-RT/corrispettivi offering but does not name the products.

research.summary: By mid-2026, agent-facing DX has standardized around four layers: (1) a remote, OAuth-protected MCP server that exposes both docs search and live/sandbox API actions (Stripe mcp.stripe.com, Square mcp.squareup.com, PayPal, Plaid, Vercel; Twilio's is docs-only); (2) machine-readable docs — /llms.txt with embedded agent instructions, .md versions of every page, and llms-full.txt — now auto-generated for free by docs platforms like Mintlify and ReadMe, which is why even mid-size compliance vendors (Persona, Middesk, Alloy, Sardine, Unit21, Lithic) already ship them; (3) embedded docs AI assistants (kapa.ai, Inkeep) which are table stakes; and (4) agent-executable onboarding — Stripe's claimable sandboxes (`stripe sandbox create`, no account needed), Agent Skills (SKILL.md, the open standard adopted by ~40 clients since Dec 2025) published at /.well-known/skills, and agent toolkits for OpenAI/Vercel/LangChain SDKs. The classic gold standards remain load-bearing: interactive quickstarts with logged-in API keys (Stripe), 500+ entry error dictionaries with remediation (Twilio), monthly named API versions with codemod-style upgrade guides (Stripe 2026-05-27.dahlia), realistic sandboxes with magic test values and webhook simulation (Plaid/Stripe/Twilio), and public status pages. A major 2026 disruption: Anthropic acquired Stainless (~$300M, May 18, 2026) and is winding down the hosted SDK generator, making Speakeasy and Fern the default managed OpenAPI→SDK+MCP pipelines. For a 200-person compliance company, roughly 80% of the frontier is attainable cheaply (docs platform defaults, llms.txt with an instructions block, .md pages, docs-search MCP, Ask-AI widget, error catalog, sandbox webhooks simulation, Speakeasy/Fern SDK+MCP pipeline); the genuinely expensive hyperscaler moves are claimable anonymous sandboxes, an in-dashboard action-taking assistant, and a fully audited write-capable remote MCP.

key_facts.fact: Stripe runs a remote MCP server at https://mcp.stripe.com (OAuth per MCP spec, or restricted API key as bearer token) exposing ~25 tools spanning live API actions (create_customer, create_payment_link, create_refund, cancel_subscription...) plus search_stripe_documentation and search/fetch_stripe_resources; admins enable MCP access in the Dashboard separately for sandbox vs live, and OAuth sessions are revocable in user settings. A private 'agentic finance with Treasury' preview lets agents move money, pay bills and manage cards.

Stripe serves every docs page as Markdown by appending .md to the URL, hosts /llms.txt (which now opens with imperative agent instructions, e.g. 'never hardcode an old version number from training data'), offers claimable anonymous sandboxes via `stripe sandbox create` (no account; 7 days to claim) so coding agents can start integrating without a human signup, and publishes an Agent Skills catalog at https://docs.stripe.com/.well-known/skills/index.json installable via `npx skills add`, `claude plugin install stripe@claude-plugins-official`, or `codex plugin add stripe@openai-curated`.
key_facts.source: https://docs.stripe.com/building-with-ai (verified via .md fetch and curl of docs.stripe.com/llms.txt)

key_facts.fact: Cloudflare publishes a dedicated 'Docs for agents' page: /llms.txt (index grouped by product), /llms-full.txt (entire corpus in one file), per-product llms.txt variants, /index.md for any page, Accept: text/markdown content negotiation, token-count estimates per endpoint, an OpenAPI schemas repo, and 13+ managed remote MCP servers including a docs server, Observability, Workers Bindings, plus 'Code Mode' — a single MCP server covering 2,500+ API endpoints.

Plaid ships two official MCP surfaces: a remote Dashboard MCP server with production diagnostics tools (plaid_debug_item, plaid_get_link_analytics, plaid_get_usages, plaid_list_teams; OAuth client_credentials with mcp:dashboard scope, 15-min tokens) and a local AI coding toolkit MCP that generates realistic mock user data, simulates webhooks, creates Sandbox public tokens, and exposes docs search via 'Bill' (search_documentation).

Square's official MCP server is remote at https://mcp.squareup.com/sse (OAuth, granular permissions, recommended) or local with SANDBOX=true against the seller Sandbox; Square's /llms.txt is written explicitly for agents, including API-behavior guidance ('every write endpoint accepts an idempotency key; every list endpoint supports cursor-based pagination') and instructions to fetch llms-full.txt only when full ingestion is needed.

Twilio's hosted MCP server (https://mcp.twilio.com/docs, Public Beta) is deliberately read-only and docs-shaped: two tools (twilio__search, twilio__retrieve) implementing search-then-retrieve over 1,800+ endpoints generated from Twilio's public OpenAPI specs, no auth required; a self-hostable twilio-labs/mcp generator exposes the full API as MCP tools with --services/--tags filtering because the full tool set exceeds LLM context limits.

PayPal expanded its remote MCP server (hosted on Cloudflare) to include every tool in its Agent Toolkit — inventory, payments, shipping tracking, refunds, subscriptions — with 'restricted tool visibility' so LLMs only see tools permitted by the token; available both as local client-server and remote hosted modes.

Anthropic acquired Stainless — the SDK-generation platform behind OpenAI's, Google's, Perplexity's and Cloudflare's SDKs — for a reported $300M+ (announced May 18, 2026) and is winding down all hosted Stainless products including the SDK generator; new signups/projects/generation are closed and recommended migration paths are Speakeasy, Fern, APIMatic, or open-source OpenAPI Generator.

Speakeasy and Fern are now the leading managed OpenAPI→SDK pipelines; both also generate MCP servers from OpenAPI specs (Speakeasy: per-operation tools, x-speakeasy-mcp customization, Gram cloud hosting with OAuth; Stainless's approach had been two tools — code-execution + docs-search — argued to be more token-efficient than tool-per-endpoint).

Agent Skills (SKILL.md + .well-known/skills), open-sourced by Anthropic on Dec 18, 2025 at agentskills.io, became a cross-vendor standard within ~90 days — adopted by VS Code, OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, JetBrains Junie, Goose and ~40 clients total by mid-2026 — making vendor-published integration skills a new distribution channel alongside MCP.

llms.txt adoption is ~10% of websites overall but near-universal among developer-tool/API companies, and its practical consumers are IDE coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Windsurf) and MCP servers rather than search crawlers; verified live by HTTP probe on docs.stripe.com, developers.cloudflare.com, plaid.com, twilio.com, developer.squareup.com — and on mid-size fintech/compliance vendors docs.withpersona.com, docs.middesk.com, docs.alloy.com, docs.sardine.ai, docs.unit21.ai, docs.lithic.com, docs.moov.io, developer.intuit.com (all HTTP 200).
key_facts.source: https://presenc.ai/research/state-of-llms-txt-2026 plus direct curl probes (this research, June 12 2026)

key_facts.fact: Mid-size compliance vendors get the AI-docs layer nearly free from their docs platform: Middesk's and Persona's llms.txt (ReadMe-hosted) include an 'Instructions for AI Agents' block, .md-suffix Markdown for every page, per-section llms.txt indexes, and a hosted docs MCP server at docs.<domain>/_mcp/server; Mintlify likewise auto-generates /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, .md pages, and an MCP server with zero config on all tiers including free.

Docs AI assistants are table stakes: kapa.ai serves 200+ companies (OpenAI, Monday.com, Nokia, Sentry) answering 2M+ questions/month with citation-linked answers; Inkeep has moved up-stack to a full agent platform; Mintlify/ReadMe bundle 'Ask AI' into the docs platform itself. Stripe's own assistant goes further: it answers from docs/support via RAG and can execute actions in the Dashboard ('Create a product named Llama toy that costs 35 dollars').

Classic gold standards that still anchor DX: Stripe interactive quickstarts with embedded per-account test API keys, scroll-synced code highlighting, and 'launch with Stripe AI assistant in VS Code'; Twilio's Error and Warning Dictionary of 500+ numbered codes with possible causes and solutions; Stripe's monthly backward-compatible releases with named versions (current 2026-05-27.dahlia), a public changelog, and upgrade guides; Twilio docs have 'View as Markdown / Copy as Markdown' on every page.

Postman relaunched as 'AI-native' for the agentic era: an MCP request client, an MCP generator that builds servers from any of the 100,000 APIs in the Postman API Network (or your internal APIs via AI Tool Builder), and a Postman MCP server (npm + Docker, HTTP and stdio) for managing workspaces/collections/mocks from AI agents.

research.details:
## A) AI/AGENT-ERA DX — THE FRONTIER (state as of June 2026)

### A1. Official MCP servers from API companies

| Company | Endpoint / repo | Auth | What it exposes | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Stripe** | `https://mcp.stripe.com` (remote); local `npx @stripe/mcp` (github.com/stripe/ai/tree/main/tools/modelcontextprotocol) | OAuth (per MCP spec) or restricted API key (rk_*) as Bearer; Dashboard admin toggle, sandbox vs live managed separately; revocable OAuth sessions in user settings | ~25 tools: live API actions (create_customer, create_invoice, finalize_invoice, create_payment_link, create_price, create_product, create_refund, create_coupon, update_dispute, cancel/update_subscription, list_* for most resources, retrieve_balance, get_stripe_account_info) + `search_stripe_documentation` (docs+support KB) + `search_stripe_resources` / `fetch_stripe_resources` (account data). One-click installs for Cursor/VS Code/Claude Code/ChatGPT; works with OpenAI Responses API for autonomous agents. **Preview: 'Agentic finance with Treasury'** — agents move money, pay bills, create/manage cards (email-gated preview). | GA | 
| **Cloudflare** | `*.mcp.cloudflare.com` catalog (github.com/cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare) | OAuth per server | 13+ servers: Docs, Workers Bindings (create/read D1, R2, KV on the fly while coding), Workers Builds, Observability (query logs/errors across Workers), Radar, Containers, Browser Rendering, Logpush, AI Gateway, DNS analytics... plus **Code Mode**: one MCP server covering 2,500+ API endpoints via code execution | GA |
| **PayPal** | Remote MCP hosted on Cloudflare + local mode (`@paypal/agent-toolkit`) | Token-scoped; **restricted tool visibility** (LLM only sees tools the token permits) | Full agent-toolkit parity: invoices, payments, refunds (process refund, get refund details), subscriptions (update), inventory, shipment tracking, disputes | GA, tools still expanding (developer.paypal.com/tools/mcp-server/) |
| **Plaid** | Remote: Dashboard MCP server; Local: AI coding toolkit MCP (subprocess of Claude Desktop/Cursor/VS Code/Zed) | Remote: OAuth `client_credentials`, scope `mcp:dashboard`, 15-min tokens + refresh | Remote = production ops: `plaid_debug_item` (diagnose broken Items), `plaid_get_link_analytics` (Link conversion/error funnels), `plaid_get_usages`, `plaid_list_teams`. Local = dev: generate realistic custom user data, **simulate webhooks**, create Sandbox public tokens, `search_documentation` ('Bill', Plaid's docs AI platypus). OpenAI connector partnership announced (plaid.com/blog/openai-plaid-mcp/) | GA |
| **Square** | Remote `https://mcp.squareup.com/sse` (recommended) or local (github.com/square/square-mcp-server) with `SANDBOX=true` | OAuth with granular permissions | Full Square Connect API surface: payments, orders, catalog, inventory, customers, bookings...; sandbox mode flips to test environment without touching real money | GA (developer.squareup.com/docs/mcp) |
| **Twilio** | Hosted `https://mcp.twilio.com/docs`; self-host generator twilio-labs/mcp (`@twilio-alpha/mcp`) | Hosted: **none needed (read-only, public specs only)**; self-hosted: your credentials | Hosted = docs-shaped, 2 tools: `twilio__search` (NL query → ranked operations) and `twilio__retrieve` (full param/response schemas), over 1,800+ endpoints / 30+ products, generated from OpenAPI, `filter.version` support. Self-hosted exposes APIs as callable tools with `--services`/`--tags` filtering (full toolset exceeds context limits) | Public Beta (twilio.com/docs/ai/mcp) |
| **Vercel** | `https://mcp.vercel.com` | OAuth, read-only at launch | Docs search + projects, deployments, logs | Public Beta (vercel.com/blog/introducing-vercel-mcp-connect-vercel-to-your-ai-tools) |
| **Others (context)** | Cloudflare 'MCP Demo Day' (May 2025): Asana, Atlassian, Intercom, Linear, PayPal, Sentry, Block/Square, Stripe, Webflow all shipped remote MCPs on Cloudflare | | | blog.cloudflare.com/mcp-demo-day/ |

Ecosystem notes:
- OpenAI's ChatGPT connectors list includes Cloudflare, HubSpot, Intercom, PayPal, Plaid, Shopify, Stripe, Square, Twilio, Zapier (eco.com/support/en/articles/14845480-mcp-and-payments-a-2026-guide).
- MCP was donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation Dec 9, 2025 (Anthropic + Block + OpenAI co-founders) — it is now vendor-neutral infrastructure (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Model_Context_Protocol). Official registry: registry.modelcontextprotocol.io.
- Design split worth copying: **docs-only read-only MCP (Twilio, no auth, zero risk)** vs **action-taking MCP (Stripe/Square/PayPal: OAuth, granular permissions, admin enablement, sandbox/live separation, restricted tool visibility)**. Stripe explicitly warns about prompt injection and recommends human confirmation on tools (docs.stripe.com/mcp).
- Two MCP architectures: tool-per-endpoint (Speakeasy default, PayPal, Stripe) vs search+retrieve/code-execution (Twilio hosted, Stainless, Cloudflare Code Mode) — the latter is more token-efficient at large API surfaces (speakeasy.com/blog/comparison-mcp-server-generators).

### A2. llms.txt / .md docs / machine-readable docs

Verified live by direct HTTP probe (June 12, 2026), all 200 unless noted:
- docs.stripe.com/llms.txt; **no** llms-full.txt (404) — Stripe favors .md-per-page + MCP search instead
- developers.cloudflare.com/llms.txt **and** /llms-full.txt; per-product variants (/workers/llms.txt etc.)
- plaid.com/llms.txt and plaid.com/docs/llms.txt; twilio.com/llms.txt (covers marketing+docs, .md per page); developer.squareup.com/llms.txt (+ llms-full.txt)
- **Compliance/fintech mid-size vendors all 200**: docs.withpersona.com, docs.middesk.com, docs.alloy.com, docs.sardine.ai, docs.unit21.ai, docs.lithic.com, docs.moov.io, developer.intuit.com. 404/unresolved: docs.unit.co, docs.column.com, docs.increase.com.
- Middesk/Persona llms.txt (ReadMe platform) include an 'Instructions for AI Agents' header: append `.md` for clean Markdown, per-section `/llms.txt` indexes, and **a hosted docs MCP server at `docs.<domain>/_mcp/server`** — i.e., ReadMe gives every customer a docs MCP automatically. Sardine/llms-full.txt = Mintlify pattern.

Patterns that define the frontier:
- **Stripe**: append `.md` to any docs URL → plain Markdown (announced Mar 2025, x.com/StripeDev/status/1901662810897101256). Rationale: fewer formatting tokens; tab-hidden content rendered; Markdown hierarchy parses well (docs.stripe.com/building-with-ai).
- **Stripe llms.txt has an instructions section** — imperative guidance to agents (e.g., check npm/PyPI for latest SDK version, never pin versions from training data). Analysis: apideck.com/blog/stripe-llms-txt-instructions-section — llms.txt evolving from sitemap to *policy/guidance channel for agents*.
- **Square llms.txt** embeds API semantics for agents: 'every write endpoint accepts an idempotency key; every list endpoint supports cursor-based pagination', plus explicit guidance to fetch only relevant links vs llms-full.txt.
- **Cloudflare 'Docs for agents'** (developers.cloudflare.com/docs-for-agents/): llms.txt, llms-full.txt (for offline indexing/bulk vectorization/large-context models), per-product scoped files, `/index.md` per page, `Accept: text/markdown` content negotiation, 'Copy as Markdown' button, **token-count estimates per endpoint**, OpenAPI at cloudflare/api-schemas.
- **Twilio**: 'View as Markdown' and 'Copy as Markdown' on every docs page (apidog.com/blog/twilios-api-the-other-gold-standard-and-why-its-stripes-true-equal/).
- **Vercel**: vercel.com/llms.txt (docs path 308-redirects there); llms-full 200.
- Adoption reality check: ~10% of all sites have llms.txt, flat across traffic tiers; consumption is primarily **IDE agents and MCP servers fetching on demand**, not search-engine crawlers; treat it as developer-support infrastructure, not SEO (presenc.ai/research/state-of-llms-txt-2026, codersera.com/blog/llms-txt-complete-guide-2026/). For API/docs products the recommendation is llms.txt + llms-full.txt + .md pages (limy.ai/blog/llms.txt-in-2026-the-full-guide).
- Docs platforms automate all of this: **Mintlify** auto-generates /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, .md per page, and an MCP server, zero-config, all tiers incl. free (mintlify.com/blog/generate-mcp-servers-for-your-docs, mintlify.com/library/best-llms-txt-platforms). **ReadMe** supports llms.txt toggle, .md pages, hosted `_mcp/server`, 'Ask AI' (AI Booster Pack $150/mo), Owlbert doc linting; no llms-full.txt (readme.com/blog/readme-vs-mintlify). **Fern** similar (buildwithfern.com/post/best-llms-txt-implementation-platforms-ai-discoverable-apis).

### A3. Docs copilots / chat-with-docs — table stakes by 2026
- **kapa.ai**: purpose-built technical RAG; 200+ companies (OpenAI, Monday.com, Nokia, Sentry); 2M+ questions/month; every answer cites and deep-links to exact page/heading/code sample (kapa.ai, kapa.ai/blog/top-5-ai-documentation-chatbots-2026).
- **Inkeep**: moved up-stack from docs Q&A to agent platform (no-code builder + TypeScript SDK, multi-agent workflows for support/CX/GTM) (inkeep.com/blog/inkeep-vs-kapa).
- Docs-platform-native 'Ask AI' (Mintlify launched Oct 2025; ReadMe) is cheaper but less mature than dedicated tools.
- **Stripe Dashboard assistant** (docs.stripe.com/assistant): RAG over docs + support articles, *and executes Dashboard actions* on prompt ('Create a product named Llama toy that costs 35 dollars'). Stripe also shipped an AI assistant inside the VS Code extension (stripe.dev/blog/stripes-ai-assistant-vs-code).
- **Plaid 'Bill'**: docs chatbot persona, also exposed as the `search_documentation` MCP tool — the same assistant serves web docs and IDE agents (plaid.com/docs/resources/).
- What's table stakes by 2026: citation-linked answers; same retrieval backend exposed via web widget AND MCP; answer-quality analytics feeding docs-gap triage. Differentiators: action-taking assistants (Stripe), production-diagnostics tools (Plaid Dashboard MCP).

### A4. Agent-executable quickstarts / agent toolkits / skills
- **Stripe claimable sandboxes**: `stripe sandbox create` provisions an **anonymous account with sandbox keys, no signup**; claim within 7 days via `stripe sandbox claim`. Explicitly built so coding agents can start integrating before a human creates an account. AI app builders (Base44, Manus, Replit, v0) embed Stripe via claimable sandboxes; Stripe offers the same to other agent platforms (docs.stripe.com/building-with-ai, docs.stripe.com/sandboxes/claimable-sandboxes).
- **Agent Skills standard (SKILL.md)**: Anthropic open-sourced Dec 18, 2025 (agentskills.io); adopted by VS Code + OpenAI within 48h, 32 tools by Mar 2026, ~40 by June 2026 (Copilot, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, Goose, JetBrains Junie, AWS Kiro, Spring AI, Snowflake Cortex...) (venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-launches-enterprise-agent-skills-and-opens-the-standard, paperclipped.de/en/blog/agent-skills-open-standard-interoperability/, thenewstack.io/agent-skills-anthropics-next-bid-to-define-ai-standards/).
- **Stripe skills distribution**: hosted at docs.stripe.com/.well-known/skills/index.json; install via `npx skills add https://docs.stripe.com`, `claude plugin install stripe@claude-plugins-official`, `codex plugin add stripe@openai-curated`, or Cursor marketplace plugin. Skills = vendor-curated integration best practices the agent follows while coding (docs.stripe.com/building-with-ai).
- **Stripe agent toolkit** (`@stripe/agent-toolkit`, `stripe_agent_toolkit`): native adapters for OpenAI Agents SDK, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, CrewAI; Python+TS; restricted keys recommended; also supports usage-based billing for agents and monetizing MCPs (docs.stripe.com/agents, docs.stripe.com/agents/quickstart).
- **PayPal agent toolkit**: same pattern (pulsemcp.com/servers/paypal-agent-toolkit). **Plaid AI coding toolkit**: local MCP + mock data + webhook simulation (plaid.com/blog/ai-development-toolkit/).
- Quickstart-as-prompt pattern: 'paste this prompt into Cursor/Claude and it integrates the API end-to-end', often paired with CLI auto-provisioning of keys into .env (agentready.cloud as a generic example; Stripe quickstarts launch directly into VS Code with the AI assistant).
- Stripe also exposes machine-readable agentic-commerce rails (Machine Payments Protocol, stripe.com/blog/machine-payments-protocol; vs Coinbase x402 — workos.com/blog/x402-vs-stripe-mpp-...): relevant context, not core DX.

## B) CLASSIC GOLD STANDARDS (still load-bearing in 2026)

### B1. Interactive playgrounds & personalized quickstarts
- **Stripe quickstarts** (docs.stripe.com/quickstarts): full end-to-end interactive samples; language/framework switcher; scroll-synced line highlighting; **your own test API keys embedded in code samples when logged in**; option to launch the quickstart with Stripe's AI assistant in VS Code.
- Stripe docs render API reference with copyable per-language snippets and a 'developer satisfaction ~99%, converts developers 3x industry average' reputation (dev.to/yukioikeda/why-stripes-api-is-the-gold-standard...; treat the stats as marketing-grade).
- Stripe Projects CLI for provisioning stacks; Stripe CLI `stripe listen`/`trigger` for local webhook testing (docs.stripe.com/stripe-cli).

### B2. Sandbox / test-mode design
- **Stripe**: sandboxes are full isolated environments (docs.stripe.com/sandboxes), magic test cards/values (docs.stripe.com/testing), test clocks for time-dependent billing, claimable sandboxes (above).
- **Plaid**: Sandbox with deterministic test credentials (user_good/pass_good) and `/sandbox/*` endpoints to force item states and **fire webhooks on demand** (plaid.com/docs/sandbox/); now ALSO wrapped as MCP tools (mock data, webhook simulation).
- **Twilio**: test credentials + magic phone numbers that deterministically trigger specific error codes (twilio.com/docs/iam/test-credentials).
- **Square**: separate Sandbox sellers, sandbox switch on the MCP server (developer.squareup.com/docs/devtools/sandbox/overview).
- 2026 evolution: sandbox actions exposed through MCP so agents can self-test integrations (Plaid local toolkit; Square SANDBOX=true; Stripe sandbox-vs-live MCP enablement).

### B3. Error catalogs with remediation
- **Twilio Error & Warning Dictionary** (twilio.com/docs/api/errors): 500+ numbered codes in ranges (10000s account/auth, 20000s messaging, 30000s voice, 40000s numbers, 50000s SIP, 60000s video), each with possible causes AND solutions; machine-handled programmatically; debugger surfaces them per request (help.twilio.com/articles/14967123536795).
- **Stripe**: typed error taxonomy (decline codes with docs.stripe.com/declines guidance), error objects link to docs. Pattern to copy for compliance APIs: every error code gets a stable ID, a cause, a remediation, and a deep link — and now should also exist as .md so agents can self-remediate.

### B4. Changelog / versioning UX
- **Stripe** (docs.stripe.com/changelog, /upgrades, /api/versioning): monthly releases, **backward-compatible within a named major version**; versions named date+codename (current **2026-05-27.dahlia**); account-pinned API versions; upgrade guides per release. 
- **Twilio changelog** (twilio.com/en-us/changelog) filterable by product.
- Frontier twist: changelogs and upgrade guides as .md/llms.txt entries so coding agents perform upgrades; Stripe's llms.txt explicitly links 'API upgrades'.

### B5. OpenAPI → SDK pipelines (major 2026 shakeup)
- **Anthropic acquired Stainless** (announced May 18, 2026; reported $300M+). All hosted Stainless products including the SDK generator are winding down; new signups/projects/generation closed immediately; existing customers keep generated SDKs but lose the auto-regeneration pipeline. Affected: OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Perplexity, Cloudflare SDKs (thenewstack.io/anthropic-stainless-sdk-acquisition/, workos.com/blog/stainless-alternatives, letsdatascience.com/news/anthropic-acquires-stainless-winds-down-public-sdk-generator-65931918). NOTE: corroborated across multiple outlets but not verified on stainless.com directly in this research.
- **Alternatives** (workos.com/blog/stainless-alternatives, speakeasy.com/blog/comparison-sdk-generators-openapi): **Speakeasy** — OpenAPI-native, 10 languages (TS, Python, Go, Java, C#, PHP, Ruby, Kotlin, Unity, Terraform), most direct managed replacement; also generates MCP servers (tool-per-operation, x-speakeasy-mcp ext, Gram cloud hosting w/ OAuth). **Fern** — custom DSL w/ OpenAPI import, TypeScript-first, clean output, also a docs platform. **APIMatic**, **OpenAPI Generator** (OSS, 'functional but rarely idiomatic'), **oagen** (WorkOS's OSS IR-based framework).
- Implication: SDK pipeline and MCP generation are converging into one vendor decision; Speakeasy/Fern both market 'API → SDK + docs + MCP' as a bundle.

### B6. Postman / collections
- Postman relaunched 'AI-native... for the agentic era' (blog.postman.com/new-postman-is-here/): MCP requests live alongside HTTP/GraphQL/gRPC in collections; **MCP Generator** builds servers from the 100,000-API Postman API Network or internal APIs (postman.com/explore/agent-generator, learning.postman.com/docs/postman-ai-agent-builder/ai-tool-builder/overview/); Postman's own MCP server (npm + Docker, HTTP/stdio) manages workspaces/collections/mocks/monitors from AI agents; AI Agent block in Flows. Publishing a verified Postman workspace/collection remains a cheap discovery channel.

### B7. Status / uptime transparency
- status.stripe.com, status.plaid.com, status.twilio.com all live (verified 200). Plaid additionally exposes per-institution health in the Dashboard, and its remote MCP exposes usage/Link-conversion analytics — a step beyond static status pages: *programmatic, agent-queryable operational telemetry*.

### B8. Integration certification programs
- **Avalara Certified Implementer / certified integrations** (avalara.com/us/en/partners/partner-programs/avalara-implementation-certification.html, developer.avalara.com/certification): the canonical compliance-API example — ISVs/SIs build against a test plan, get certified badge + marketplace listing; drives partner-led distribution.
- Twilio ISV/partner programs and Plaid partner ecosystem operate similarly; Stripe runs app review for its Apps Marketplace and a Verified Partner program. For a compliance API, an Avalara-style 'certified integration' program (test plan + badge + co-marketing) is the proven template.

## VERDICT: WHAT'S REALISTIC FOR A ~200-PERSON COMPLIANCE API COMPANY

**Tier 1 — effectively free / weeks (docs-platform defaults + content discipline):**
- llms.txt + llms-full.txt + .md per page + per-section indexes + hosted docs MCP: auto-generated by Mintlify (all features incl. MCP on free tier) or ReadMe (`_mcp/server`). Persona/Middesk/Alloy/Sardine/Unit21 already have this — it is now *baseline parity* in compliance fintech, not differentiation.
- Add a Stripe-style 'Instructions for AI Agents' block to llms.txt (version pinning, auth quirks, idempotency, pagination semantics — Square's is the model).
- 'Ask AI' widget: kapa.ai or docs-platform native; require citation deep-links; mine unanswered questions for docs gaps.
- Error catalog: stable numbered codes, cause + remediation per code, each at a .md URL (Twilio model).
- Changelog + pinned API versioning with upgrade guides in Markdown.
- Postman collection + published OpenAPI spec.

**Tier 2 — one small team / a quarter:**
- OpenAPI → SDKs + MCP via Speakeasy or Fern (Stainless no longer an option). Curate MCP tools rather than tool-per-endpoint; for big surfaces use search+retrieve (Twilio) or code-mode patterns.
- Read-only hosted docs/search MCP (no auth, zero risk — Twilio model) as the public default; this is the highest-leverage, lowest-risk MCP for a compliance company.
- Sandbox webhook simulation + deterministic magic test values (Plaid model), then wrap sandbox actions as local MCP tools so agents can self-test.
- Publish Agent Skills (SKILL.md at /.well-known/skills) encoding compliance-integration best practices — cheap (Markdown), cross-client (~40 clients read it), and uniquely valuable in a regulated domain where 'correct by default' matters.
- Quickstart-as-prompt: a copy-paste agent prompt + CLI that drops sandbox keys into .env.
- Avalara-style certified-integration program if partner-led distribution matters.

**Tier 3 — hyperscaler territory (deliberate, scoped bets only):**
- Write-capable remote MCP with OAuth, granular scopes, restricted tool visibility, admin enablement, session revocation (Stripe/Square/PayPal). For a compliance API, scope writes to *sandbox-only* first; production writes raise prompt-injection and audit questions Stripe itself flags.
- Claimable anonymous sandboxes (`vendor sandbox create` with no signup) — high engineering + abuse-prevention cost, but the single strongest agent-acquisition move on the board.
- In-dashboard action-taking assistant (Stripe Assistant class) and production-diagnostics MCP (Plaid `plaid_debug_item` class — note: this one is unusually well-suited to compliance APIs, where 'why did this verification fail' is the #1 support question, and may be worth pulling forward into Tier 2).

checked.note: REFUTED on the platform attribution; the features themselves are real. CORRECT FACT: Middesk's and Persona's docs are hosted on FERN (buildwithfern.com), not ReadMe. Evidence: (1) HTML fingerprint — curl of https://docs.middesk.com/ contains 1,717 occurrences of 'fern' and https://docs.withpersona.com/ contains 1,883; zero ReadMe markers. (2) The /_mcp/server path is Fern's documented convention: Fern's own docs (https://buildwithfern.com/learn/docs/ai-features/mcp-server) state Fern auto-generates and hosts an MCP server at your-documentation-site.com/_mcp/server for sites with Ask Fern enabled, plus /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, per-URL /llms.txt indexes, and .md for any page — exactly the feature set observed. (3) Feature claims verified live (June 12, 2026): both https://docs.middesk.com/llms.txt and https://docs.withpersona.com/llms.txt return HTTP 200 and open with an 'Instructions for AI Agents' block ('append `.md` to the page URL', 'append `/llms.txt` to any section URL', 'connect to the MCP server at https://docs.<domain>/_mcp/server'); both /_mcp/server endpoints return HTTP 200. (4) Mintlify portion: zero-config auto-generated search MCP server at /mcp confirmed (https://www.mintlify.com/docs/ai/model-context-protocol — 'Mintlify generates a search MCP server for your site and hosts it at the /mcp path'), with /.well-known/mcp discovery; but 'on all tiers including free' is not explicitly stated in Mintlify's docs (no tier restriction mentioned — unverified rather than confirmed). Additional nuance for the 'nearly free' framing: Fern's hosted MCP requires Ask Fern to be enabled, which may not be included on every plan. The conclusion 'mid-size compliance vendors get the AI-docs layer from their docs platform' stands, but the platform is Fern, and any downstream statement crediting ReadMe with the _mcp/server / Instructions-for-AI-Agents pattern is wrong.

research.summary: fiskaly's GitHub org (30 public repos) is effectively dormant as an integrator resource: all five historic Cloud-TSE SDKs (dotnet, java, php, swift, node) are archived (four on 2022-12-07, node on 2024-09-11), the Go SDK repo has been deleted outright, and every SDK README still targets the obsolete SIGN DE v1 kassensichv.io API. No official SDK exists for SIGN ES, SIGN IT, SIGN FR, E-INVOICE, or the 2026-02-03 date-versioned APIs — fiskaly's official position (SIGN DE v1-to-v2 migration guide) is that "there is no longer any need to integrate an SDK," recommending a plain HTTP client. The only recent public org activity is internal tooling (docker.* CI wrapper images, a Grafana SurrealDB plugin, hiring coding-challenges) plus telling forks of Invopop's GOBL and GOBL-FatturaPA libraries (pushed June 2026), which signal fiskaly builds its SIGN IT backend in Go on GOBL — none of it integrator-facing. OpenAPI 3.0.3 specs ARE openly published, but only on the docs sites, not GitHub/npm: the new workspace.fiskaly.com portal serves downloadable specs (fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml, sign-fr, sign-pt.2026-05-04, e-invoice-de/be) via a "Download OpenAPI specification" button, and developer.fiskaly.com exposes the templated unified OAS plus per-country overlays. Postman support is official-but-gated for SIGN DE V2 (personalized environment generated from the Quick Start page) and referenced for SIGN IT; the only public Postman workspace found is community-made. Open issues on the archived repos document years of unanswered requests ("How to connect to V2?", "Please update the doc to reflect that this sdk is deprecated or not", M1 Mac install failure) — all frozen when repos were archived. Net assessment for a new SIGN IT integrator: the downloadable OpenAPI spec (8 resources: tokens, subjects, organizations, taxpayers, locations, systems, records, files) is the de-facto SDK; everything else — client generation, auth/token refresh, 60-day credential rotation, and migration across four API versions in ~17 months (2024-09-20 → 2024-10-31 → 2025-08-12 → 2026-02-03) — is left to the integrator.

**Signal:** the GOBL/FatturaPA forks (pushed the day before this research) plus the oapi-codegen/sta Docker tooling show fiskaly's SIGN IT backend is Go-based on Invopop's GOBL with internally generated OpenAPI clients — none of which is offered to integrators.

## 2. Are official SDKs current? NO
- No SDK exists for SIGN ES, SIGN IT, SIGN FR, SIGN PT, E-INVOICE, SUBMIT DE, or any 2026-02-03 / 2026-05-04 API version (checked GitHub org, npm, GitHub-wide repo search).
- Official policy (https://developer.fiskaly.com/sign-de/migration): "There is no longer any need to integrate an SDK, since V2 now uses a new API together with a web-based management dashboard, and can be addressed conveniently with a HTTP client of your choice." (V2 dropped the local SMAERS component that v1 SDKs wrapped.)
- Doc rot: the FAQ (https://developer.fiskaly.com/en/faq) still says "A list of our client implementations is available as open source at github.com/fiskaly" — all archived.
- Only recent official package: **@fiskaly/ui-mcp** 3.1.0 (npm, 2026-02-14, maintainers patrick.gaubatz/johannesferner @fiskaly.com) — an MCP server for fiskaly's *design system* (@fiskaly/ui), repo github.com/fiskaly/fiskaly-style is **private**. Aimed at AI code editors building fiskaly's own UI, not at fiscalization integrators.
- Community fills gaps: fiskaly-chat-mcp 0.1.0 (npm 2026-05-25, third-party donovanhutchinson — MCP server querying fiskaly's documentation chat API; implies the docs site exposes an AI chat endpoint); EstebanVini/fiskaly_sdk (unofficial Python, 2025); treatwell/uala-fiskaly-ruby (Ruby); advancedmediatechnology/laravel-ereceipt (PHP, fiskaly eReceipt Laravel integration, 2025); tualo/fiskaly, knedlicc/fiskaly, GuilhermeVozniak/fiskaly, drechsler-development/fiskaly (small personal wrappers, Go/PHP/JS, 2025–2026). Source: https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q=fiskaly

**New portal workspace.fiskaly.com** ("We're upgrading! ... Preview" banner on developer.fiskaly.com) — has an explicit "Download OpenAPI specification" button; specs at /specs/ (all verified HTTP 200, OpenAPI 3.0.3, servers live.api.fiskaly.com / test.api.fiskaly.com):

- NOT found there: fiskaly.sign-es.*, fiskaly.kassensichv.*, sign-se (404s — older products still only on the legacy portal)

**Legacy portal developer.fiskaly.com** (Docusaurus + redocusaurus): main JS bundle (assets/js/main.2c5ea9ba.js) maps unified products to a shared templated OAS + country overlays:
- Unified OAS 2026-02-03: https://live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml (216,705 B; `info.version: '2026-02-03'`; placeholder syntax `((<oas_info_title))` resolved by overlay)
