**API doc routes found in the bundle** (developer.fiskaly.com/api/...): kassensichv v0/v1/v2; sign-de/submission/v1; dsfinvk v0/v1; sign-es v0.localhost/v1; sign-it 2024-10-31, 2025-08-12, 2026-02-03 (+/local); sign-fr 2025-08-12, 2026-02-03; e-invoice-de 2026-02-03; e-invoice-be 2026-02-03; ereceipt v0/v1; management; safe; sign-pt and sign-se route to workspace.fiskaly.com (sign-pt at 2026-05-04). The /api index still labels SIGN IT "X-Api-Version: 2024-09-20" — i.e., 4 SIGN IT versions in ~17 months.

## 4. Sample apps / demos / Postman
- SIGN DE V2: official Postman guide pages — https://developer.fiskaly.com/sign-de/postman, /sign-de/setting-up, /sign-de/v2-environment, /sign-de/run-demo ("Running the demo"). The Postman *environment* is personalized: "go to the API V2 Quick Start page and enter your API key and secret in order to obtain your personalized Postman environment" — no public .json collection files linked.
- SIGN IT: integration guide (https://developer.fiskaly.com/sign-it/2026-02-03/integration_guide) references a Postman collection via the API Quick Start section plus a video tutorial; the flow is an 11-step manual process (dashboard org → API key → token → UNIT org → subject API key → taxpayer → location → system → two-step records INTENTION→TRANSACTION); credentials must be updated every 60 days.
- Public Postman network: only community workspace "enrolment / fiskaly-v2" (https://www.postman.com/enrolment/fiskaly-v2/documentation/4qtf7a1/sign-api-v2) — not an official fiskaly workspace.
- No official sample apps / demo repos on GitHub for SIGN ES/IT/FR/E-INVOICE. Support video: https://support.fiskaly.com/hc/en-001/articles/13028862505116 (creating api_key/secret + Postman).
- workspace.fiskaly.com includes an AI docs assistant ("dedicated AI assistant for documentation queries"); third-party fiskaly-chat-mcp wraps the same chat API for MCP clients.

## 5. Community signals (issues frozen by archiving — fiskaly never responded in-thread)
- fiskaly-sdk-dotnet#38 (2021-08-25, 0 comments): "How to connect to V2? ... When will be ready dot-net to Kassensich v2?" — https://github.com/fiskaly/fiskaly-sdk-dotnet/issues/38
- fiskaly-sdk-node#14 (2021-12-22, 0 comments): "This is an sdk for the version 1 api, isn't it? Please update the doc to reflect that this sdk is deprecated or not." — https://github.com/fiskaly/fiskaly-sdk-node/issues/14
- fiskaly-sdk-node#15 (2022-01-17): "The SDK can't be installed on a M1 Mac unfortunately... With ffi-napi 4.0.3 it's working. Would it be possible to update that subdependeny?" — https://github.com/fiskaly/fiskaly-sdk-node/issues/15
- Other pain points left open: java#32 "Latest release is not available in maven"; java#29 React Native hang in release mode; dotnet#23 missing strong-name; swift#33 SPM support; php#13–15 production error triage (503 "failed to fetch remote JWK", 500 "SlowDown"). ~32 open issues total across the five archived SDK repos.

## 6. SDK/tooling gap assessment for a new SIGN IT integrator
**What exists:** a downloadable, fully-resolved OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec (workspace.fiskaly.com/specs/fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml; 8 resources: /tokens /subjects /organizations /taxpayers /locations /systems /records /files; live/test hosts live.api.fiskaly.com / test.api.fiskaly.com), versioned docs with integration guide + glossary + connection-loss/receipt-lottery/compliance pages, a Postman collection via Quick Start, and a docs AI chat.
**What's missing:** (1) zero official client libraries in any language — auth token issuance/refresh, retries, idempotency, and offline/connection-loss queueing (which the old SDKs handled and which the SIGN IT docs devote a page to) must be hand-built; (2) version churn risk — four SIGN IT date versions since launch (2024-09-20/2024-10-31/2025-08-12/2026-02-03) with no migration tooling or changelog automation; (3) no official sample app or reference implementation for SIGN IT (GitHub assets stop at SIGN DE v1, 2020–2022); (4) operational chores codified in docs, not code — e.g., mandatory credential rotation every 60 days, TEST vs LIVE resource separation, two-step record creation (INTENTION→TRANSACTION); (5) no official public Postman workspace and no specs on GitHub/npm, so spec-diffing across versions requires scraping the docs portal; (6) GitHub is a dead-end support channel (archived repos, no org README) — the funnel is the Zendesk support portal and dashboard. **Practical path:** generate a client from the published YAML (fiskaly itself uses oapi-codegen/sta per its docker.* repos), pin X-Api-Version, wrap token refresh + retry + offline buffering yourself, and watch developer.fiskaly.com/blog (Dev News changelogs) for version announcements.

Is the private fiskaly/fiskaly-style monorepo a sign of further npm packages (@fiskaly scope) that could become public integrator tooling?
What exactly does the SIGN IT Postman 'API Quick Start' provide (full collection vs. only a personalized environment) — requires a dashboard account to verify?

opportunities.title: fiskaly Sandbox MCP Server — an action-taking MCP that lets any AI agent complete a full SIGN IT integration
opportunities.problem: fiskaly's agent story stops at reading. The documented 9-tool @fiskaly/docs-mcp is not published on npm (registry returns 'Not found'), the ApiTryIt component ships in the workspace JS bundle but never renders, /api/console is a 404, there are zero SDKs for any current product (all GitHub SDK repos archived, fiskaly-sdk-go gone), and the integration guide is an 11-step manual flow with 90-day Fisconline credential rotation, mandatory X-Idempotency-Key UUIDs, and a two-call INTENTION→TRANSACTION record pattern. Meanwhile the 2026 benchmark (Stripe mcp.stripe.com, Square mcp.squareup.com, Plaid, PayPal) is action-taking MCP servers that execute against the sandbox — and POS vendors' developers increasingly ARE coding agents in Cursor/Claude Code. Today an agent integrating SIGN IT must hand-roll auth, token refresh, idempotency, versioning headers, and state polling from a spec with 168 blank descriptions and zero examples.
opportunities.solution: A remote+local MCP server in Go (matching fiskaly's stack) bound to test.api.fiskaly.com, with three tool layers. (1) Docs layer, compounding workspace.fiskaly.com's existing llms-full.txt/specs: search_docs, get_endpoint, explain_error_code. (2) Action layer that absorbs all protocol friction (auto JWT mint/refresh from API key+secret, auto X-Api-Version and UUIDv4 X-Idempotency-Key, state polling): provision_merchant (createOrganization UNIT → createSubject → createTaxpayer with Fisconline credentials → createLocation → createSystem → PATCH to COMMISSIONED in one tool), issue_receipt (records INTENTION → TRANSACTION RECEIPT → poll ACCEPTED→COMPLETED, return the AdE progressive number from compliance.data), correct_receipt, cancel_receipt, get_record, check_credential_expiry. (3) Judge layer: audit_session replays everything the agent did against machine-readable compliance rules (INTENTION before TRANSACTION, idempotency key uniqueness, credential age vs the 60/90-day rotation rule, 12-day transmission window) and emits a go-live readiness report. Resources: the OpenAPI spec, state-machine docs, compliance rules YAML. Prompts: 'integrate-my-pos' guided flow. TEST-only by design (LIVE is legally transmitted to AdE — the safety boundary is itself an interview talking point).
opportunities.value: This is 'make receipts easy' translated for the agent era: time-to-first-compliant-receipt drops from days of reading docs and Postman setup to minutes inside the tool POS developers already use. It directly serves fiskaly's actual customers (POS ISVs racing the Italian 2026-27 window where ~80% of 1.7M RTs hit end-of-life) and turns fiskaly into the first European fiscalization provider with a Stripe-grade agent surface — a moat efsta/fiskaltrust don't have. It also rescues the credibility gap of the documented-but-unpublished docs MCP.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-4 days for one engineer, fully functional. Stack: Go + the official MCP Go SDK (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk — verified current: mcp.NewServer, generic mcp.AddTool with auto JSON-schema from struct tags, AddPrompt/AddResource, StdioTransport for Claude Code plus StreamableServerTransport for remote); request/response types generated from the publicly downloadable workspace.fiskaly.com/specs/fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml using oapi-codegen — the same tool fiskaly wraps internally (docker.oapi-codegen repo), a deliberate nod. Day 1: codegen + auth/idempotency/version middleware + raw CRUD tools against a free TEST account (all fiskaly accounts start in TEST with full functionality). Day 2: workflow tools (provision_merchant, issue_receipt with polling). Day 3: judge layer + compliance rules YAML + prompts/resources. Day 4: polish, demo script, README. Hedge: if TEST onboarding stalls, a 200-line local simulator implementing the documented state machine keeps the demo live; the server takes a --base-url flag so both work. Demo flow: `claude mcp add fiskaly-sandbox`, then in Claude Code: 'Onboard Trattoria da Mario in Milan and issue a 42 euro receipt' — the agent provisions the full hierarchy, issues INTENTION+TRANSACTION, polls to COMPLETED, shows the AdE progressive number; then 'audit my integration' produces the judge report; then deliberately reuse an idempotency key and watch the judge flag it.
opportunities.wow_factor: It speaks the interviewer's exact language: the audit_session tool IS a 'judge agent auditing a coder agent so fiscal signatures stay compliant' — the job description's core idea, shipped as a working artifact. It demonstrates MCP server design (tool granularity: workflow tools vs tool-per-endpoint vs Twilio-style search+retrieve token economics), Go, spec-driven codegen so the server can never drift from the API, and a live agentic demo in Claude Code rather than slides. And it visibly goes beyond workspace.fiskaly.com: their MCP is docs-only and unpublished; this one executes.

opportunities.title: Compliance-as-Code: machine-readable fiscal rules + a Compliance Judge that audits any integration
opportunities.problem: Every rule that keeps an Italian merchant legal exists only as prose: 12-day transmission window, 90-day Fisconline credential expiry (renew at 60), paper-receipt + e-invoice fallback during AdE outages, INTENTION-before-TRANSACTION ordering, POS-RT linkage windows (45 days from 5 March 2026, then rolling monthly), with sanctions of 70% of VAT (min €300), €100 per late transmission, €1,000-4,000 for missing POS-RT linkage. Integrators must re-derive these invariants from docs that have 168 blank descriptions; nothing machine-checks whether their integration actually honors them. workspace.fiskaly.com's regulatory.json is marketing-level country metadata and human-interventions.json only flags which steps are automatable — neither is executable.
opportunities.solution: A versioned compliance-rules format (YAML, CalVer-pinned to the API version) encoding each invariant as a checkable assertion with regulation citation and sanction, plus a Compliance Judge: a Go service combining deterministic checkers (parse an integrator's captured API traffic log or test-suite output: ordering, idempotency, header presence, timing windows, state-machine legality) with an LLM reviewer (reads the integrator's source for risky patterns like hardcoded credentials, no outage fallback path, blocking checkout on signing). Output: a findings report mapping each violation to the rule, the law, and the fine. Delivery surfaces: MCP tool, CLI, and a 'fiskaly-audit' GitHub Action so it runs in the integrator's CI on every change.
opportunities.value: Shifts fiskaly from 'API provider' to 'compliance guarantor' — the thing customers actually pay for. POS vendors get pre-certification confidence before Go LIVE (where every record legally reaches AdE), support load drops, and the rules file becomes a durable asset: when AdE specs iterate (v1.0→v1.3 in 14 months), fiskaly ships a rules diff instead of a prose changelog. It is also the substrate fiskaly's own internal Judge agents need to audit their Coder agents — one artifact serving customers and the AI-First initiative simultaneously.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3-4 days. Day 1: author rules YAML for ~12 Italian invariants from the documented record lifecycle, idempotency spec, credential rules, and AdE FAQ. Day 2: Go CLI with deterministic checkers over a JSONL request/response log (easy to capture from the sandbox or synthesize). Day 3: Claude API pass for source-code review with rule-grounded prompts; build one deliberately broken sample integration (reused idempotency key, TRANSACTION without INTENTION, 95-day-old credentials) as the demo victim. Day 4: GitHub Action wrapper + report rendering. Demo: run the audit on the broken integration, watch it produce a findings table with euro-denominated sanction exposure, fix one finding live, re-run to green.
opportunities.wow_factor: Verbatim alignment with the role: 'judge agents that audit coder agents so fiscal signatures stay tamper-proof and legally compliant.' It shows the candidate thinks in invariants, eval harnesses, and hybrid deterministic+LLM verification — the architecture skill the 90% of this job is made of — and it proposes something no benchmark company (Stripe included) ships: executable regulatory rules.

opportunities.title: Agent-run Docs CI: every quickstart executes nightly, a fixer agent opens the PRs
opportunities.problem: The SIGN IT docs are provably unverified by machines: 168 unresolved template placeholders silently render as blank descriptions (IT_en.yaml doesn't define them), '401.xf' and 'stabability' typos, E-Invoice links pasted into the SIGN IT intro, a leftover /api/sign-it/local dev page, /downloads 404, a FAQ pointing to an all-archived GitHub org, workspace's /api/console linked but 404, Belgium labeled 'Live' on one page and 'Beta' on another, sign-pt missing from products.json. None of this survives one night of automated checking — it persists because docs correctness is a human chore.
opportunities.solution: A multi-agent docs pipeline that treats documentation as code under test. Stage 1 (deterministic): linter that merges the unified OAS template with each country overlay and fails on unresolved ((<key)) placeholders, dead links, and cross-page status contradictions. Stage 2 (executor agent): runs every quickstart and code sample end-to-end against test.api.fiskaly.com nightly — 'the quickstart is a test'; a quickstart that doesn't produce a COMPLETED record fails the build. Stage 3 (fixer Coder agent): drafts the patch — adds the missing overlay key, fixes the link, regenerates the sample. Stage 4 (Judge agent): reviews the patch against style and factual-grounding rules before a human merges. Runs as a GitHub Action against the publicly fetchable spec + overlay URLs.
opportunities.value: Docs are fiskaly's product surface — for a compliance API, a blank description is a compliance risk a customer inherits. This makes correctness continuous instead of episodic, scales to all 5 locales and 14+ products without headcount, and compounds the workspace platform (its content becomes the tested corpus). For agent consumers it matters double: coding agents trust and replicate whatever the docs say, including the errors.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days, demoable on real public data with zero internal access. Day 1: Go/TypeScript linter that fetches live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml + the IT_en.yaml overlay, performs the same substitution the docs site does, and reports unresolved keys — it will rediscover the 168 placeholders live on stage, plus the dead links. Day 2: executor agent (Claude API with bash/HTTP tools) that runs the SIGN IT quickstart flow against the sandbox and emits pass/fail. Day 3: fixer agent generating a real patch (a complete IT_en.yaml with the missing keys drafted from spec context) packaged as a PR, with a judge prompt gating it. Demo: trigger the Action, show the findings dashboard, show the auto-generated PR.
opportunities.wow_factor: It is literally the job — multi-agent SDLC automation with coder/judge separation and human-in-the-loop merge — applied to the exact artifact the interview question asks about. The live moment where the linter pulls fiskaly's real spec and surfaces 168 invisible defects in 10 seconds is a stronger critique than any slide, and the pipeline is the constructive answer to it.

opportunities.title: Verified examples factory: sandbox-executed x-codeSamples injected into the spec
opportunities.problem: The SIGN IT 2026-02-03 spec contains zero operation-level examples and zero x-codeSamples; fiskaly's official position since the v2 migration is 'no SDK needed, use an HTTP client' — yet ships nothing to paste. Workspace quickstart guides have hand-authored 5-language tabs but its API operation pages render only Redoc's auto-generated JSON. Hand-authored samples rot (four SIGN IT versions in 17 months); the industry default pipeline shifted after Anthropic acquired Stainless (May 2026), making spec-driven generation via Speakeasy/Fern the norm. Both humans and LLMs learn the API from examples — today they learn from nothing.
opportunities.solution: An agent pipeline: for each of the 27 operations, a generator agent produces samples in Go/TypeScript/Python/PHP/cURL from the OpenAPI spec plus the documented flow semantics (INTENTION-first, idempotency headers); an executor agent runs every sample against test.api.fiskaly.com; a judge verifies the HTTP result AND the semantic outcome (record reached COMPLETED, taxpayer reached COMMISSIONED) before signing it; signed samples are injected as x-codeSamples into the YAML so both Redoc portals (old and new) render them with no platform work — and llms-full.txt inherits them for free. Re-runs on every spec change, so a new API version ships with day-one verified examples. The same verified corpus is the natural input to a Speakeasy/Fern SDK+MCP pipeline later.
opportunities.value: Copy-paste-that-works is still the highest-ROI docs feature for time-to-integration, and verified examples are equally training data for the coding agents writing integrations — wrong or absent examples become wrong integrations of a fiscal API. 'Every sample executed against the sandbox before publication, every release' is a trust claim no fiscalization competitor makes.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days. Scope to the 8-call golden path (token → organization → subject → taxpayer → location → system → record INTENTION → record TRANSACTION) in 3 languages = 24 samples. Day 1: spec parsing + generator agent (Claude API, spec context + flow rules). Day 2: executor harness running samples in Docker against the sandbox, judge checks status codes and resulting resource states, store execution receipts (trace IDs, timestamps). Day 3: x-codeSamples injection script + before/after Redoc rendering side by side. Demo: edit a field name in the spec, re-run, watch the affected samples regenerate, execute, and re-verify.
opportunities.wow_factor: A clean coder/executor/judge loop with verifiable ground truth — the sandbox is the oracle, which is exactly how you design feedback loops for SDLC agents when correctness is legal, not aesthetic. The 'execution receipt' artifact (proof each published sample really ran) is a memorable, fiskaly-flavored touch: signed receipts for code samples from the receipts company.

opportunities.title: fiskaly Agent Skill + Integration Copilot: from a POS vendor's description to a running SIGN IT integration
opportunities.problem: Onboarding SIGN IT is an 11-step manual guide (dashboard org → API key → token → UNIT org → scoped subject → taxpayer → location → system → two-call records), with no SDK, gated Postman environments, and tribal knowledge (Italy supports only RECEIPT/CORRECTION/CANCELLATION; FISCAL_DEVICE is the only IT system type; first AdE portal login is manual). Meanwhile Agent Skills (SKILL.md at /.well-known/skills, open-sourced Dec 2025, adopted by ~40 clients) became the distribution channel for exactly this kind of procedural vendor knowledge — Stripe publishes a catalog installable via `npx skills add`; fiskaly publishes nothing there.
opportunities.solution: Two compounding pieces. (1) A published fiskaly-sign-it Agent Skill at workspace.fiskaly.com/.well-known/skills: SKILL.md encoding the full integration procedure, Italy-specific constraints, anti-patterns (extending workspace CLAUDE.md's existing list), and references to the spec — instantly usable in Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot. (2) An Integration Copilot built on it: give it a POS vendor's plain-language description ('restaurant POS, Node backend, 200 merchants in Lombardy, need receipts + corrections') and it interviews for gaps, plans the org hierarchy (GROUP per country, UNIT per merchant), scaffolds a typed integration module in the vendor's language with token refresh, idempotency, and retry/outage handling baked in, then proves it by running the flow to a COMPLETED record in the sandbox.
opportunities.value: Collapses fiskaly's sales-to-integration funnel: the evaluation build that takes a POS vendor weeks happens in an afternoon, which is decisive in the Italian land-grab window (certified software solutions go fully operational 2027; ~80% of RTs end-of-life by then). The Skill makes fiskaly knowledge ambient in every major coding agent at near-zero cost; the Copilot is the role's mandate — 'turn high-level requirements into production-ready applications' — pointed at customers.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3-4 days, but scope discipline is critical — this is the most demo-risky of the five, which is why it ranks below the MCP server it should sit on top of. Day 1: author SKILL.md from the integration guide + spec + CLAUDE.md anti-patterns; validate it standalone in Claude Code. Day 2-3: copilot as a Claude Agent SDK script: requirements intake → plan → scaffold a Node or Go module from templates + spec types → execute against the sandbox → self-correct on failures (the documented error model with E_* codes and X-Trace-Identifier makes the feedback loop tractable). Day 4: record the end-to-end run. Demo: paste a two-sentence vendor description, watch it become a repo with a green integration test and a COMPLETED record ID.
opportunities.wow_factor: Shows fluency with the newest distribution standard (Skills) on top of MCP, and demonstrates requirement-to-production agent orchestration with a real external system as the verifier. Positioning it explicitly as a layer above the MCP server shows architectural judgment about how the pieces compose — copilot for the first mile, MCP for the daily loop, judge for the trust layer.

result.top_pick: fiskaly Sandbox MCP Server — an action-taking MCP that lets any AI agent complete a full SIGN IT integration
result.prototype_pitch: Build "fiskaly-sandbox-mcp": a Go MCP server (official modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk; stdio for Claude Code, streamable HTTP for remote) with types generated from the publicly downloadable fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml via oapi-codegen — the same codegen fiskaly wraps internally — and three tool layers: docs (search_docs, get_endpoint, explain_error_code over the published spec and llms-full.txt), actions against test.api.fiskaly.com with all protocol friction absorbed in middleware (auto JWT mint/refresh, X-Api-Version, UUIDv4 X-Idempotency-Key, state polling) including two workflow tools — provision_merchant (UNIT org → subject → taxpayer → location → system → COMMISSIONED) and issue_receipt (INTENTION → TRANSACTION → poll to COMPLETED, returning the AdE progressive number) — and a judge layer, audit_session, that replays everything the agent did against a machine-readable compliance-rules YAML (INTENTION ordering, idempotency uniqueness, 60/90-day credential rotation, 12-day window) and emits a go-live readiness report. Demo storyline (under 5 minutes, live in Claude Code): `claude mcp add fiskaly-sandbox`, then "Onboard 'Trattoria da Mario' in Milan and issue a 42 euro receipt" — full provisioning and a COMPLETED fiscal record on screen; then "audit my integration" for a green judge report; then deliberately reuse an idempotency key and watch the judge catch it with the rule, the regulation, and the fine. Interview depth: why workflow-level tools beat tool-per-endpoint for token economics and safety (Twilio search+retrieve vs Stripe action patterns); TEST-only by design because every LIVE record legally reaches AdE — and what an OAuth, human-confirmation, restricted-tool-visibility path to LIVE would require; how audit_session is the same judge-auditing-coder architecture fiskaly wants internally, so the artifact doubles as a design proposal for their SDLC agents; and how spec-driven generation means the server regenerates on every CalVer release and can be productized across all Unified API countries (FR, BE, DE, PT) for free. Hedge for demo safety: a 200-line local simulator of the documented state machine behind a --base-url flag, so the demo survives sandbox onboarding delays or an AdE test outage.

opportunities.title: Zero-to-Receipt: agent-executable onboarding (claimable TEST sandbox + SIGN IT action-MCP + Agent Skill, audited by a Judge agent)
opportunities.problem: The SIGN IT funnel leaks at the very first step and never recovers. fiskaly's own /human-interventions.json flags account creation and API-key issuance as human-required, the quickstart is a personalized Postman environment gated behind HUB signup, and the integration guide is an 11-step manual flow (dashboard org -> API key -> token -> UNIT org -> scoped subject key -> taxpayer with Fisconline credentials -> location -> system -> INTENTION -> TRANSACTION). Research findings: no SDKs exist for SIGN IT (all GitHub SDK repos archived, Go SDK never public), the documented @fiskaly/docs-mcp is unpublished on npm and docs-only (9 read tools), and the dx-benchmark shows the 2026 frontier is agent-executable onboarding: Stripe claimable sandboxes (`stripe sandbox create`, no account needed), action-taking MCP servers (Stripe/Square/PayPal/Plaid), and Agent Skills at /.well-known/skills adopted by ~40 clients. fiskaly has the discovery layer (llms.txt, CLAUDE.md, Ask AI) but zero execution layer — an AI coding agent reading workspace.fiskaly.com still cannot DO anything.
opportunities.solution: Three pieces that turn time-to-first-signed-receipt (TTFSR) from hours-to-days into under 5 minutes, for humans AND agents: (1) a claimable-sandbox factory — a Go service holding a master TEST key that uses the existing API itself (createOrganization for a UNIT, createSubject with X-Scope-Identifier) to mint isolated, anonymous, pre-commissioned TEST stacks (org + key + taxpayer + location + system) on demand, claimable later into a real HUB account; (2) a SIGN IT action-MCP server (Go) with ~8 tools: provision_sandbox, get_integration_context, commission_stack, issue_receipt (encapsulates the INTENTION->TRANSACTION two-call pattern + idempotency keys), poll_record, get_compliance_artifact — TEST-host-only by construction; (3) a published SKILL.md so `npx skills add fiskaly` lets Claude Code/Cursor/Codex integrate SIGN IT autonomously. (4) The differentiator: a Judge agent that audits every tool call in the transcript against compliance rules derived from the docs (X-Idempotency-Key on every POST/PATCH, never reuse UUIDs, INTENTION strictly before TRANSACTION, never touch live.api.fiskaly.com, respect the ACCEPTED->COMPLETED state machine) and blocks or flags violations with citations into the spec.
opportunities.value: Make receipts easy = make the first receipt instant. Italy is the commercial window: ~80% of 1.7M RTs hit end-of-life by 2027 and certified software solutions go to full market operation in 2027, so thousands of POS vendors will evaluate SIGN IT in the next 18 months — the vendor with the shortest TTFSR wins the evaluation. It also makes fiskaly the default choice OF coding agents: as integration code is increasingly written by Claude Code/Cursor, an installable skill + action MCP is a distribution channel competitors (efsta, fiskaltrust) don't have. The sandbox factory is pure dogfooding — it is built on fiskaly's own multi-tenancy API, proving the API's power.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: Fully functional in 3-4 days by one Go engineer with a fiskaly TEST account. Day 1: sandbox factory (Go + net/http, master TEST key, createOrganization/createSubject/createTaxpayer/createLocation/createSystem against test.api.fiskaly.com; fallback to a pre-provisioned pool if scoped key creation hits limits). Day 2: MCP server in Go (official MCP Go SDK or mark3labs/mcp-go), tools wrapping the record lifecycle including JWT refresh and idempotency-key management; SKILL.md package. Day 3: Judge agent — a Claude-API-powered auditor consuming the MCP transcript + the OpenAPI spec, emitting a pass/fail compliance report; wire it as a gate on tool results. Day 4: demo polish + TTFSR stopwatch. Demo flow: prompt Claude Code with 'add compliant Italian receipts to this Go POS app' -> agent provisions sandbox, writes integration, first record reaches COMPLETED with the AdE progressive number in compliance.data -> wall-clock shows <5 min -> then inject a violation (reused idempotency key, or a live-host call) and show the Judge catch and block it.
opportunities.wow_factor: It IS the job description, demonstrated live: a Coder agent doing SDLC work, a Judge agent auditing it so fiscal compliance stays tamper-proof, human-in-the-loop at the claim step. It one-ups the benchmark companies (Stripe's claimable sandbox + Square's MCP + Anthropic's Skills) in a single coherent stack, compounds workspace.fiskaly.com's discovery layer instead of duplicating it (their MCP is docs-only and unpublished; their /api/console is a 404), and gives 20 minutes of deep interview material: judge-rule derivation from specs, prompt-injection defenses for action MCPs, sandbox/live separation, tool-per-endpoint vs search+execute architectures.

opportunities.title: AdE Simulator + Receipt Lifecycle Visualizer: chaos engineering for fiscal compliance
opportunities.problem: A SIGN IT integrator's hardest code paths are untestable today. Records transition asynchronously (ACCEPTED -> COMPLETED/FAILED/REJECTED, PROCESSING -> FINISHED) entirely inside fiskaly's service; there is no documented way to force a FAILED or REJECTED record in TEST, no webhook docs (webhooks appear only in one changelog line), no Retry-After or rate-limit numbers, and DEGRADED mode is 'not yet implemented'. Yet the docs' own 'Connection Loss' page prescribes a legally critical AdE-outage procedure (paper receipt, then e-invoice within 12 days with 'Portal unavailable' note) that no integrator can rehearse, and Fisconline credentials expiring every 90 days is a guaranteed production incident nobody can simulate. Benchmark: Plaid's sandbox simulates webhooks and Stripe ships magic test values precisely for this.
opportunities.solution: A Go proxy/simulator ('fisk-sim') that sits in front of test.api.fiskaly.com (or fully emulates the records surface from the OpenAPI spec) plus a live web visualizer. Magic test values (e.g., amount 666.00 -> REJECTED, 503.00 -> AdE outage with retry guidance, a magic tax_id -> expired Fisconline credentials) deterministically drive every documented failure path. The visualizer animates each record through INTENTION -> TRANSACTION -> state/mode transitions in real time, decodes the base64 transmission.request/response AdE payloads, and renders the 12-day compliance countdown when an outage scenario fires. Scenario scripts replay the full Connection Loss procedure end-to-end. Agentic layer: failure scenarios are generated by an agent from the spec + docs pages, and a judge agent verifies the simulator's responses conform to the documented state machine and error model (E_* codes) so the simulator can never teach integrators wrong behavior.
opportunities.value: Compliance failures in production mean real fines for merchants (70% of VAT, min EUR 300; EUR 100 per late transmission) — 'make receipts easy' must include making failure handling easy. Integrators who have rehearsed outages, rejections, and credential expiry go live faster and generate fewer support tickets; the visualizer doubles as a sales/demo artifact that makes the invisible async lifecycle legible to POS product managers, shortening evaluation cycles.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days in Go. The records state machine, error envelope, and outage procedure are fully specified in the downloadable OpenAPI spec (workspace.fiskaly.com/specs/fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml) and docs — enough to build a faithful emulator without any fiskaly-internal access. Day 1: Go HTTP simulator implementing /tokens + /records with the state machine and magic values. Day 2: SSE-driven visualizer (single-page app) animating record states and decoding transmission payloads. Day 3: scenario scripts + agent-generated scenario pack + judge validation report. Demo: run a POS client against fisk-sim, trigger an AdE outage mid-checkout, watch the visualizer walk through the documented emergency procedure, then flip the proxy to the real TEST API unchanged.
opportunities.wow_factor: Nothing like it exists on either fiskaly docs platform (workspace's ApiTryIt is dormant and has no failure simulation). It reframes docs as an executable rehearsal environment, and the judge-validated-simulator angle ('an agent built the test harness, another agent proved it matches the law/spec') is a direct, concrete instance of the Judge/Coder pattern the role is hiring for.

opportunities.title: Self-verifying docs: a multi-agent pipeline that fills the 168 blank descriptions and generates execution-proven examples and code samples
opportunities.problem: The rendered SIGN IT 2026-02-03 reference ships 168 unresolved template placeholders silently rendered as EMPTY descriptions (IT_en.yaml doesn't define them), zero operation-level request/response examples (0 'examples:' blocks), and zero x-codeSamples — so Redoc shows auto-generated skeletons in an API where the hard part is exactly the payloads (two-call INTENTION/TRANSACTION, Fisconline credentials, compliance.data). Twilio/Stripe-grade docs treat examples as load-bearing; fiskaly's unified-template architecture means whole countries inherit these holes. Hand-fixing doesn't scale across 4 country overlays x multiple CalVer versions; naive LLM generation risks hallucinated payloads in a legal-compliance API — the worst possible place for them.
opportunities.solution: A multi-agent SDLC pipeline, runnable in CI: (1) a Diff agent parses the unified OAS template against each country overlay and inventories every unresolved placeholder and example-less operation; (2) a Writer agent drafts descriptions, request/response examples, and x-codeSamples (curl/Go/Node/Python) grounded in the spec schemas plus the integration guide; (3) a Judge agent admits nothing it cannot prove: every generated request example is executed against test.api.fiskaly.com (or schema-validated where execution needs state) and every response example is diffed against the actual API response; rejects go back to the Writer with the failure. Output is a pull-request-shaped enriched IT_en.yaml overlay that drops straight into fiskaly's existing client-side templating — no platform change needed — plus a CI gate that fails when examples drift from API behavior on a new CalVer version.
opportunities.value: Directly answers the exercise's docs question at mission level: examples are the single highest-leverage docs artifact for TTFSR, and execution-proven examples also feed the Ask AI RAG and llms-full.txt, making the workspace assistant stop citing blank descriptions. Because SIGN IT shares its template with SIGN FR / E-INVOICE BE / E-INVOICE DE, one pipeline fixes four products, and re-runs automatically on every new API version — turning docs from a manual cost center into a generated, tested build artifact.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3 days. The spec, overlay, and templating mechanism are all public (live.unified.fiskaly.com OAS + /static/unified/2026-02-03/IT_en.yaml, mapping reverse-engineered from main.2c5ea9ba.js). Day 1: Go/TS tool that merges template+overlay and inventories the 168 gaps. Day 2: Writer+Judge loop on the Claude API with a TEST-account executor for the happy-path resources (tokens, organizations, taxpayers, systems, records). Day 3: render before/after in Redoc locally and produce the CI report. Demo: show a blank operation page, run the pipeline live, show the Judge rejecting a deliberately hallucinated example because the live API returned 422, then the corrected example landing and the page re-rendering with working curl/Go samples.
opportunities.wow_factor: It attacks the most embarrassing measurable defect in the exact docs URL the exercise cites — with the exact architecture the job posting describes (Coder agent audited by Judge agent, hallucinations structurally impossible because admission requires live execution). 'Docs that test themselves on every release' is a story no typo fix can compete with.

opportunities.title: 'First receipt in 5 minutes' interactive quickstart: finish the dormant ApiTryIt into an auth-aware, key-personalized guided flow with a TTFSR timer
opportunities.problem: The human onboarding path has no in-browser execution: the old portal's quickstart is 'download a Postman collection', and on workspace.fiskaly.com the React ApiTryIt request-runner exists in the shipped JS bundle but does not render on live Redoc pages (browser-verified), the linked /api/console is a 404, and even if mounted it has no auth-flow helper, no token persistence, and no code-snippet generation. Meanwhile the actual flow a newcomer must execute is unusually stateful (JWT from /tokens, X-Api-Version, X-Idempotency-Key per call, UNIT scoping, two-call records) — exactly the kind of flow Stripe solved years ago with logged-in keys embedded in interactive quickstarts.
opportunities.solution: A guided, stateful 'first signed receipt' web flow that compounds the dormant component: paste (or sandbox-factory-provision) TEST credentials once; the flow then walks token -> taxpayer -> commission -> system -> INTENTION -> TRANSACTION with each step pre-filled from the previous response (IDs threaded automatically, idempotency keys auto-generated, JWT auto-refreshed), live request/response panes, generated curl/Go/Node snippets per step that include the user's real values, and a visible time-to-first-signed-receipt stopwatch that ends when the record hits COMPLETED. Ship it as a self-contained page that could mount inside Starlight next to the existing Redoc pages.
opportunities.value: This is the conversion surface of the funnel: a POS developer evaluating SIGN IT against fiscal-printer incumbents decides in the first session. Removing Postman, header bookkeeping, and ID-threading collapses the first session from an afternoon to minutes, and the TTFSR stopwatch turns onboarding quality into a metric fiskaly can track and improve release over release.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days: a small React/Astro app + a thin Go CORS/token-broker backend talking to test.api.fiskaly.com; step definitions generated from the OpenAPI spec so new versions update the flow automatically. Demo: cold start with fresh TEST keys, end with a COMPLETED record and its compliance artifact PDF, stopwatch under 5 minutes; then show the same step definitions consumed headlessly by an agent — proving one source of truth serves both audiences.
opportunities.wow_factor: Lowest-risk, most immediately shippable of the five — and it explicitly finishes something fiskaly already half-built (ApiTryIt + the 404 console), which shows the candidate researched their platform deeply rather than pitching from a template. Weaker on the agentic axis than picks 1-3, which is why it ranks fourth; its agentic hook is spec-generated step definitions shared with the MCP.

opportunities.title: Agent-maintained SDK + MCP factory: regenerate Go/TS/Python clients and migration guides on every CalVer release
opportunities.problem: fiskaly has no SDKs for any current product: all five GitHub SDK repos are archived targeting the obsolete SIGN DE v1 API, a public Go SDK never existed, the official stance is 'use a plain HTTP client', the stale FAQ still points to the archived repos — yet workspace's /api/overview falsely claims official Node/Python/Java SDKs. Meanwhile SIGN IT shipped 4 date versions in ~17 months with breaking renames (/assets->/organizations, /entities split), and every integrator hand-rolls JWT refresh, idempotency keys, the two-call record pattern, and 60/90-day credential rotation. Post Stainless-winddown (Anthropic acquisition, May 2026), Speakeasy/Fern pipelines are the industry default — fiskaly even runs oapi-codegen internally for itself but offers nothing to integrators.
opportunities.solution: An agent-operated release pipeline: when a new CalVer spec appears at /specs/, a Builder agent regenerates idiomatic Go (and TS/Python) clients — oapi-codegen or Speakeasy core, plus a hand-shaped ergonomic layer (auto token refresh, idempotency middleware, typed record state machine, a single IssueReceipt() that does INTENTION+TRANSACTION); a Migration agent diffs old vs new spec and writes the upgrade guide with codemod hints (the artifact every version bump currently lacks); a Judge agent runs the generated SDK's integration tests against test.api.fiskaly.com and blocks release on any failure. Same pipeline emits the MCP server, so SDK and agent-tooling never drift.
opportunities.value: Makes the false SDK promise on workspace true, converts fiskaly's brutal-for-integrators release cadence from churn into a maintained upgrade path, and resurrects the dead GitHub org as a trust signal — all with near-zero ongoing human cost because the pipeline reruns itself per release. For an API whose only client surface today is 'the YAML file', a guaranteed-tested Go SDK is the difference between a weekend spike and a sprint for every one of the POS vendors entering the 2027 Italian window.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3-4 days, scoped to Go + SIGN IT: Day 1-2 generate and hand-shape the Go SDK from fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml with the ergonomic layer and live integration tests against TEST. Day 3: Migration agent demo on the real 2025-08-12 -> 2026-02-03 diff (both specs are public), producing the upgrade guide fiskaly never wrote. Day 4: Judge gate + GitHub Actions wiring. Demo: `go get`, ten lines to a signed receipt; then run the pipeline on the version diff and show the generated migration guide and passing test gate.
opportunities.wow_factor: Strong Golang signal for a Golang-titled role and a clean Coder/Judge/release-gate story; ranks last only because an SDK is the most expected proposal of the five — the agent-maintained-per-release angle and the auto-written migration guides are what lift it above 'just generate an SDK'.

result.top_pick: Zero-to-Receipt: agent-executable onboarding (claimable TEST sandbox + SIGN IT action-MCP + Agent Skill, audited by a Judge agent)
result.prototype_pitch: Build "Zero-to-Receipt" as three small Go components plus a live demo script. (1) Sandbox factory: a Go service holding one master TEST key that uses fiskaly's own multi-tenancy API (createOrganization UNIT + createSubject via X-Scope-Identifier, then taxpayer/location/system pre-commissioned with dummy Fisconline credentials) to mint an isolated, anonymous, claimable TEST stack in one POST — fiskaly's Stripe-style claimable sandbox, built entirely on the public API. (2) A SIGN IT action-MCP server in Go with ~8 tools (provision_sandbox, get_integration_context, commission_stack, issue_receipt wrapping INTENTION->TRANSACTION with managed idempotency keys and JWT refresh, poll_record, get_compliance_artifact), hard-wired to test.api.fiskaly.com, packaged with a SKILL.md installable into Claude Code. (3) A Judge agent that audits the full tool-call transcript against rules derived from the docs/spec — idempotency key on every POST/PATCH, no UUID reuse, INTENTION strictly before TRANSACTION, TEST host only, state machine respected — and blocks violations with spec citations. Demo storyline (8 minutes): show the leaky status quo (HUB signup, Postman download, 11 manual steps, blank reference descriptions), then prompt Claude Code with "add compliant Italian receipts to this Go POS app"; the agent provisions a sandbox, writes the integration, and the first record reaches COMPLETED with the AdE progressive number in compliance.data — TTFSR stopwatch under 5 minutes, zero human steps; finally inject a deliberate violation (reused idempotency key, then an attempted live-host call) and let the Judge catch and block both, displaying its audit report. Interview deep-dive material: why TTFSR is the metric that decides the 2027 Italian RT-replacement window; judge-rule derivation from OpenAPI + prose docs and how the same pattern keeps fiskaly's internal Coder agents compliant; prompt-injection and blast-radius design for action-taking MCPs (Stripe's published warnings, restricted tool visibility, sandbox/live separation); why this compounds rather than duplicates workspace.fiskaly.com (their MCP is docs-only and unpublished, /api/console is a 404, their llms.txt/CLAUDE.md become the get_integration_context payload); and the roadmap from prototype to product — claim-flow into HUB, per-tool OAuth scopes, and publishing the skill to /.well-known/skills as a new acquisition channel.

opportunities.title: CERTIFY: an agentic 'certify my integration' harness with a Judge agent that issues audit-style compliance reports
opportunities.problem: Italian fiscalization is bought as legal certainty, but fiskaly customers have no way to prove their integration is compliant before going live. The research shows: TEST and LIVE are fully isolated and 'testing in LIVE is forbidden since every LIVE record is transmitted to the tax authority' (sign-it-api digest); the docs ship zero request/response examples and zero scenario tests beyond a Postman collection; AdE outage handling lives on a separate 'Connection Loss' page (paper receipt + e-invoice within 12 days) that nothing enforces; record FAILED/REJECTED states are final with no remediation guidance; and Italian penalties are concrete (70% of VAT min €300 for bad memorization/transmission, €100 per late transmission capped €1,000/quarter, 3-day-to-1-month closure — italy-context digest). Meanwhile dx-benchmark shows the frontier (Plaid webhook simulation, Stripe magic test values) — fiskaly has nothing like scenario simulation. A POS vendor only discovers their outage handling or void flow is wrong when a real merchant gets fined.
opportunities.solution: A Go CLI + service ('fiskaly certify') that runs a battery of compliance scenarios against a customer's SIGN IT integration and produces an audit-style report. Scenarios encoded in YAML: happy-path receipt (INTENTION→TRANSACTION), CORRECTION, CANCELLATION, idempotency replay (expect X-Idempotency-Replayed) and 409/422 conflict handling, lottery-code receipt, AdE-outage replay (fiskaly simulator returns FAILED — does the integration issue the paper-doc/12-day e-invoice fallback?), Fisconline credential-expiry behavior. Two execution modes: (a) against the real test.api.fiskaly.com sandbox, (b) against a local spec-derived SIGN IT simulator that injects faults the sandbox can't (outage, 429, FAILED states). A multi-agent pipeline runs it: a Planner agent sequences scenarios, an Executor drives the customer integration, a Chaos agent injects faults, and a Judge agent evaluates the full transcript against a machine-readable rule set citing the actual norms (D.Lgs. 127/2015 art. 2, provv. 111204/2025, the 12-day rule) and outputs PASS/FAIL per scenario with sanction exposure in euros and remediation steps. Report is a signed HTML/PDF a customer can show their auditor.
opportunities.value: This turns 'make receipts easy' into 'make compliance provable'. It converts fiskaly's deepest asset — regulatory knowledge — into a product surface: customers buy certainty, and a Certify report is certainty in artifact form. It shortens integration time (the 11-step manual flow becomes test-driven), reduces support load from FAILED-state confusion, and creates a moat competitors (efsta, fiskaltrust) don't have. It is also the natural pre-2027 wedge: when certified software solutions (SSW) go fully live in 2027 with a producer/certifier/Commissione approval chain (italy-context digest), fiskaly already owns the 'conformity testing' mindshare and tooling.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-4 days for one strong engineer, fully functional. Day 1: register a free fiskaly TEST account (all accounts start in TEST with full API functionality); build a minimal Go scenario runner that executes the two-call INTENTION→TRANSACTION flow plus CORRECTION/CANCELLATION against test.api.fiskaly.com, with YAML scenario definitions. Day 2: generate a local SIGN IT simulator from the downloadable OpenAPI spec (workspace.fiskaly.com/specs/fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml) with fault injection (FAILED records, 429, outage), and write a deliberately buggy sample POS integration as the test subject. Day 3: the Judge — deterministic assertions (state machine, idempotency headers, payload fields) plus a Claude-based judge that evaluates non-deterministic behavior (did the integration's outage response satisfy the 12-day fallback?) against rules.yaml with regulation citations; emit the HTML audit report. Day 4: polish demo + report design. Stack: Go (the role's language), the published OAS, Claude API for the judge, no fiskaly-internal access needed. Demo flow: run certify on the buggy POS → red report with citations and €-exposure → fix two bugs live → green 'audit-ready' report.
opportunities.wow_factor: It is the job ad made flesh: 'judge agents that audit coder agents so fiscal signatures stay legally compliant' — here a Judge agent audits the customer's integration, with a Planner/Executor/Chaos/Judge multi-agent architecture, deterministic-vs-LLM responsibility split, and human-in-the-loop flags (manual AdE first login, credential renewal can't be automated — mirroring workspace's own human-interventions.json). It demonstrates exactly the 90%-design/10%-code skill the role wants, in Go, and the interview discussion writes itself: how the same Judge harness becomes fiskaly's internal regression gate for agent-written code.

opportunities.title: Compliance provenance layer: regulation-to-endpoint mapping that also heals the 168 blank descriptions
opportunities.problem: The rendered SIGN IT 2026-02-03 spec contains 168 unresolved description placeholders that silently render as empty descriptions (sign-it-api digest) — precisely where customers most need legal context (e.g. oas_components_schemas_italian_fiscalization_description). Meanwhile the regulatory ground truth exists and is machine-readable-adjacent: provv. 111204/2025 with tech specs v1.0→v1.3 (annexes updated 17 Apr 2026), provv. 424470/2025 for POS-RT, D.Lgs. 127/2015 (italy-context digest). workspace.fiskaly.com's regulatory.json only has coarse per-country flags — nothing maps a provvedimento article to an API field. So an integrator seeing 'lottery_code' or 'tax_id_number required' has no idea which legal obligation it implements or what breaks legally if they skip it.
opportunities.solution: A multi-agent docs pipeline: an Extractor agent parses the provvedimenti/spec PDFs and the AdE XSDs into a normalized rule graph; a Mapper agent links each rule to OpenAPI paths/fields, emitting an OpenAPI overlay with x-regulation extensions ({article, source URL, obligation, sanction}); a Writer agent generates the missing 168 descriptions grounded ONLY in the rule graph; a Judge agent verifies every generated sentence has a citation and rejects hallucinated claims. Output: an enriched spec + a rendered 'why does this field exist' traceability matrix, published as a machine-readable regulation.json v2 that agents (and workspace's Ask AI) can consume.
opportunities.value: Directly compounds (not duplicates) workspace.fiskaly.com: their RAG assistant and llms.txt get a verified legal-provenance substrate instead of prose. It scales across all 6+ countries with the same pipeline — the agentic answer to fiskaly's real bottleneck, which is that regulation changes faster than docs (4 API versions in 17 months, spec v1.0→v1.3 in 14 months). And it fixes a visible, embarrassing defect (168 blank descriptions) as a side effect.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3 days. Inputs are all public: the unified OAS + IT_en.yaml overlay (the digest documents exactly which 168 keys are missing), provvedimento PDFs and AdE technical-spec annexes. Build in Go + Claude API: pipeline stages as separate agents with typed handoffs; Judge enforces citation coverage as a hard gate. Demo: show a blank description on the live docs, run the pipeline, show the same field with a generated description citing provv. 111204 §X, then show the x-regulation overlay diffable in CI so a future spec v1.4 automatically flags unmapped rules.
opportunities.wow_factor: It is a writer-agent/judge-agent feedback loop applied to fiskaly's actual content debt — the interviewer can verify the 168 placeholders themselves. The citation-gated Judge is a concrete answer to 'how do you stop agents hallucinating legal claims', the exact trust problem of fiskaly's AI-first initiative.

opportunities.title: Error catalog + incident remediation agent ('my record FAILED — what now, legally?')
opportunities.problem: The error model is a bare {status, code, error, message} wrapper; no Retry-After header, no rate-limit numbers documented anywhere, and FAILED/REJECTED are final states with all transitions performed internally by fiskaly (sign-it-api digest). The AdE-outage remediation (paper document, then e-invoice within 12 days with prescribed data) lives on one disconnected page. dx-benchmark names Twilio's 500+ entry error dictionary with causes and solutions as a load-bearing gold standard fiskaly entirely lacks. For a fiscal API, an unexplained FAILED is not a bug — it is potential sanction exposure (€100 per late/omitted transmission, capped €1,000/quarter).
opportunities.solution: Two layers. (1) A generated error catalog: every E_* code and HTTP status from the spec, enriched by an agent pipeline with cause, retry semantics (idempotency-key reuse rules, 409 vs 422 disambiguation), and legal consequence + deadline where applicable. (2) A remediation agent: paste a failed Record JSON or X-Trace-Identifier, and it returns a grounded action plan — 'transmission FAILED at 14:02; per Connection Loss procedure issue paper documento commerciale now; you have until <date> (12 days) to issue the e-invoice with these prescribed fields; sanction if missed: €100, capped €1,000/quarter' — with citations, plus the exact follow-up API calls. Ships as a CLI and as an MCP tool so support engineers and customer agents both consume it.
opportunities.value: Converts fiskaly support tickets into self-service, and converts panic into procedure — the purest form of 'compliance confidence as the product'. The deadline-aware remediation plans (12-day clocks, quarterly sanction caps) are something no generic docs assistant can do safely without the grounded rule set, differentiating it from workspace's Ask AI chat.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2 days. Parse the public OAS for all error responses/codes; hand-curate the Italian remediation rules (Connection Loss page, sanction table from D.Lgs. 87/2024) into rules.yaml; Go CLI + Claude agent that classifies an input record/error and renders the action plan with computed deadlines from the record's timestamps. Demo: feed it three real failure payloads from the TEST sandbox (auth expiry, idempotency 422, simulated FAILED transmission) and show three distinct, cited, deadline-computed action plans.
opportunities.wow_factor: Smallest scope, highest demo density: live API failures turning into legally-cited action plans in seconds. Also shows judgment about what must stay deterministic (deadline arithmetic, sanction amounts from a table) vs what the LLM may phrase — a core judge-agent design question for the role.

opportunities.title: Compliance Sentinel: an always-on agent that watches deadlines fiskaly's API knows about but never surfaces
opportunities.problem: SIGN IT has silent time bombs the API does not alert on: Fisconline credentials expire every 90 days (renewal recommended at 60) and the DEGRADED mode that would signal invalid credentials is documented as 'not yet implemented' (sign-it-api digest); POS-RT linkage has rolling monthly registration windows (6th day of second month after activation to last working day) with €1,000-4,000 fines plus license suspension (italy-context digest); daily corrispettivi must be transmitted within 12 days. Today every integrator must independently rebuild this deadline tracking — or get fined.
opportunities.solution: A monitoring agent that polls the customer's own fiskaly account via the public API (listTaxpayers/listSystems/listRecords), maintains a deadline ledger (credential ages, records stuck in ACCEPTED/PROCESSING, systems without POS linkage, transmission gaps vs daily-closing expectations), and emits prioritized alerts with remediation ('Taxpayer X credentials are 61 days old — rotate now via PATCH /taxpayers/{id}; in 29 days transmissions will silently fail'). A weekly 'compliance posture' report per UNIT organization aggregates exposure in euros. Human-in-the-loop by design: the agent never mutates state, it proposes the exact PATCH and waits for approval.
opportunities.value: fiskaly's mission is keeping merchants compliant continuously, not just at integration time. Sentinel extends compliance confidence from day 1 to day 1000 and creates recurring-value justification for fiskaly's per-POS pricing. It also generates the telemetry fiskaly needs to prioritize implementing DEGRADED mode properly.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days against the TEST sandbox: Go daemon polling list endpoints with the JWT flow, SQLite deadline ledger, rule engine for the three deadline classes, Claude agent only for report narration and alert prioritization. Demo: seed a sandbox account with an aging-credential taxpayer and a stuck record, run the sentinel, show the alert feed and the weekly posture report.
opportunities.wow_factor: Shows product thinking beyond docs — it weaponizes gaps the candidate found in fiskaly's own spec (90-day credentials, unimplemented DEGRADED) into a customer-facing agent, and the never-mutate/propose-PATCH pattern is a textbook human-in-the-loop design the role explicitly asks about.

opportunities.title: Version-diff explainer + migration agent: 'what changed, why legally, and patch my code'
opportunities.problem: SIGN IT shipped 4 API versions in ~17 months (2024-09-20→2024-10-31→2025-08-12→2026-02-03, github-sdks digest) with genuinely breaking renames (/assets→/organizations, /entities split into /taxpayers+/locations, credentials.tax_id_number becoming required), there are no SDKs to absorb the churn (all SDK repos archived; official stance is 'use a plain HTTP client'), and the changelog never explains WHICH regulation forced WHICH change. Stripe's named monthly versions with upgrade guides are the benchmark (dx-benchmark). Every fiskaly version bump is currently an unguided, customer-funded migration.
opportunities.solution: An agent pipeline that (1) semantically diffs two OAS versions into a typed change list (breaking/additive/cosmetic), (2) maps each change to its regulatory driver using the provenance layer (e.g. 'tax_id_number required ← AdE web-procedure auth requirements'), (3) generates a migration guide, and (4) — the agentic leap — runs a Migrator agent against a customer codebase that proposes concrete patches (renamed paths, new required fields), verified by re-running the CERTIFY harness from opportunity 1 as the Judge before the PR is opened.
opportunities.value: Removes the biggest hidden cost of fiskaly's (necessary) version velocity, raising customer retention through migrations and making future version bumps cheap for fiskaly to ship — regulatory agility becomes a feature instead of a tax.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3-4 days: both OAS versions are publicly downloadable (live.unified.fiskaly.com 20781ec5 and c18c9c21 hashes); Go diff tool over parsed specs; Claude for regulatory mapping and guide generation; Migrator demoed on a small sample integration repo, with the harness as acceptance gate. Demo: diff 2025-08-12→2026-02-03 live, show the generated guide, then watch the agent patch the sample repo and pass certification.
opportunities.wow_factor: Coder agent + Judge agent + automated SDLC on a real, public, breaking API diff — the closest mirror of fiskaly's internal 'agents that turn requirements into production-ready applications' applied outward to customers. Strongest as a follow-on once CERTIFY exists, which is why it ranks last standalone.

result.top_pick: CERTIFY: an agentic 'certify my integration' harness with a Judge agent that issues audit-style compliance reports
result.prototype_pitch: Build "fiskaly certify" — a Go CLI that runs ~8 YAML-defined compliance scenarios (happy-path INTENTION→TRANSACTION receipt, CORRECTION, CANCELLATION, idempotency replay + 409/422 conflict, lottery-code receipt, AdE-outage replay, credential-expiry behavior) against a deliberately buggy sample POS integration, in two modes: live against the real test.api.fiskaly.com sandbox (free TEST account, full API functionality) and against a local fault-injecting simulator generated from the public OpenAPI spec (workspace.fiskaly.com/specs/fiskaly.sign-it.2026-02-03.yaml) for failures the sandbox can't produce. A four-agent pipeline runs it — Planner sequences scenarios, Executor drives the integration, Chaos injects FAILED/429/outage, and a Judge agent (Claude) evaluates the full transcript against a citation-gated rules.yaml (D.Lgs. 127/2015 art. 2, provv. 111204/2025, the 12-day e-invoice fallback, D.Lgs. 87/2024 sanction table) — and emits an audit-style HTML report: PASS/FAIL per scenario, regulation citation, euro sanction exposure, and exact remediation. Demo storyline (5 minutes): run certify on the buggy POS → red report showing it mishandles AdE outages and reuses idempotency keys, with '€1,000/quarter exposure' and cited articles → fix the two bugs live → re-run → green 'audit-ready' certificate. Interview deep-dive topics: the deterministic-vs-LLM responsibility split in the Judge (state-machine and deadline checks are code; behavioral interpretation is the model, hard-gated on citations); how the identical harness becomes fiskaly's internal regression gate for Coder agents (the job's literal Judge-audits-Coder mandate); human-in-the-loop boundaries (manual AdE first login, Fisconline rotation — mirroring workspace's human-interventions.json); and the product path from prototype to "fiskaly CERTIFY" as the pre-2027 wedge into Italy's SSW certification regime, plus scenario packs per country as fiskaly's durable moat.

opportunities.title: The Docs Judge: a multi-agent conformance CI for fiskaly's 7-country docs matrix (template <-> country overlays <-> rendered docs <-> live API behavior)
opportunities.problem: fiskaly's Unified API docs are a build pipeline with no QA gate. One templated OpenAPI spec backs SIGN IT/FR + E-INVOICE BE/DE, with per-country overlay files substituted client-side — and the IT overlay fails to define 168 template keys, so the live SIGN IT 2026-02-03 reference silently renders 168 blank descriptions (verified live: /tmp/signit-rendered.yaml contains exactly 168 unique unresolved ((<...)) keys). Around it: zero request/response examples, no rate-limit numbers or Retry-After, webhooks mentioned only in a changelog line, '401.xf'/'stabability' typos, a leaked /api/sign-it/local dev page, and cross-platform contradictions (workspace claims Node/Python/Java SDKs that don't exist, /api/console 404s, Belgium 'Live E-Invoice' vs 'SIGN BE Beta', products.json missing the launched sign-pt, generic 'api.fiskaly.com/api/v3 + 100 req/min' conflicting with product pages). Every new country multiplies this surface — Sweden and Poland are next.
opportunities.solution: A Go-based multi-agent docs CI ('Docs Judge') that audits the whole matrix on every docs/spec change: (1) Harvester agent fetches the unified template, all country overlays, workspace /specs/ files, products.json/regulatory.json and rendered pages; (2) deterministic linters resolve placeholders and flag unresolved keys, missing examples, and cross-country/cross-platform field+status diffs; (3) Prober agent executes real calls against test.api.fiskaly.com and diffs actual behavior vs spec claims (idempotency replay header, error envelope, 429 without Retry-After, undocumented response headers); (4) an LLM Judge agent triages findings by severity, drafts the actual fixes (missing overlay values, example payloads, corrected status labels) and emits a PR-ready patch behind a human-approval queue. The same judge then gates future docs releases as CI — and is the template for fiskaly's planned Judge-audits-Coder SDLC agents.
opportunities.value: Docs ARE the product for an API-only compliance company — a blank description on a fiscal field is a compliance risk for 1,600+ B2B customers integrating tax-authority-facing flows. This makes 'one platform, many fiscal regimes' actually scale: each new country (SE, PL) lands with a machine-enforced guarantee of zero unresolved placeholders, consistent semantics, and docs that match live API behavior. It compounds workspace.fiskaly.com rather than duplicating it: workspace renders docs; the Judge guarantees what gets rendered is true.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: Highly feasible in 2-4 days because all inputs are public and already harvested: the templated OAS (live.unified.fiskaly.com/20781ec5/en/oas.yaml), country overlays (/static/unified/2026-02-03/{IT,FR,DE,BE}_en.yaml), workspace /specs/ downloads, and a free sandbox account for probing. Stack: Go CLI (matches the Golang role) orchestrating worker agents — placeholder resolver + cross-country differ are pure Go (regex/YAML diff, deterministic, fast, cheap); behavior prober uses net/http against test.api.fiskaly.com; the Judge is Claude API with a structured-findings schema, severity rubric, and fix-drafting prompt; output is an HTML/Markdown report plus a git patch for IT_en.yaml. Demo flow: run `docsjudge audit sign-it 2026-02-03` live in the interview, watch it find the real 168 blanks, the BE Live/Beta mismatch and the missing-Retry-After in under a minute, then approve one Judge-drafted fix and show the generated overlay patch.
opportunities.wow_factor: It IS the job ad, running live against fiskaly's own production docs: a Judge agent auditing artifacts, deterministic-vs-LLM task split, human-in-the-loop approval, written in Go. The opening line — 'your live Italian API reference has 168 silently blank descriptions; here's the agent that catches it, fixes it, and prevents it for Sweden' — is a finding their team almost certainly doesn't know, with a working tool attached. Deep interview material: judge prompt/rubric design, why linting stays deterministic and judgment goes to the LLM, feedback loops from human overrides, and how the same architecture audits Coder agents for tamper-proof fiscal signatures.

opportunities.title: Version Delta Pilot: agent-generated migration guides and codemods between date versions (2024-10-31 -> 2025-08-12 -> 2026-02-03 -> 2026-06-01)
opportunities.problem: SIGN IT shipped 4 date versions in ~17 months with genuinely breaking changes (/assets renamed to /organizations, /entities split into /taxpayers + /locations, content.details moved to content.operation.details, credentials.tax_id_number made required) — and fiskaly's official stance is 'no SDK needed, use a plain HTTP client', so every one of those breaks lands as manual work on every integrator. The research shows migration knowledge exists only as prose changelog entries and a hand-written guide; there is no machine-readable diff, no automated changelog between versions, and the old /api index still advertised X-Api-Version 2024-09-20.
opportunities.solution: A pipeline that turns any pair of date-versioned specs into (a) a semantic, categorized changelog (breaking/additive/doc-only) via oasdiff, (b) an LLM-narrated migration guide with before/after payloads, and (c) a Coder agent that scans an integrator's codebase, locates affected calls, and proposes the migration as a PR — with a Judge agent validating the migrated code by replaying the flows against test.api.fiskaly.com before any human review. Published per-version on the docs site as 'Your delta from 2025-08-12'.
opportunities.value: Version velocity is the cost of fiskaly's regulatory business — regimes change, specs must follow. Today that velocity is taxed onto 1,600+ customers as manual migration risk on compliance-critical code. Automating spec-diff -> guide -> codemod -> verified PR converts fiskaly's biggest integrator pain into a differentiator, and directly exercises the role's mandate: autonomous systems turning requirements into production-ready code with judge oversight.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 2-3 days: all four spec versions are publicly fetchable (unified OAS endpoints + workspace /specs/; 2025-08-12 and 2026-02-03 already cached locally at /tmp/signit-2025-08-12-oas.yaml and /tmp/signit-2026-02-03-oas.yaml). Stack: Go CLI wrapping oasdiff for the structural delta, Claude for guide narration and codemod generation, a small deliberately-stale sample integration (Go, written against 2025-08-12) as the migration target, and a sandbox-replay Judge step. Demo: run the differ, show the generated migration guide, watch the Coder agent open a diff renaming /entities calls to /taxpayers+/locations, watch the Judge replay INTENTION->TRANSACTION against the test API and approve.
opportunities.wow_factor: Shows the full multi-agent SDLC loop the role describes — Differ -> Writer -> Coder -> Judge -> human — on fiskaly's real version history, not a toy. The 'Judge replays the migrated flow against your own sandbox before a human sees the PR' beat is exactly the tamper-proof/compliance-safe automation story in the job ad.

opportunities.title: Country Expansion Copilot: 'You integrated SIGN IT — here is your exact delta to SIGN FR/PT/BE', computed from the unified spec
opportunities.problem: fiskaly's strategic pitch is one platform, many regimes — but the docs make every country look like a fresh integration. The cross-country knowledge is locked inside the unified template as inline prose flags ('Not supported in 🇮🇹 Italy', 'In Italy, the supported transaction operations are RECEIPT/CORRECTION/CANCELLATION' vs the spec's full enum including ABORT, DRAFT_RECEIPT, INVOICE...), per-country required fields (IT's Fisconline credentials with 90-day expiry), and overlay diffs. Workspace's PM Hub Market Matrix is hand-authored marketing-level content, not spec-derived, and the platforms already contradict each other on country status — so customers evaluating expansion get no trustworthy, engineering-grade delta.
opportunities.solution: A delta compiler that parses the unified template + all country overlays into a structured per-country capability model (operations, record types, required fields, headers, lifecycle differences, country-flag exceptions), then generates: (1) an always-current cross-country capability matrix derived from the spec (so it cannot drift, unlike the PM Hub version), and (2) personalized expansion reports — point it at a customer's integration code or API logs, it maps which endpoints/fields they use, and an agent writes 'your SIGN IT integration covers 85% of SIGN FR; here are the 9 concrete deltas, with the code-level changes'. The same model feeds llms.txt/CLAUDE.md so coding agents get country semantics for free.
opportunities.value: Land-and-expand is fiskaly's growth engine (DE/AT/ES/IT/FR/PT/BE live, SE/PL coming): making the second country a computed diff instead of a second project directly increases multi-country attach rate, and makes 'one platform' a demonstrable engineering fact rather than a slogan. It compounds workspace by giving its hand-authored matrix a spec-derived source of truth.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: 3-4 days: the country flags follow recognizable patterns in the template descriptions and the overlay YAMLs are small (IT_en.yaml ~79KB) — a Go parser plus an LLM extraction pass over ambiguous prose builds the capability model; deltas are then pure data diffs; report generation is one Claude call with the structured delta as context. Demo: a small web view (or rich CLI) showing the live IT<->FR matrix, then feeding a sample SIGN IT integration repo and generating its personalized 'delta to France' report including the offline-replay headers FR supports and IT doesn't.
opportunities.wow_factor: Turns the interview's strategic question ('drive the mission to the next level') into running code: the demo literally computes fiskaly's one-platform story from their own spec. Strong PM-plus-engineering signal, and the structured country model is reusable by every other agent fiskaly builds — a platform answer, not a feature answer.

opportunities.title: Country Compiler: an agent pipeline that drafts and certifies a new country's docs overlay (Sweden dry-run) with a zero-placeholder, citation-grounded guarantee
opportunities.problem: Adding a country today means hand-authoring a new overlay file against the 633-placeholder unified template plus guides — and the process demonstrably fails: Italy shipped with 168 keys undefined, sign-pt is missing from products.json despite being launched, and country status labels disagree across pages. With InfraSec making Sweden the sixth market and Poland's KSeF flagged on the blog, this manual bottleneck is about to be hit twice more.
opportunities.solution: A multi-agent authoring pipeline for new-country onboarding: a Drafter agent fills every template key for the target country from structured inputs (regulatory.json-style facts, existing overlays as style exemplars, public regulatory sources like SKVFS 2020:9); a Grounding Judge verifies each regulatory claim against cited sources and refuses uncited assertions; a Consistency Judge enforces terminology parity with the other six overlays and the hard invariant 'zero unresolved placeholders'; a human reviewer approves per-section. Output: a complete, certified SE_en.yaml ready for the existing rendering pipeline, plus an audit trail of which agent wrote what and which judge approved it.
opportunities.value: Directly attacks fiskaly's scaling constraint — time-to-market per fiscal regime — at the documentation/spec layer, with the compliance-grade auditability the domain demands ('which agent claimed this, based on which provvedimento'). This is the 90%-design role in production form: it automates a real internal SDLC workflow fiskaly's own team performs every time a market launches.
opportunities.prototype_feasibility: Feasible but the most ambitious of the four — scope to a vertical slice: 1 day building the placeholder-key extractor and pipeline skeleton in Go, 1-2 days on Drafter + two Judge agents (Claude with structured citation schema), demo on ~30 representative keys for Sweden using public SKVFS facts and the IT/FR overlays as exemplars, showing one drafted section being rejected by the Grounding Judge for a missing citation and then revised. Honest framing in the interview: this is a pilot of the pipeline, not a finished Swedish overlay.
opportunities.wow_factor: The most 'go crazy' option: agents authoring compliance documentation under judge supervision, with rejection/revision loops shown live. The Grounding-Judge-rejects-uncited-claim moment is a visceral demonstration of exactly the safety architecture the job ad describes for fiscal signatures. Ranked last only because demo risk is higher and the wow depends on content quality, not because the ambition is wrong.

result.top_pick: The Docs Judge: a multi-agent conformance CI for fiskaly's 7-country docs matrix (template <-> country overlays <-> rendered docs <-> live API behavior)
result.prototype_pitch: Build "docsjudge", a Go CLI that runs a four-stage agent pipeline against fiskaly's real production docs supply chain. Stage 1 (Harvester) fetches the unified OAS template, the IT/FR/BE/DE country overlays, workspace.fiskaly.com /specs/ files, products.json and the rendered pages. Stage 2 (deterministic linters, pure Go) resolves the template and flags every unresolved placeholder — finding the real 168 silently-blank descriptions in the live SIGN IT 2026-02-03 reference (independently verified: exactly 168 unique unresolved keys) — plus zero-example operations and cross-country/cross-platform contradictions (Belgium 'Live' vs 'Beta', products.json missing sign-pt, phantom SDK claims). Stage 3 (Prober) executes real requests against test.api.fiskaly.com with a free sandbox account and diffs documented vs actual behavior: X-Idempotency-Replayed presence, error envelope shape, 429 with no Retry-After. Stage 4 (Judge, Claude API) triages all findings by compliance severity, drafts concrete fixes — missing overlay values, example payloads, corrected labels — and emits a PR-ready IT_en.yaml patch behind a human-approval queue, exiting non-zero so it works as a CI gate. Demo storyline (90 seconds): run one command live in the interview, watch the finding counter hit 168 against fiskaly's own site, open the Judge's severity-ranked report, approve one drafted fix, show the git patch. Interview depth: why linting stays deterministic while judgment goes to the LLM (cost, reproducibility, audit trail), judge rubric and prompt design, feedback loops from human overrides into the rubric, how the zero-placeholder invariant becomes the launch gate for Sweden and Poland overlays, and how this exact architecture generalizes to the job's core mandate — Judge agents auditing Coder agents so fiscal signature code stays tamper-proof. Scope control for 2-4 days: days 1-2 harvester + linters + report (already de-risked: all artifacts fetched and the 168 count reproduced locally at /tmp/signit-rendered.yaml), day 3 Judge with fix-drafting, day 4 sandbox prober + polish; the prober is the cut line if time runs short.
